body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Go to the root path of the web server, i.e. localhost:8000
2. See a blank page and an error.

## Expected behavior

I see the welcome screen.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Server
 - Version: 7.4.3
 - Browser and OS: Firefox 68, Ubuntu 20.04

## Symptom

Loading the main page causes a syntax error while parsing the main.js bundle due to an illegal regular expression.

```
SyntaxError: invalid identity escape in regular expression
```

## Root cause

This is due to an illegal regular expression escape used here:

https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/blob/v7.4.3/webapp/src/blocks/boardView.ts#L62

## Proposal

A code search suggests that `sortBoardViewsAlphabetically` is not meaningfully used in the codebase; it only has callsites in unit tests.

It seems that it was previously used in `webapp/src/components/sidebar/sidebarBoardItem.tsx`, however as of aa540e73ce669a1acd491a5edd7d55199ef1b87c, its use was decommissioned and the original utility was not removed.

Removing this function altogether remediates the SyntaxError issue in my local environment. I propose this method and associated unit tests simply be removed.
title: Bug: Invalid regular expression exception for emoji replacement in Firefox

triage.summary: Invalid regex escape in boardView.ts causes SyntaxError in Firefox, rendering a blank page. The unused `sortBoardViewsAlphabetically` function and its unit tests should be removed.
triage.test_hint: Delete sortBoardViewsAlphabetically function from boardView.ts and remove associated unit tests. Verify main page loads in Firefox without SyntaxError.

> postgres://boardsuser:buo9ieHo@postgres/boards?sslmode=disable&connect_timeout=10

## Expected behavior

Normal connection

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Server
 - Version: 7.10.6
 - Browser and OS: None

## Additional context (optional)

Logs:
```
2024/08/07 09:39:51 {ServerRoot:http://0.0.0.0:8000 Port:8000 DBType:postgres DBConfigString:"postgres://boardsuser:buo9ieHo@postgres/boards?sslmode=disable&connect_timeout=10" DBTablePrefix: UseSSL:false SecureCookie:false WebPath:./pack FilesDriver:local FilesS3Config:{AccessKeyID: SecretAccessKey: Bucket: PathPrefix: Region: Endpoint: SSL:false SignV2:false SSE:false Trace:false Timeout:0} FilesPath:./files MaxFileSize:0 Telemetry:true TelemetryID: PrometheusAddress::9092 WebhookUpdate:[] Secret: SessionExpireTime:2592000 SessionRefreshTime:18000 LocalOnly:false EnableLocalMode:true LocalModeSocketLocation:/var/tmp/focalboard_local.socket EnablePublicSharedBoards:false FeatureFlags:map[] EnableDataRetention:false DataRetentionDays:0 TeammateNameDisplay:username ShowEmailAddress:false ShowFullName:false AuthMode:native LoggingCfgFile: LoggingCfgJSON: AuditCfgFile: AuditCfgJSON: NotifyFreqCardSeconds:0 NotifyFreqBoardSeconds:0}
info  [2024-08-07 09:39:51.864 Z] Focalboard server                        caller="model/version.go:64" version=7.10.6 edition=linux build_number=5895202942 build_date="Thu Aug 17 19:40:59 UTC 2023" build_hash=4c579d34acd822b3ca24a0e0ba0133decacb28b0
error [2024-08-07 09:39:51.865 Z] Database Ping failed                     caller="server/server.go:220" error="dial tcp [::1]:5432: connect: connection refused"
```

I'm running focalboard in docker and dbconfig is passed as an env variable:
`
FOCALBOARD_DBCONFIG="postgres://${FOCALBOARD_USER}:${FOCALBOARD_PASSWORD}@postgres/${FOCALBOARD_DB}?sslmode=disable&connect_timeout=10"
`

Full docker-compose.yaml (Also, I've seen in another issue that you seek a postgres docker deployment and I'd be glad to share my project if that would be desirable):
```
version: "3.0"
services:
  nginx:
    restart: on-failure
    image: nginx:${NGINX_VERSION}
    volumes:
      - ./nginx/sites-available:/etc/nginx/sites-available
      - ./nginx/sites-enabled:/etc/nginx/sites-enabled
      - ./nginx/conf.d:/etc/nginx/conf.d
    ports:
      - ${NGINX_PORT}:80
    networks:
      - focalboard

  postgres:
    restart: on-failure
    image: postgres:${POSTGRES_VERSION}
    volumes:
      - postgresdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_USER=${POSTGRES_USER}
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}
    networks:
      - focalboard
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready", "-d", "${POSTGRES_USER}"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 60s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 80s

  init-db:
    image: postgres:${POSTGRES_VERSION}
    command: bash -c  "
      echo ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD} | psql -U ${POSTGRES_USER} -h postgres -W -c \"
        CREATE DATABASE ${FOCALBOARD_DB};\" -c \"
        CREATE USER ${FOCALBOARD_USER} WITH PASSWORD '${FOCALBOARD_PASSWORD}';\""
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_USER=${POSTGRES_USER}
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}
    networks:
      - focalboard
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy

  focalboard-server:
    restart: on-failure
    build: focalboard-server
    command: /opt/focalboard/bin/focalboard-server
    environment:
      - FOCALBOARD_SERVERROOT=http://0.0.0.0:8000
      - FOCALBOARD_DBTYPE=postgres
      - FOCALBOARD_DBCONFIG="postgres://${FOCALBOARD_USER}:${FOCALBOARD_PASSWORD}@postgres/${FOCALBOARD_DB}?sslmode=disable&connect_timeout=10"
    networks:
      - focalboard
    depends_on:
      init-db:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

volumes:
  postgresdata:

networks:
  focalboard:

```
* Sev 2: Affects major functionality with a difficult or non-obvious workaround
title: Bug: focalboard server ignores postgres hostname

triage.summary: Focalboard server ignores the hostname in a postgres connection URI and always attempts to connect to localhost ([::1]:5432). The connection string parser likely falls back to a default host when it fails to extract the host from the URI format, causing Docker deployments with remote postgres hostnames to fail.
triage.test_hint: Write a unit test for the postgres connection string parsing logic that supplies a URI with a non-localhost hostname (e.g. 'postgres://user:pass@postgres/boards?sslmode=disable') and asserts the resolved host is 'postgres', not 'localhost' or '[::1]'.

Some of them doesn't have connections between cards and attachments or images, and the problem is that i cant delete them even having and admin rights.

The board is disappeared.

## Error

```
{
  "caller": "app/plugin_api.go:980",
```

title: Bug: Can't delete board after import

triage.summary: Panic (slice bounds out of range [1:0]) in retrieveFileIDFromBlockFieldStorage when deleting a board that contains blocks with malformed/missing file attachment data, preventing board deletion even for admins.
triage.test_hint: In sqlstore/blocks.go around line 361, add a bounds check in retrieveFileIDFromBlockFieldStorage before slicing the string/slice; also add a unit test that imports a board with a block containing an empty or malformed file field and then deletes the board without panic.

title: Bug: Mattermost plugin not loading in v9.0.0

triage.summary: Focalboard plugin crashes on load in Mattermost v9.0.0 with RangeError: Incorrect locale information provided, thrown from Intl.supportedLocalesOf inside the locale/date formatting utility. The locale string passed (likely 'en') is not a valid BCP 47 tag on newer V8/Node runtimes used in MM v9.
triage.test_hint: Reproduce by loading Focalboard plugin with locale set to 'en'; fix by normalising locale to a valid BCP 47 tag (e.g. 'en-US') or wrapping Intl.supportedLocalesOf in a try/catch before calling it. Unit test the locale normalisation function with bare language codes like 'en', 'fr', 'de'.

body:
#### Summary
TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'getText')

#### Steps to reproduce
Mattermost Team Edition 7.8.3 with Boards 7.10.0
Desktop client 5.3.1 on Windows 10

1. Open an existing card on a board
2. Click in the field to add a comment
3. Hit Shift+Enter twice
4. Navigate up twice using the up-arrow key

#### Observed behavior (that appears unintentional) 
The red "A JavaScript error has occurred" banner appears, but the UI continues to function normally.
See attached log - both MatterPoll and Boards throw the error.

[boards.log](https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost-server/files/11431555/boards.log)
title: Boards: JS errors when adding blank lines to a new comment on a card and navigating with cursors keys

triage.summary: TypeError when navigating blank lines in card comment editor with arrow keys. The comment input's rich text editor does not guard against undefined nodes when calling getText() during cursor navigation in empty/blank lines created by Shift+Enter.
triage.test_hint: In a card comment field, press Shift+Enter twice to create blank lines, then press the up-arrow key twice and verify no JS error is thrown. Unit test should mock an editor state with empty/undefined block nodes and assert getText() is called safely.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Create a new board without using a template. You should get a kanban view grouped by status
2. Create a new card and change the type of the status property to be a non-groupable one (for example, URL)
3. The board should cause an error when trying to be visited

## Expected behavior

We should define a way to render the kanban when no groupable property exists or the one selected is non-groupable

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Mattermost Boards (plugin)
 - Version: v7.2-RC3
 - Browser and OS: Firefox on Linux
title: Bug: error when rendering a board with a bad group by property

triage.summary: Kanban board crashes when the group-by property is changed to a non-groupable type (e.g., URL). Need to handle missing/invalid groupBy property gracefully by falling back to a default groupable property or showing an empty/ungrouped state.
triage.test_hint: Change a Status property to URL type on a kanban board, then navigate to the board and verify no error is thrown; board should render in a fallback state or prompt the user to select a valid group-by property.

1. Go to a board with a kanban board
2. Go to the kanban board
3. Open a card
4. Remove the current group by property in the card (needs to be the last select property available)
5. It fails and redirects you to the error page

title: Bug: Deleting the current group by property generate a javascript error

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Create a board (e.g. from the Personal Tasks template)
2. Open a card, and delete all the Select properties
3. The board goes blank

## Expected behavior

The board should show the cards, under an empty group column.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Desktop / Personal Server / Mattermost Boards (plugin)
 - Version: v0.15
 - Browser and OS: Chrome on Mac

## Additional context (optional)

The error in the console is "ASSERT [Board views must have groupByProperty set]".

Note that other views (table, calendar, gallery) continue to work, but it's not obvious to the user how to fix this state - they see all the cards disappear.
title: Bug: Cards disappear after deleting last Select property

triage.summary: When the last Select property used as groupByProperty is deleted, the board view goes blank because groupByProperty becomes unset, triggering an assertion failure. The fix should handle the case where groupByProperty is null/undefined in board view rendering, gracefully falling back to an empty group or prompting the user to select a new grouping property.
triage.test_hint: Delete all Select properties from a board using a grouped board view; verify cards remain visible under an empty/ungrouped column and no console assertion errors appear.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Open a card
2. Type **Japanese** text on comments field and press enter key.
3. After the submit button is displayed for a moment, the text is lost and the submit button is invisible. (please see the screenshot)

## Expected behavior

The text should be entered into comments field and should be able to submit.

## Screenshots

![screenshot](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3491553/154037682-f379415c-ab26-42bd-b1a6-8544a250664a.gif)

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Desktop
 - Impacted version >= v0.12.0
     - Not reproduced in v0.11.0 (can enter the text and submit it.)
 - Browser and OS: Edge 96 on Windows 10

## Additional context

- Probably be related to #2273
- **When using copy and paste, text will be entered correctly.**
- This also reproduces in Mattermost plugin. (I tried Mattermost 6.3.2 and v0.12.1 plugin.)
title: Bug: Unable to enter Japanese text into comments field

triage.skip_reason: Requires UI/input event handling investigation and manual verification with IME input; no clear spec for fix
triage.summary: Japanese (IME) text input in the comments field is lost on Enter key press since v0.12.0. The Enter key event fires before IME composition is finalized, causing the text to be submitted/cleared prematurely. Likely related to #2273 where keydown/keyup handling was changed.
triage.test_hint: Check that the comment input uses compositionstart/compositionend events (or isComposing flag on KeyboardEvent) to guard Enter key handling, so IME composition is not interrupted. Reproduce with Japanese input on Windows using Edge or Chrome.

body:
**Summary:**
When a user tries to access a focalboard workspace for a channel the user is no longer a member of, the page keeps adding `/plugins/focalboard` to the URL constantly and trying to access the page. E.g:
`http://ha-test-server-ha-test-server.dev.spinmint.com:8066/plugins/focalboard/workspace/plugins/focalboard/plugins/focalboard/plugins/focalboard/plugins/focalboard/plugins/focalboard/plugins/focalboard/plugins/focalboard/plugins/focalboard/plugins/focalboard/plugins/focalboard/plugins/focalboard/plugins/focalboard/plugins/focalboard/plugins/focalboard ...........`

Eventually `414 Request-URI Too Large` is returned.

**Steps to reproduce the behavior:**
1. Create or join a channel
2. Open focalboard from within that channel
3. Leave the channel
4. Observe the focalboard tab - it keeps reloading

**Expected behavior:**
The page should throw some sort of permissions error and not reload constantly.

**gif:**
https://i.gyazo.com/e9bdfc25ad73d4463c0cd3c735e39107.mp4

Mattermost server version: master
Focalboard plugin version: 0.2.0
title: Bug: When workspace is no longer available to the user, `/plugins/focalboard` is constantly added to the path until 414 is returned

triage.summary: When workspace is inaccessible (user left channel), the frontend redirect loop keeps prepending '/plugins/focalboard' to the path, eventually causing a 414 URI Too Large error. The redirect/navigation logic needs to detect an unauthorized/unavailable workspace response and show an error state instead of looping.
triage.test_hint: Simulate a 403/404 response from the workspace API and assert that the app stops redirecting and displays an error or permission-denied message instead of appending to the path.

body:
**Summary:**
The title for new cards that gets created on board views keeps the "Untitled" text as its title.
This occurs only when editing the Description text directly after the title has been updated. It does still occur if changing e.g. the Status first, then changing the Title and lastly the Description.

**Steps to reproduce the behavior:**
1. Go to a board view.
2. Click on **+ New** to add a new card.
3. Update the Title to whatever.
4. Update the Description.
5. Close the card by pressing outside the card.
6. Notice that the Title has now been reverted back to "Untitled".

**Expected behavior:**
The Title to keep the provided text.

**Screenshots:**
When taking screenshots of the issue I noticed that the title actually is saved after taking a screenshot, so it seems to save when focus is moved from the application or similar.

**Platform:**
 - Windows 10, Focalboard v. 0.6.6 (Windows Store app).
title: Bug: Titles for new cards on board views keeps "Untitled" text

triage.summary: Title for new cards on board view reverts to 'Untitled' when the user edits the Description immediately after updating the Title, without first moving focus away from the title input. The title save/blur event is likely not being triggered before the description editor captures focus, causing the title change to be lost.
triage.test_hint: Create a new card, update title, immediately click into description field and type, then close the card — verify title is persisted. Also test: create card, update title, change a property (e.g. Status), update description, close — verify title persists.

body:
#### Summary
title.


#### Ticket Link
introduced in #4746, fixes #4947


#### Additional Information
I've not touched the bad query in [mm auth layer](https://github.com/mattermost-community/focalboard/blob/de5e5cc4141f14f69bf0e5666383997b81f851d6/server/services/store/mattermostauthlayer/mattermostauthlayer.go#L539-L561) because the schema is too different from the correct schema for the `file_info` table.
```sql
CREATE TABLE file_info (
    id varchar(26) NOT NULL,
    create_at BIGINT NOT NULL,
    delete_at BIGINT,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    extension VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    size BIGINT NOT NULL,
    archived BOOLEAN
, path varchar(512));
```
title: fix file info delete query

triage.summary: Fix incorrect SQL query for deleting file_info records, introduced in #4746. The mm auth layer variant is left unaddressed due to schema differences.
triage.test_hint: Write or run integration tests that delete a file attachment and verify the file_info row is marked deleted (delete_at set) without SQL errors.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Go to board
2. Select existing card (or create a new one)
3. Add checkbox or write in existing checkbox list
4. Try adding a new checkbox item or move an existing item up / down
5. see error - when trying to add a new one it deletes a previous checkbox, or even a checkbox in a different list; when trying to move them up/down it moves other checkboxes instead of the selected one. Sometimes the entire card freezes and have to refresh page

## Expected behavior

I expect to be able to add a new checkboxes without this affecting the rest of the content in the box. 
I expect to be able to move an item up/down when clicking "move up/move down" instead of seeing checkboxes moving randomly when selecting these functions.

## Screenshots (optional)

If applicable, add screenshots or a screen recording to elaborate on the problem.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal desktop and browser (have tried and experienced the same issue in both)
 - Version: v.8.0 on desktop
 - Browser and OS: Chrome on Windows 11

## Additional context (optional)

Add any other context about the problem here, and any notes about the severity:
* Sev 2: Affects major functionality with a difficult or non-obvious workaround - I can work around it by rewriting the content of the checkboxes but this is time consuming, non-obvious and non permanent either because the next time I need to move them or create a item the existing ones might get messed up again. I use this functionality for team meetings where we definitely need checkboxes for agenda and things to-do so it is an essential tool.
title: Bug: checkboxes in card not working properly

triage.skip_reason: No repro steps that are deterministic enough; likely involves complex editor state/ordering logic requiring manual investigation and UI interaction
triage.summary: Checkbox items in card editor behave incorrectly: adding a new checkbox deletes or corrupts existing ones, moving items up/down affects wrong checkboxes, and the card can freeze requiring a page refresh.
triage.test_hint: Focus on checkbox list block ordering logic in the card editor; test add/delete/reorder operations on multi-item checkbox lists, especially across multiple separate checkbox blocks.

title: Bug: No longer able to access board via channel permission

triage.skip_reason: Requires deep backend investigation into channel-based board permission logic; no clear repro path for automated fix; likely involves auth middleware or board membership resolution via channel membership
triage.summary: Users who are members of a channel with a linked board receive 403 'access denied to board' errors and cannot view the board, even though channel membership should grant access. The permission check appears to not correctly resolve channel membership into board access rights.
triage.test_hint: Test that a user who is a member of a channel linked to a board can successfully GET /api/v2/boards/{boardID} and view the board in the right panel without a 403 error.

title: Bug: Unable to input text in non-latin keyboard layout

triage.skip_reason: Requires investigation into keyboard event handling and React DOM manipulation for IME/non-latin input composition events; likely involves keydown/keypress handlers interfering with browser navigation shortcuts mapped to Cyrillic keys, plus a React DOM tree mutation error that needs careful debugging
triage.summary: Typing in non-latin keyboard layouts (e.g. Russian) in the card comment input causes a React DOM error (Node.removeChild: node is not a child) and navigates away from the card, showing 'card doesn't exist or is inaccessible' error. Likely a keydown handler incorrectly intercepting Cyrillic keypresses as hotkeys and triggering navigation, compounded by a React reconciliation bug.
triage.test_hint: Add an integration test: focus the comment input, simulate keydown/keypress events with Cyrillic character key codes (e.g. key='й', code='KeyQ'), and assert no navigation occurs and no DOM exception is thrown; also test with IME composition events (compositionstart/compositionupdate/compositionend).

body:
After uploading the latest version of the mattermost plugin, compiled on my computer following the installation documentation, I stumbled upon a rather annoying bug : cards or templates can’t be deleted and templates can’t be edited (at least they are not saved after edition).

## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Go to your mattermost team space, select Boards (mattermost plugin, compiled on my computer from the repo)
2. Go to any board
3. Select a card 
4. Click on ... and select delete
5. The card isn’t deleted

AND

1. Go to any board
2. Click on the down arrow next to New
3. Select an existing template, click on Edit
4. Change name and close modal
5. Go back to templates list
6. The name hasn’t been changed

## Expected behavior

Being able to delete cards and edit templates names

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Mattermost Boards (plugin)
 - Version: Latest
 - Browser and OS: Brave on MacOS

## Additional context (optional)

* Sev 1: Affects critical functionality without a workaround
title: Bug: Unable to delete cards or templates, unable to edit templates names in Boards (focalboard mattermost plugin)

triage.skip_reason: No repro on official build — self-compiled plugin; root cause unclear (could be build artifact, API permission, or frontend state issue). Needs environment details and server logs.
triage.summary: Cards cannot be deleted and template names cannot be saved in the Mattermost plugin build. Delete action silently fails; template name edits are not persisted.
triage.test_hint: Verify delete card API call succeeds (check network tab for 4xx/5xx), confirm template PATCH/PUT request is sent and returns 200, and test against an official plugin build to isolate self-compile issue.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Download and extract focalboard-win.zip v7.11.3 (following instructions from [the official website](https://www.focalboard.com/download/personal-edition/desktop/))
2. Select Focalboard.exe
3. Nothing happens. No error popup, no app icon in the taskbar
4. Restart computer & re-download
5. Select Focalboard.exe
6. Nothing happens still

## Expected behavior

App opens

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Desktop
 - Version: Any except for v0.6.1 (tried with v7.11.3, v7.0.1, 0.15.0, and v0.6.1 so far)
 - Browser and OS: Brave, Windows 10

## Additional context (optional)

I'm able to open v0.6.1 and the latest Microsoft Store versions successfully, but every other Personal Desktop version from github refuses to open. Same steps and behavior for each version

I strongly dislike using apps tied to Microsoft Store, so I want to get the standalone version working
title: Bug: Personal Desktop version doesn't start (Windows 10)

triage.skip_reason: No repro steps reproducible in CI; Windows-specific desktop crash with no logs/stack trace provided, requires local environment debugging and likely Electron/Windows compatibility investigation
triage.summary: Focalboard Personal Desktop (Windows 10) silently fails to launch for all versions after v0.6.1 when run as standalone exe; Microsoft Store version works fine, suggesting a packaging or Electron runtime/dependency issue introduced after v0.6.1
triage.test_hint: Compare Electron version and native dependency bundling between v0.6.1 (working) and v0.15.0+ (broken); check for missing VC++ redistributables, WebView2 runtime dependency, or code-signing issues that silently abort launch on Windows 10

body:
Reproduced for both 'mysql' and 'postgres'.

## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Use a 'Meeting Agenda' template board
2. Create a new Task, name it
3. Attach a file (a picture in my case).
4. Try deleting picture immediately (or close and open task and try deleting it).
5. The error on the server is:

MYSQL: 
```
Nov 27 08:14:05 debian-s-1vcpu-1gb-nyc3-01 focalboard-server[26049]: error [2023-11-27 08:14:05.962 Z] API ERROR caller="api/api.go:200" code=500 error="Error 1146 (42S02): Table 'boards.FileInfo' doesn't exist" api=/api/v2/boards/bhzb1f43dibrh7n3yyf37foumzw/blocks/ijwq4xxh4ajni7m56twothzw4yw
```

POSTGRES: 
```
Nov 27 08:03:59 debian-s-1vcpu-1gb-nyc3-01 focalboard-server[25505]: error [2023-11-27 08:03:59.086 Z] API ERROR caller="api/api.go:200" code=500 error="pq: relation "fileinfo" does not exist" api=/api/v2/boards/bkqgipcf35j8emfy5ntjiog9rpe/blocks/ipc3rzfgqqjfj7qjyt6crsi415c
```
## Expected behavior

An attachment should be deleted with no issues.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Server
 - Version: v7.11.3
 - Browser and OS: Firefox, Safari on Mac.

## Additional context (optional)

 - Server is working on Debian.
Add any other context about the problem here, and any notes about the severity:
* Sev 1: Affects critical functionality without a workaround
* Sev 2: Affects major functionality with a difficult or non-obvious workaround
* Sev 3: Affects minor, non-critical functionality
title: Bug: Error deleting an attachment

triage.skip_reason: Requires database schema investigation and backend fix; unclear if FileInfo table is missing from migrations or if wrong DB is being queried; needs environment reproduction
triage.summary: Deleting an attachment fails with a 500 error because the 'FileInfo' table does not exist in the Focalboard database for both MySQL and PostgreSQL. The backend appears to be querying a table that was never created via migrations, or is incorrectly referencing a Mattermost-only table.
triage.test_hint: Verify that the FileInfo table migration exists and runs correctly for both mysql and postgres standalone deployments; write an integration test that attaches a file to a card and then deletes it, asserting no 500 error is returned.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Install the latest focalboard with the guides provided in https://www.focalboard.com/download/personal-edition/ubuntu/
  - installed with postgresql
  - focalboard v7.11.3 fresh installation
2. Create account, create board, ...
3. Add any file on `Attachment` on board
4. Delete file on `Attachment`

## Expected behavior

File needs to be deleted and queries need to be executed as expected.

## Current behavior

File does not get deleted. Cards with attachments will never be deleted.

The log show that the ```relation `fileinfo`  does not exist.```.

```sh
$ systemctl status focalboard.service
...
Sep 29 00:16:43 apps focalboard-server[65981]: debug [2023-09-29 00:16:43.394 +09:00] Broadcast block change                   caller="ws/server.go:554" teamID=0 blockID=(block_id) remoteAddr="[::1]:38372"
Sep 29 00:16:43 apps focalboard-server[65981]: debug [2023-09-29 00:16:43.394 +09:00] Broadcast block change                   caller="ws/server.go:554" teamID=0 blockID=(block_id) remoteAddr="[::1]:37072"
Sep 29 00:16:43 apps focalboard-server[65981]: debug [2023-09-29 00:16:43.395 +09:00] POST Blocks                              caller="api/blocks.go:318" block_count=1 disable_notify=false
Sep 29 00:16:43 apps focalboard-server[65981]: debug [2023-09-29 00:16:43.398 +09:00] Block change event                       caller="notifylogger/logger_backend.go:48" action=add board="board" card="test" block_id=(block_id)
Sep 29 00:16:43 apps focalboard-server[65981]: debug [2023-09-29 00:16:43.522 +09:00] attachSession                            caller="api/auth.go:333" single_user=false
Sep 29 00:17:21 apps focalboard-server[65981]: debug [2023-09-29 00:17:21.121 +09:00] attachSession                            caller="api/auth.go:333" single_user=false
Sep 29 00:17:21 apps focalboard-server[65981]: error [2023-09-29 00:17:21.148 +09:00] pq: relation "fileinfo" does not exist   caller="api/api.go:183"
Sep 29 00:17:21 apps focalboard-server[65981]: error [2023-09-29 00:17:21.148 +09:00] API ERROR                                caller="api/api.go:200" code=500 error="pq: relation "fileinfo" does not exist" api=/api/v2/boards/(board_name)/blocks/(block_id)
```

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Server
 - Version: v7.11.3
 - Browser and OS: Debian Bookworm

## Additional context

I think the focalboard was using `fileinfo` relation in the past. but now it seems to be replaced to `file_info`. (Correct me if I'm wrong)
I don't really know why `fileinfo` is still used when `file_info` exists.

psql log relation list

```sh
$ sudo --login --user postgres
postgres@apps:~$ psql
psql (15.3 (Debian 15.3-0+deb12u1))

# \dt
                  List of relations

 public | db_lock               | table | focalboard
 public | file_info             | table | focalboard
 public | notification_hints    | table | focalboard
 public | preferences           | table | focalboard
 public | schema_migrations     | table | focalboard
 public | sessions              | table | focalboard
 public | sharing               | table | focalboard
 public | subscriptions         | table | focalboard
 public | system_settings       | table | focalboard
 public | teams                 | table | focalboard
 public | users                 | table | focalboard
(19 rows)
```
title: Bug: Relation `fileinfo` not found

## Steps to reproduce the behavior

- I'm running stand-alone Focalboard 7.10.4 in Docker (left screenshot)
- I spun up a Mattermost instance in Docker with Boards 7.11.2 (right screenshot)
- I exported my board from Focalboard to a `boardarchive` file
- When importing the `boardarchive` to Mattermost, all of the timestamps are wrong
- Also, the order of the items in the columns is lost (unsure if this is related to the timestamp changes)

Below you can see the left (Focalboard) has the correct time of 10 months ago, while the right (Mattermost import) has the incorrect time of 12 days ago
![Screenshot_20230829_115834](https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/assets/4955337/52bb70e5-7724-4423-9202-bfc4ba26abc0)

Here is the JSON from the Focalboard export for that entry.
```
{"type":"block","data":{"id":"ayiaef857sjy1tb5psu3hkp9pka","parentId":"cukrxnfw8n3dmjnbgjc1n3uz3wa","createdBy":"u1b6q77ixji8mtg84agcfbfp6ew","modifiedBy":"u1b6q77ixji8mtg84agcfbfp6ew","schema":1,"type":"comment","title":"I think this is done, but Ansible playbooks will need to be redone to ensure it's done for the correct user","fields":{},"createAt":1667328548338,"updateAt":1667328548338,"deleteAt":0,"boardId":"bfy769tekmtgw9gcuzc8b5cqsne"}}
```

You can see the timestamp is `1667328548338`, which was 10 months ago (the correct time). I'm unsure how Mattermost is getting 12 days ago...
![Screenshot_20230829_120249](https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/assets/4955337/be1cca08-4b67-4c92-a29d-758fa965777b)

Here is a different example where the time on the import was updated to the current time...

![Screenshot_20230829_120757](https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/assets/4955337/ce94ab9a-defd-4ffc-8cb5-0f0fdf62fa6b)

```
{"type":"block","data":{"id":"aent5ct6fjid678h7irtu74e7ry","parentId":"cu56gdg5dnbdumqa4ntkijn4fra","createdBy":"u1b6q77ixji8mtg84agcfbfp6ew","modifiedBy":"u1b6q77ixji8mtg84agcfbfp6ew","schema":1,"type":"comment","title":"https://docs.netgate.com/pfsense/en/latest/recipes/dns-over-tls.html#enable-dns-over-tls-server-optional","fields":{},"createAt":1681324659924,"updateAt":1681324659924,"deleteAt":0,"boardId":"bfy769tekmtgw9gcuzc8b5cqsne"}}
```

## Expected behavior

I think I should be given the option to either preserve timestamps, or completely overwrite them with the current timestamps.

See above...

After updating Mattermost (Team Edition) from 7.1.5 (ESR) to 7.8.2 (ESR) or from 7.8.2 to 7.8.9 (ESR) the following error occurs on startup. Running `journalctl -u mattermost -r` gives the following output:
> Aug 20 14:21:54 mattermost mattermost[1370]: {"timestamp":"2023-08-20 14:21:54.498 +02:00","level":"error","msg":"Failed to sync plugin from file store","caller":"app/plugin.go:366","bundle":"plugins/focalboard.tar.gz","error":"installExtractedPlugin: Unable to restart plugin on upgrade., error initializing the DB: Error 1054: Unknown column 'dirty' in 'field list' in line 0: SELECT version, dirty FROM `focalboard_schema_migrations` LIMIT 1"}
> 
> Aug 20 14:21:54 mattermost mattermost[1370]: {"timestamp":"2023-08-20 14:21:54.478 +02:00","level":"error","msg":"Table creation / migration failed","caller":"app/plugin_api.go:976","plugin_id":"focalboard","error":"\"Error 1054: Unknown column 'dirty' in 'field list' in line 0: SELECT version, dirty FROM `focalboard_schema_migrations` LIMIT 1\""}


## Steps to reproduce the behavior

Update Mattermost (Team Edition) from 7.1.5 (ESR) to 7.8.2 (ESR) or 7.8.9 (ESR):
```
sudo su
systemctl stop mattermost

# Backup
cd /opt
rm -rf mattermost-back* mattermost-upgrade
cp -ra mattermost/ mattermost-back-$(date +'%F-%H-%M')/

# Upgrade
wget https://releases.mattermost.com/7.8.9/mattermost-team-7.8.9-linux-amd64.tar.gz
tar -xf mattermost*.gz --transform='s,^[^/]\+,\0-upgrade,'

find mattermost/ mattermost/client/ -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 \! \( -type d \( -path mattermost/client -o -path mattermost/client/plugins -o -path mattermost/config -o -path mattermost/logs -o -path mattermost/plugins -o -path mattermost/data \) -prune \) | sort | sudo xargs rm -r

cp -an mattermost-upgrade/. mattermost/
chown -R mattermost:mattermost mattermost
setcap cap_net_bind_service=+ep ./mattermost/bin/mattermost

# Restart service
systemctl start mattermost

# View log
journalctl -u mattermost -r
```

## Expected behavior
Mattermost should start without errors.

## Edition and Platform
 - Mattermost Boards (plugin)
 - Version: 7.8.2
 - Server OS: Ubuntu 20.04

body:
## Overview

I'm running Focalboard in Kubernetes. My database is a separate Postgres 13 VM that uses a Let's Encrypt SSL certificate (setup like [this](https://www.postgresql.org/docs/13/ssl-tcp.html#SSL-SERVER-FILES)).

I was stuck on Focalboard `7.5.2` for a while because of [this](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/4651) bug.  I just tried to upgrade to `7.8.4` and was met with the error below. This occurs on `7.9.3` also.



```
2023/04/07 18:09:59 readConfigFile
2023/04/07 18:09:59 {ServerRoot:http://localhost:8000 Port:8000 DBType:postgres DBConfigString:postgres://username:password@db05.internal.mydomain.com:5432/dbfocalboard?sslmode=verify-full&connect_timeout=10 DBTablePrefix: UseSSL:false SecureCookie:false WebPath:./pack FilesDriver:local FilesS3Config:{AccessKeyID: SecretAccessKey: Bucket: PathPrefix: Region: Endpoint: SSL:false SignV2:false SSE:false Trace:false Timeout:0} FilesPath:/data/files MaxFileSize:0 Telemetry:true TelemetryID: PrometheusAddress: WebhookUpdate:[] Secret: SessionExpireTime:2592000 SessionRefreshTime:18000 LocalOnly:false EnableLocalMode:true LocalModeSocketLocation:/var/tmp/focalboard_local.socket EnablePublicSharedBoards:false FeatureFlags:map[] EnableDataRetention:false DataRetentionDays:0 TeammateNameDisplay:username ShowEmailAddress:false ShowFullName:false AuthMode:native LoggingCfgFile: LoggingCfgJSON: AuditCfgFile: AuditCfgJSON: NotifyFreqCardSeconds:0 NotifyFreqBoardSeconds:0}
info  [2023-04-07 18:09:59.677 Z] Focalboard server                        caller="model/version.go:60" version=7.8.4 edition=linux build_number=dev build_date=n/a build_hash=6fc1d9f8db9d366a16cd0ce18d34a5d7b8d5b1e5
error [2023-04-07 18:09:59.684 Z] Database Ping failed                     caller="server/server.go:220" error="x509: certificate signed by unknown authority"
fatal [2023-04-07 18:09:59.684 Z] server.NewStore ERROR                    caller="main/main.go:136" error="x509: certificate signed by unknown authority"
  main.main
      /go/src/focalboard/server/main/main.go:136
  runtime.main
      /usr/local/go/src/runtime/proc.go:250
  runtime.goexit
      /usr/local/go/src/runtime/asm_amd64.s:1571

```

I had to change `sslmode=verify-full` to `sslmode=require` to get Focalboard to start.  From [this](https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/lib/pq#hdr-Connection_String_Parameters) page for the Go Postgres driver:

> Valid values for sslmode are:
>  *  disable - No SSL
>  *  require - Always SSL (skip verification)
>  *  verify-ca - Always SSL (verify that the certificate presented by the server was signed by a trusted CA)
>  *  verify-full - Always SSL (verify that the certification presented by the server was signed by a trusted CA and the server host name matches the one in the certificate)

It is now working with `7.8.4` and `7.9.3`. Did something change with the included package of `ca-certificates` in the Docker image? The SSL cert I'm using is signed by Let's Encrypt, so it should be accepted everywhere...


## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Run Postgres in a separate VM using a Let's Encrypt SSL certificate
2. Try to run Focalboard `7.8.4` in Docker using `sslmode=verify-full`  in the Postgres connection string
3. Error

## Expected behavior

Focalboard should accept CA certificates from Let's Encrypt.

## Screenshots (optional)

N/A

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Server
 - Version: 7.8.4
 - Browser and OS: Firefox+Chrome on Linux (Kubernetes)

## Additional context (optional)

N/A
title: Bug: `x509: certificate signed by unknown authority`

triage.skip_reason: Requires understanding of TLS/certificate trust chain configuration in Go database drivers and Kubernetes deployment context; environment-specific and no trivial fix
triage.summary: Focalboard fails to connect to PostgreSQL with SSL (sslmode=verify-full) when the server certificate is signed by Let's Encrypt, because the Go x509 certificate pool does not trust the CA. Regression from 7.5.2 to 7.8.4+.
triage.test_hint: Spin up a Postgres instance with a Let's Encrypt (or self-signed CA) TLS cert, configure DBConfigString with sslmode=verify-full, and verify Focalboard connects successfully without x509 unknown authority error. Also test sslmode=require and sslrootcert parameter passthrough.

title: Cannot start instance: Migration error 'boards' - no rows in result set

triage.skip_reason: No repro steps beyond docker pull; root cause in DB migration collation query returning no rows needs investigation of MySQL 8.0.32 schema state
triage.summary: Boards service fails to start on MySQL 8.0.32 during collation/charset migration because the query returns no rows, preventing the entire Mattermost server from starting.
triage.test_hint: Add handling for sql.ErrNoRows in the charSet fetching logic within the fix-collations-and-charsets migration; write an integration test against a fresh MySQL 8.0.32 instance to ensure the migration completes or skips gracefully when the expected row is absent.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

Cannot be use, cannot change title, cannot move things, error logs shown this:

> {"timestamp":"2023-01-26 05:14:30.778 Z","level":"error","msg":"Error 1062: Duplicate entry 'c3x4b75znn7nnuy3smzt6xoik9a-0000-00-00 00:00:00' for key 'PRIMARY'","caller":"app/plugin_api.go:976","plugin_id":"focalboard"}
> {"timestamp":"2023-01-26 05:14:30.782 Z","level":"error","msg":"API ERROR","caller":"app/plugin_api.go:976","plugin_id":"focalboard","code":"500","error":"\"Error 1062: Duplicate entry 'c3x4b75znn7nnuy3smzt6xoik9a-0000-00-00 00:00:00' for key 'PRIMARY'\"","api":"/api/v2/boards/b75zuzydbp3dpxgxhgfribmphhr/blocks/c3x4b75znn7nnuy3smzt6xoik9a"}

Bug reported from our [feedback form](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1fZIy5WChN40eNJ95nUMb2a2ZwUzc_cqCDlS4_7fOmPs/edit?resourcekey#gid=1652426489&range=470:470)

## Expected behavior

Able to edit cards in Boards.

## Screenshots (optional)

If applicable, add screenshots or a screen recording to elaborate on the problem.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Mattermost Boards (plugin)
 - Version: v7.5.1
 - Browser and OS:
title: Bug: Unable to edit cards in Boards

triage.skip_reason: No repro steps beyond error logs; requires DB investigation into duplicate primary key with zero-date timestamp, likely a migration or upsert bug needing deep backend analysis
triage.summary: Duplicate primary key error (block_id + '0000-00-00 00:00:00') on block update causes 500 errors, making cards completely uneditable. Likely caused by a malformed insert/upsert that writes a zero-value timestamp into the primary key, conflicting on subsequent updates.
triage.test_hint: Reproduce by updating a block via PUT /api/v2/boards/{boardID}/blocks/{blockID} on a MySQL instance; check focalboard_blocks and focalboard_blocks_history tables for rows with insert_at = '0000-00-00 00:00:00'; investigate the upsert/history insert logic in store/sqlstore for missing or zero timestamp handling.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Open Focalboard. notice the port in the window title.
2. Select **Settings** -> Turn off **Random Icons**.
3. Select **Settings** -> **Set theme**, and select any theme.
4. Open any board.
5. Close Focalboard.
6. Open it again.
7. Nothing changed and check the window title, you'll see a different port, also the board I opened didn't open again!!

## Expected behavior

- The random icons settings should be turned off as I selected.
- The theme should be changed as I selected.
- The port should be constant and shouldn't changed each time.
- The board I closed the app on, when I open it again, it should open that board.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Desktop
 - Version: 7.5.2
 - Browser:  another problem here.. My default browser is Chrome, but it opens in Edge!!
 - OS: Windows 11
title: Bug: Personal desktop app choose different port each time and don't save the settings

triage.skip_reason: Multiple interrelated issues (port persistence, settings persistence, last-opened board, browser choice) in desktop Electron app require manual investigation of config storage and IPC layer
triage.summary: Personal Desktop app (Windows 11, v7.5.2) fails to persist settings (random icons, theme), picks a random port on each launch instead of a fixed one, does not restore the last-opened board on restart, and opens links in the wrong browser (Edge instead of Chrome).
triage.test_hint: Verify that the desktop app writes config to a stable location on Windows, that the port is saved and reused across restarts, that theme/random-icons preferences survive a restart, and that the last-opened board URL is restored on next launch.

title: Bug: App doesn't start

triage.skip_reason: Crash involves native webview/CGo SIGSEGV on Linux desktop, likely requires system-level debugging and platform-specific fix; no clear minimal repro beyond launching the app
triage.summary: Personal Desktop app crashes on launch with SIGSEGV inside webview CGo call on Ubuntu 22.04 (v7.4.3). Signal conflict between JSC GC signal and Go runtime causes fatal panic during webview_run.
triage.test_hint: Reproduce on Ubuntu 22.04 with v7.4.3 desktop build; check WebKit/JSC signal handling conflict with Go runtime signal SIGSEGV at webview_run; consider JSC_SIGNAL_FOR_GC env var workaround and upgrading webview dependency.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Leave Boards open overnight in a browser or in the desktop app
2. Go to the Boards tab/window the next day
3. See error - I get error message that I am not logged in or an error has occurred. If I switch to Channels then back to Boards from the app switcher, Boards loads just fine again.

## Expected behavior

I remain logged into Boards.

## Screenshots (optional)
From multiple sessions:

### Chrome

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/93531870/194361198-290a4307-0575-49a6-9c2d-1087e4d0f598.mov

### Chrome Incognito
https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/93531870/194357064-5ac040b6-cba3-4fa9-b80a-96d49d56f830.mov

### Desktop app
https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/93531870/194357078-b1e461e9-fe97-435c-9de0-bb4f6781f276.mov



## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Mattermost Boards (plugin)
 - Version: v7.3 Bug Bash
 - Browser and OS: Chrome on Mac, MM Desktop app on Mac
title: Bug: Constantly logged out of Boards when left open overnight/extended periods

triage.skip_reason: Session/token refresh logic likely involves both frontend and backend auth flow; requires investigation of token expiry handling and reconnection logic across plugin boundaries
triage.summary: Users are logged out of Boards after leaving it open for extended periods (overnight). The session appears to expire or the auth token is not refreshed properly. Switching away and back to Boards resolves it temporarily, suggesting the issue is with token renewal or WebSocket reconnection rather than actual session expiry.
triage.test_hint: Leave Boards open for 8+ hours (or mock token expiry), then verify the page recovers gracefully without showing a logout/error screen; check that auth token refresh is triggered on visibility/focus restore.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

In both of the below cases, the backdrops/text on certain properties such as column labels, statuses, and multiselect properties appears as light-on-light making them difficult to read.

#### Repro steps 1
1. In Channels, select a dark theme
2. Switch to Boards using one of the following methods that opens Boards in a new tab:
    1. Clicking on the App Bar button in the top right and selecting a board to navigate to
    2. Pasting the URL for a board into the address bar

#### Repro steps 2
1. In Channels, select a dark theme
2. Switch to Boards using one of the following methods that opens Boards in the current tab:
    1. Clicking on the product switcher in the top left and selecting Boards
    2. Clicking on a URL for a board pasted in a message
3. Note that those elements appear correctly coloured at this point
4. Refresh the page

## Expected behavior

The text appears readable as it does when you switch using the methods that open Boards in the current tab

## Screenshots (optional)

![Screen Shot 2022-09-08 at 11 22 54 AM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3277310/189163389-a256f23c-1609-4476-a2c7-da0315a3a095.png)

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Boards plugin on community.mattermost.com
 - Version: v7.2.1
title: Bug: Dark themes sometimes applied incorrectly in plugin/product

triage.skip_reason: UI/theming issue requiring visual verification and understanding of plugin/product theme initialization flow; no clear minimal fix without deeper investigation
triage.summary: Dark theme from Mattermost Channels is not correctly applied to Boards when opened in a new tab or after a page refresh, causing light-on-light text rendering on column labels, statuses, and multiselect properties.
triage.test_hint: Test by setting a dark theme in Channels, then opening Boards via App Bar or direct URL in a new tab, and also by refreshing after navigating via product switcher; verify color labels and multiselect chips are readable.

title: Bug: migration issue re: pg_type_typname_nsp_index

triage.skip_reason: Requires deep understanding of PostgreSQL migration internals and the focalboard_workspaces table lifecycle; unclear if this is a schema conflict, leftover type artifact, or migration ordering issue — needs investigation before a fix can be attempted
triage.summary: PostgreSQL migration fails with duplicate key violation on pg_type_typname_nsp_index when installing focalboard-v7.0.1 as a prepackaged plugin. The CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS for focalboard_workspaces conflicts with an existing pg type entry, suggesting a stale or partial schema artifact from a prior installation.
triage.test_hint: Reproduce by upgrading Mattermost with focalboard-v7.0.1 prepackaged plugin on a PostgreSQL instance that previously had focalboard installed; check for leftover type entries in pg_type matching focalboard_workspaces and verify migration idempotency.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Create a team (Team A).
2. Create some boards in Team A.
3. Try searching for those boards, and notice they do come up in search results, which is all good.
4. Not leave this team (team A).
5. Go back to boards, and try searching for those boards you created in Team A. Notice that they still show up in search results. You can, however, not join them.

## Expected behavior

Boards corresponding to a team a user is not part of should not show up in search results.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Mattermost Boards (plugin)
 - Version: 0.16
title: Bug: Leaving a team doesn't remove user's board memberships

triage.skip_reason: Requires understanding of team membership lifecycle hooks and board membership cleanup logic; no clear spec for fix approach; likely involves backend data consistency across services
triage.summary: Leaving a Mattermost team does not remove the user's board memberships for boards in that team, causing those boards to still appear in search results even though the user cannot join them.
triage.test_hint: Verify that after a user leaves a team, their board memberships for boards in that team are removed and those boards no longer appear in search results for that user.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Open Focalboard
2. Do any action or wait for up to two minutes
3. Session expires

## Expected behavior

On desktop the session should never expire

## Screenshots

**Summary:**
When launching Focalboard Personal Desktop from the command line, a blank window opens and stays that way.

3. See the blank window open and stay blank

**Expected behavior:**
Focalboard should launch on home content.

![Focalboard blank window](https://i.ibb.co/wz6JvK9/focal-board-launch-blank.png)

**Platform:**
* Debian 10 Buster
* GNOME 3
(both are up-to-date)

```
$ /opt/focalboard-app/focalboard-app
Overriding existing handler for signal 10. Set JSC_SIGNAL_FOR_GC if you want WebKit to use a different signal

```
title: Bug: blank window on startup

triage.skip_reason: No repro environment details sufficient to debug; likely platform-specific Electron/WebKit rendering issue on Linux/GNOME requiring manual investigation
triage.summary: Focalboard Personal Desktop shows a blank window on startup when launched from command line on Debian 10 / GNOME 3. Terminal output indicates a WebKit signal handler override (signal 10), suggesting a WebKit/Electron initialization issue on Linux that prevents the app from rendering content.
triage.test_hint: Launch the desktop app on a Debian/Ubuntu Linux system with GNOME and verify the main window renders content instead of a blank screen; check Electron/WebKit console for errors related to signal handling or page load failures.

## Expected behavior

The linked board should be visible for all channel members as defined by the general access rules "everyone in team abc is editor" and "everyone in channel xyz is editor".

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Mattermost Boards (plugin)
 - Version: v8.0.0
 - Mattermost server version: v10.1.2

## Additional Context

For some existing linked boards this behaviour changed recently, thus, this issue does also occur for some already existing linked boards without any additional actions done by the board admins in the access permissions list.

2. Re-linking the board might help but not reliably for all channel members.
title: Bug: Some linked boards are not displayed anymore for most channel members

triage.skip_reason: Complex permissions/access control regression affecting channel-linked board visibility; requires deep investigation of permission resolution logic, no clear minimal repro path, and touches backend auth/data layer
triage.summary: Channel-linked boards are not visible to most channel members unless they are explicitly listed as editors in the permissions list. The general access rules ('everyone in team/channel is editor') are not being applied correctly for linked boards, representing a regression in permission inheritance logic introduced around v8.0.0.
triage.test_hint: Test that a user who is a channel member but NOT explicitly listed in board permissions can view a channel-linked board; verify board permission resolver correctly falls back to channel/team-level access rules when no explicit user entry exists.

    But the same board which worked on my desktop app is showing joining private board message on web version. 
5. For the one which works on my desktop app here is the url
  https://mattermost-server/plugins/focalboard/api/v2/teams/w8orwn8u7bg4x8da9gqosuomna/users
6. On web, i am getting `{"error":"User IDs are empty","errorCode":400} ` error

7. Other errors: That  is something like this

URL: https://mattermost-server/plugins/focalboard/api/v2/boards/b8dy7huuhtpdftx385htrmn4agh/blocks?all=true

Response: `{"error":"{boards} not found","errorCode":404}`

## Expected behavior

All the boards should work on both web and desktop and all the users must be able to see the same output.

If applicable, add screenshots or a screen recording to elaborate on the problem.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Desktop / Personal Server / Mattermost Boards (plugin)
 - Version: [v8.0]
 - Browser and OS: Chrome on Mac
title: Bug: After mattermost migration from MySQL to Postgresql boards are behaving strangely

triage.skip_reason: Data integrity issue after DB migration with no clear repro steps beyond the migration itself; requires deep investigation of MySQL-to-PostgreSQL migration artifacts, ID casing/collation differences, and plugin state inconsistencies. No isolated test case available.
triage.summary: After migrating Mattermost from MySQL to PostgreSQL, Focalboard boards behave inconsistently: some boards work on desktop but show 'join private board' on web, board block lookups return 404, and team user lookups return 400 'User IDs are empty'. Likely caused by data migration artifacts such as ID casing differences, collation mismatches, or incomplete data transfer between database engines.
triage.test_hint: Verify board and block IDs are consistent across the migrated PostgreSQL DB; check for case-sensitivity differences in ID columns between MySQL and PostgreSQL; confirm foreign key references between boards, blocks, and team membership tables are intact post-migration; reproduce by running the official pgloader/migration script against a known MySQL dump and querying the affected API endpoints.

triage.skip_reason: Build toolchain/GLIBC compatibility issue requiring changes to the build environment or CI pipeline; large scope with potential cross-platform implications and no trivial fix

triage.test_hint: Reproduce by inducing a missing category ID error then a DB connection drop; monitor goroutine count and connection pool usage to identify retry/loop amplification

## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Set the theme to dark theme (not an issue in light/default theme)
2. Open a board where cards use the `Assignee` field
3. Select/create a card and note the text in the `Assignee` field matches the background color

## Expected behavior

In the dark theme, most text should inherit its color using the CSS variable `--center-channel-color-rgb: 220, 220, 220;`. However, this does not seem to be the case for the Assignee field. In my testing, the text in this field is visible in lighter themes. It appears as if the RGB value of `51,51,51` is hardcoded in the styling for that item rather than using the theme variable.

I tried hard reloading with a clean cache, new browsers, and incognito windows, and the issue persisted.

## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Go to the template selector
2. Select "+New Template"
3. Enter a long name for the template, e.g. _Another template with a very long name_
4. Return to template selector
5. Observe that the template name is truncated
6. Move the cursor over the template title

## Expected behavior

Full title should be displayed on hover

## Screenshots

![template_titles](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15314183/152404797-ec050ecf-23eb-49c5-a41b-e04094e13dfb.jpg)

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Desktop / Personal Server / Mattermost plugin
 - Version: v0.14
 - Browser and OS: Chrome on Windows
title: Bug: Template selector > No way to see full text for long template titles

triage.summary: Template selector truncates long template titles without showing a tooltip on hover to reveal the full name.
triage.test_hint: Add a title attribute or tooltip to the template name element in the template selector so that hovering over a truncated title displays the full text.

body: Element is in tab order and does not have accessible text ([more info](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.3/link-name?application=AxeChrome)). Fix at least one of these issues: - Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers - aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty - aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty - Element has no title attribute Element location: `.help-button` Element source: `` ![help_button](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15314183/152164236-c2d6627b-6156-4f00-9579-36a4bc3121fb.jpg)
title: Bug: Accessibility > Help button should have discernible text

triage.summary: Help button anchor element lacks accessible text (aria-label/title), causing screen reader accessibility failure for the .help-button element in GlobalHeaderComponent.
triage.test_hint: Add aria-label='Help' (or i18n equivalent) to the help-button anchor element and verify with axe accessibility tooling that the link-name rule passes.

body:
**Summary:**
Styling for visited links makes them unreadable for dark theme when the board is opened from the mattermost channel.

**Steps to reproduce the behavior:**
1. Go to [focalboard channel](https://community.mattermost.com/core/channels/focalboard)
2. Set dark theme in settings, for example "Indigo"
3. Use pinned message and open "Meeting Notes" board
4. Open any card that has a link in the description
5. Visit the link in the browser
6. Return to the card and see that visited link is unreadable

**Expected behavior:**
Visited link should be easily readable

**Screenshots:**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5587620/132628399-21f172d0-5ab7-4ec1-b3a8-f3f7565d9956.png)

**Platform:**
 - Browser and version: Chrome 92
 - OS and version: Windows 10

**Additional context:**
Dark theme for standalone server doesn't have this issue but contrast is still low.
title: Bug: unreadable visited links in the card description for dark theme

triage.summary: Visited links in card description are unreadable in dark theme because the browser default visited-link color (purple) is not overridden for dark backgrounds. Fix requires adding a CSS rule for `a:visited` in the dark theme stylesheet.
triage.test_hint: Open a card with a previously-visited link while dark theme is active and verify the link color has sufficient contrast against the dark background.

title: Bug: vertical scrollbar in gallery view if text to large

triage.summary: Gallery card shows vertical scrollbar when card text content overflows; fix is to add `overflow: hidden` to `.GalleryCard` in galleryCard.scss.
triage.test_hint: Add a card with a long single-block text in gallery view, reload, and verify no vertical scrollbar appears on the card.

body:
**Summary:**
Docker build is failing because build context is wrong.

**Steps to reproduce the behavior:**
1. `git clone https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard.git && cd focalboard/docker`
2. `sudo docker build -t focalboard .`

Here you can see the error because the build context is wrong.
```
Sending build context to Docker daemon  9.216kB
Step 1/21 : FROM node:16.3.0 as nodebuild
16.3.0: Pulling from library/node
0bc3020d05f1: Pull complete 
a110e5871660: Pull complete 
83d3c0fa203a: Pull complete 
a8fd09c11b02: Pull complete 
14feb89c4a52: Pull complete 
612a5de913f3: Pull complete 
b86d81a99d41: Pull complete 
5dd61d4ad9e8: Pull complete 
7aae82345965: Pull complete 
Digest: sha256:ca6daf1543242acb0ca59ff425509eab7defb9452f6ae07c156893db06c7a9a4
Status: Downloaded newer image for node:16.3.0
 ---> 7105279fa2ab
Step 2/21 : WORKDIR /webapp
 ---> Running in 487d72a19c79
Removing intermediate container 487d72a19c79
 ---> 1fa79d2a5398
Step 3/21 : ADD webapp/ /webapp
ADD failed: file not found in build context or excluded by .dockerignore: stat webapp/: file does not exist
```

**Expected behavior:**
Expected to build successfully, but the context is wrong.

The commands on [this page](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/blob/main/docker/README.md) need to be updated. This works for me.
```
git clone https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard.git
sudo docker build -f docker/Dockerfile -t focalboard .
```

**Platform:**
 - OS and version: Arch Linux, kernel 5.13.7-arch1-1
title: Bug: Docker build error

triage.summary: Docker build fails when run from the docker/ subdirectory because the build context is wrong; README and/or Dockerfile need to be updated to use `docker build -f docker/Dockerfile -t focalboard .` from the repo root.
triage.test_hint: Run `sudo docker build -f docker/Dockerfile -t focalboard .` from the repository root and verify it completes successfully.

body:
Changed go image version according to updated dependencies in commit c8e729b6fed16f28aa69e01517eed68c92b1a5ca

<!-- Thank you for contributing a pull request! Here are a few tips to help you:

1. If this is your first contribution, make sure you've read the Contribution Checklist https://developers.mattermost.com/contribute/getting-started/contribution-checklist/
2. Read our blog post about "Submitting Great PRs" https://developers.mattermost.com/blog/2019-01-24-submitting-great-prs
3. Take a look at other repository specific documentation at https://developers.mattermost.com/contribute

Assign two reviewers for this pull request from the names suggested. If no names are suggested or you are not sure who to assign, set `Core Focalboard` as the reviewer.
-->

#### Summary
<!--
A description of what this pull request does, as well as QA test steps (if applicable and if not already added to the ticket).
-->
Right now Dockerfile is broken, because c8e729b6fed16f28aa69e01517eed68c92b1a5ca bumped `golang` version to `1.21`, but in Dockerfile `1.18` is used for build. `go.mod` file is using `toolchain` keyword, that was introduced in `1.21` - it leads into broken build:

![Screenshot_20240617_213212](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/assets/39347109/03dbf001-780d-4709-8935-6a22f6fe4c0e)

**Solution**: updated version of the golang in Dockerfile to [1.21.8@sha256:856073656d1a517517792e6cdd2f7a5ef080d3ca2dff33e518c8412f140fdd2d](https://hub.docker.com/layers/library/golang/1.21.8/images/sha256-e28f8918b44f85c5200599ff039da6004eb19081d00004b2985f10fc1be0794c?context=explore)

After that change build is working:
![Screenshot_20240617_215953](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/assets/39347109/faef6af0-7e4f-4646-9896-c4a623ef8292)


#### Ticket Link
<!--
If this pull request addresses a Github issue, please link the relevant issue, e.g.

  Fixes https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/XXXXX

Otherwise, link the JIRA ticket.
-->
No ticket
title: fix: broken Dockerfile fix

triage.summary: Update Dockerfile Go image version from 1.18 to 1.21.8 to match go.mod toolchain requirement introduced in commit c8e729b6
triage.test_hint: Run docker build and verify it completes successfully without toolchain keyword errors

git clone and docker-compose up

docker image builds

## Screenshots (optional)

```
Step 10/23 : RUN EXCLUDE_PLUGIN=true EXCLUDE_SERVER=true EXCLUDE_ENTERPRISE=true make server-docker os=${TARGETOS} arch=${TARGETARCH}
 ---> Running in 000df3241198
mkdir -p bin/docker
cd server; env GOOS= GOARCH= go build -ldflags '-X "github.com/mattermost/focalboard/server/model.BuildNumber=dev" -X "github.com/mattermost/focalboard/server/model.BuildDate=n/a" -X "github.com/mattermost/focalboard/server/model.BuildHash=de5e5cc4141f14f69bf0e5666383997b81f851d6" -X "github.com/mattermost/focalboard/server/model.Edition=linux"' -tags 'json1 sqlite3' -o ../bin/docker/focalboard-server ./main
```

title: Bug: docker-compose fails - /go/src/focalboard/server/go.mod:5: unknown directive: toolchain

triage.summary: Docker build fails because the Go version pinned in the Dockerfile does not support the 'toolchain' directive introduced in go.mod (requires Go 1.21+). Updating the base image to a newer Go version fixes the issue.
triage.test_hint: Run docker-compose build and verify the image builds successfully without go.mod parsing errors.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Normal installation of Ubuntu 24.04 LTS on a Dell computer with Intel. Normal use for one year.
2. Download version v7.10.6, the file "focalboard-linux.tar.gz", unzip it, and double-click on the executable.
3. Nothing happens, no error message or anything else. → Problem detected.  
4. Launch via command line with `./focalboard-app`.
5. Error message: `./focalboard-app: error while loading shared libraries: libwebkit2gtk-4.0.so.37: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory`.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Desktop
 - Version: V7.10.6
 - Browser and OS: Firefox 134.0.1 (64 bits) ; Ubuntu 24.04 LTS last update.

## Problem Resolution

I use this link : [https://www.chippiko.com/installing-libwebkit2gtk-4-0-ubuntu](https://www.chippiko.com/installing-libwebkit2gtk-4-0-ubuntu)

1. `sudo nano /etc/apt/sources.list`
2. `deb http://gb.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu jammy main`
3. `sudo apt update`
4. `sudo apt install libwebkit2gtk-4.0-37 `

## Expected Behavior

I would have liked an error message informing me that the library is not installed, possibly with commands to resolve it / a link to this issue, or even the automatic installation of the library.
title: Bug: Installation of the libwebkit2gtk library

triage.skip_reason: Requires UI/UX decision on error messaging and potentially a build system change to bundle or detect missing libs; no clear spec for automated fix
triage.summary: Focalboard desktop app silently fails on Ubuntu 24.04 LTS because libwebkit2gtk-4.0.so.37 is not available; the library was removed from Ubuntu 24.04 default repos. App should either display a meaningful error message, bundle the dependency, or update installation docs to note this requirement.
triage.test_hint: Launch focalboard-app binary on a clean Ubuntu 24.04 install without libwebkit2gtk-4.0 and verify a human-readable error or install prompt is shown instead of silent failure.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior - 1: Install focal board using git clone and running the docker command. - 2: setup the apache2 config - 3: open the board and look at network console and see that the ws/wss connection fails ![opera_bLSPZB3cvF](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/assets/2556269/178c31f5-dba9-46bd-897c-f087faa5f82e)
Apache2

```conf ServerName myURL.com Redirect permanent / https://myURL.com/ ServerName myURL.com ProxyPreserveHost On AllowEncodedSlashes NoDecode SSLEngine On ProxyPass /ws/ ws://127.0.0.1:8082/ ProxyPassReverse /ws/ ws://127.0.0.1:8082/ Require all granted ProxyPass http://127.0.0.1:8082/ ProxyPassReverse http://127.0.0.1:8082/ ProxyPassReverseCookieDomain 127.0.0.1 myURL.com SSLCertificateFile /etc/letsencrypt/live/myURL.com/cert.pem SSLCertificateKeyFile /etc/letsencrypt/live/myURL.com/privkey.pem SSLCertificateChainFile /etc/letsencrypt/live/myURL.com/chain.pem ProxyPass / http://localhost:8082/ nocanon ProxyPassReverse / http://localhost:8082/ ```

## Expected behavior That the focal board setup works with apache2 and is responsive and doesn't require a refresh for any change done. A best solution would be that a script for apache2 is provided. ## Edition and Platform - todays git patch and ubuntu 22.04
title: Bug: ws requests don't come through in apache2 reverse proxies.

triage.skip_reason: Requires environment-specific testing with Apache2 reverse proxy and WebSocket configuration; no clear code fix, likely a documentation/configuration issue
triage.summary: WebSocket connections fail when Focalboard is deployed behind an Apache2 reverse proxy. The WS/WSS upgrade is not properly proxied, causing real-time updates to break and requiring manual page refreshes. User needs correct Apache2 config example with proper RewriteEngine/WebSocket upgrade headers.
triage.test_hint: Verify WebSocket connectivity via browser network console after applying correct Apache2 config with RewriteEngine On, RewriteCond %{HTTP:Upgrade} websocket, and ProxyPass directives with correct upgrade/connection headers.

## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Open a Kanban view with multiple cards in multiple columns.
2. Hold Shift to select a card.
3. Hold Ctrl+Shift to select a different card in the same column.
4. Cards between the 2 selected cards are selected. But also cards in other columns.

## Expected behavior

The first card, the second card and any cards in between should be selected. No other cards should be selected, only cards in between.

## Screenshots (optional)

In the screenshot 2 cards are highlighted with a red border to show the cards that were clicked on. 2 cards in between are selected, as well as 3 cards in other columns.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11646822/225004829-dad48efc-6eaf-4b44-aa1d-c19b1da529da.png)

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Mattermost Boards (plugin)
 - Version: cloud-2023-01-16-1
 - Browser and OS: Mattermost desktop app on Windows 10
title: Bug: Ctrl+Shift to select multiple cards also selects unrelated cards

triage.summary: Ctrl+Shift multi-select on Kanban view incorrectly includes cards from other columns when selecting a range between two cards. The range selection logic likely uses a flat list of all cards across columns instead of filtering to only the cards within the same column.
triage.test_hint: Unit test: given a kanban board with multiple columns each containing multiple cards, shift-clicking card A then ctrl+shift-clicking card B in the same column should select only cards between A and B in that column, with no cards from other columns selected.

## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Create a new card. Add some properties to it and set their values. Add some comments in there as well. Do not set the card title.
2. Go back to kanban view and try deleting the card.
3. Notice the UI didn't show any delete confirmation for this card.
4. Create a new card and repeat step 1 but this time also set a card title.
5. Go back to kanban view and try deleting the card. Notice that this time you see a delete confirmation.

## Expected behavior

Card delete confirmation should show up if there is any data in the card.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Mattermost Boards (plugin)
 - Version: v7.8.0
 - Browser and OS: Firefox on MacOS

## Additional context

The log in webapp is probably if the card has no title and no content, don't show the delete confirmation dialogue. It does not take into consideration the properties and comments in the card.
title: Bug: No card delete confirmation status for some cards

triage.summary: Delete confirmation dialog is skipped for cards with no title/content but with properties or comments. The condition that determines whether to show the confirmation dialog only checks for title and content blocks, ignoring property values and comments.
triage.test_hint: Check the condition that gates the delete confirmation dialog (likely in CardDetail or KanbanCard component). It should also check for non-empty property values (card.fields.properties) and comments (contentOrder or comment blocks) before skipping the dialog. Add unit tests covering: card with no title but with properties, card with no title but with comments, card with neither title nor content nor properties nor comments (should skip dialog).

body:
## Summary

The current UI on the Boards share dialog confuses users and is inconsistent with the behavior of adding explicit members to the board. The team role behaves as an option to "allow team members to join the board", but the UI can make it seem like you are adding the entire team as explicit members of the board, which is not the case. However, when adding individual board members, you are actually adding them as explicit members of the board.

We should consider improving the team role section to make it clearer that it is an option to **open the board to the team**, but not adding the team to the board.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Users granting their entire team to the board
2. Users adding their entire team to the board

## Additional context/similar features

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/93531870/212103221-d73b54e2-50bd-4a55-b748-af49df07efc7.png)
title: Feature Idea: Share boards team access UI is misleading

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX decision requiring design input and stakeholder alignment on copy/layout changes
triage.summary: Share board dialog team access section is misleading — looks like it adds the whole team as explicit members, but actually just opens the board to team members. UI copy and layout need to be clarified to distinguish 'open to team' from 'add explicit members'.
triage.test_hint: Verify share dialog clearly differentiates between team-level access toggle and individual member addition; check that updated copy reduces ambiguity in both Mattermost-connected and standalone modes.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Open Boards
2. Click on the menu in the top left and select About Mattermost

## Expected behavior

The modal looks the same as it does in the Channels/Playbooks, and the text inside it is selectable.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Go to the root path of the web server, i.e. localhost:8000
2. See a blank page and an error.

## Expected behavior

I see the welcome screen.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Server
 - Version: 7.4.3
 - Browser and OS: Firefox 68, Ubuntu 20.04

## Symptom

Loading any board causes a runtime exception as follows:

```
TypeError: Intl.Locale is not a constructor
```

## Root cause

This is a vanilla web compatibility bug with older versions of Firefox due to missing API implementations. (I am operating Focalboard under a proprietary, restricted environment where a browser upgrade is not feasible.)

## Proposal

There exists a polyfill for this API: https://formatjs.io/docs/polyfills/intl-locale/

I fixed this issue locally with the following patch:

```diff
diff --git a/webapp/package.json b/webapp/package.json
index 25843393..832b49d7 100644
--- a/webapp/package.json
+++ b/webapp/package.json
@@ -27,6 +27,7 @@
                "@draft-js-plugins/editor": "^4.1.2",
                "@draft-js-plugins/emoji": "^4.6.0",
                "@draft-js-plugins/mention": "^5.1.2",
+               "@formatjs/intl-locale": "^3.0.7",
                "@fullcalendar/core": "^5.10.1",
                "@fullcalendar/daygrid": "^5.10.1",
                "@fullcalendar/interaction": "^5.10.1",
diff --git a/webapp/src/main.tsx b/webapp/src/main.tsx
index dd9a3168..e4a9ee32 100644
--- a/webapp/src/main.tsx
+++ b/webapp/src/main.tsx
@@ -1,5 +1,6 @@
 // Copyright (c) 2015-present Mattermost, Inc. All Rights Reserved.
 // See LICENSE.txt for license information.
+import '@formatjs/intl-locale/polyfill'
 import React from 'react'
 import ReactDOM from 'react-dom'
 import {Provider as ReduxProvider} from 'react-redux'
```

I'd like to propose something like this: it offers improved webcompat with minimal additional code, and with minor bundle size cost.
title: Bug: Invalid instantiation of Intl.Locale from formatjs dependency in Firefox

triage.summary: Firefox <74 crashes with 'Intl.Locale is not a constructor' due to missing API; fix is to add @formatjs/intl-locale polyfill and import it in main.tsx entry point.
triage.test_hint: Verify app loads without blank page in Firefox 68; confirm Intl.Locale polyfill is applied before any formatjs i18n code runs; check bundle size delta is acceptable.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behaviour

1. Create or go to a board in table view
2. Ensure that the view has an editable text column
3. Attempt to drag to select text from within that column

## Expected behaviour

The text is selected. Instead, the whole row is picked up and dragged, and if you drop it, it often gets moved even if you drop it in the same place you started.

At a higher level, I'd expect clicking and dragging the mouse to only trigger text selection as long as the cursor is within an editable textbox. I'd only expect the whole row to get dragged when first clicking outside of an editable field.

## Edition and Platform

- Boards plugin on community in MM Desktop App on Mac
- Focalboard 0.15.2 (33) standalone on Mac

## Additional context

This might be related to https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/1620, but I'm not sure if those components handle dragging/selection differently
title: Bug: Unable to drag to select text in table view

triage.test_hint: Click into a text cell in table view, then drag to select a portion of the text; verify the row does not move and the text is selected. Also verify row drag still works when drag starts outside editable fields.
