title: Bug: Card content menu causes card view to spazz out

triage.summary: Card content block context menu ('...') causes visual instability ('spazzing') when the menu extends beyond the viewport and the user scrolls. Menu positioning logic does not account for available viewport space, causing the menu to render off-screen and trigger scroll-induced layout thrashing.
triage.test_hint: Render a card with a content block near the bottom of the viewport, open the block context menu, verify the menu is fully visible within the viewport without requiring scroll, and confirm no layout jitter occurs when moving the mouse off the menu.

title: Bug: Personal server > Log errors after dropping db

triage.summary: After dropping the database and restarting the service, the server logs repeated 401 errors at debug level because stale client sessions attempt requests with tokens that no longer exist in the recreated DB. The error 'sql: no rows in result set' is surfaced verbosely instead of being handled gracefully (e.g. treated as an invalid/expired session and prompting re-login without log noise).
triage.test_hint: Write a unit test for the session store's GetSession that verifies sql.ErrNoRows is mapped to a clean 'session not found' error without propagating the raw SQL error; also add an integration test that drops and recreates the DB schema and confirms the server returns 401 without excessive error logging.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Go to Mattermost Board
2. Create Multiple Tasks with a variation of Priorities including at least 1, 2, 9 and 10
3. Set the board to sort by priority
4. See Error: Sorted Order = 1, 10, 2, 9...

## Expected behavior

Expected Sort Order = 1, 2, 9, 10
Expected logic to place 10 after 9, not between 1 and 2. Suspect that 20 would also be incorrectly placed between 2 and 3 rather than after 19.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Mattermost Boards (plugin)
 - Version: 5.0.4
 - Browser and OS: Windows

## Additional context (optional)

Add any other context about the problem here, and any notes about the severity:

* Sev 3: Affects minor, non-critical functionality
title: Bug: MM Boards Priority Sequencing > 9 Error

triage.summary: Priority field sorting uses lexicographic (string) comparison instead of numeric comparison, causing values like 10 to sort between 1 and 2 rather than after 9.
triage.test_hint: Add unit tests for the sort comparator on numeric/priority fields verifying that [1,2,9,10,20] sorts in ascending numeric order.

body: ## Steps to reproduce the behavior 1. Open the Mattermost Desktop App (tested on v5.0.4) 2. Note that user status on the Channels tab shows you are logged in 3. Select the Boards tab 4. User status shows you are not logged in / offline. Note: This also repros in Chrome. The user state on Boards always shows "offline". ## Expected behavior User status in the Boards tab should always match the one in the Channels tab ## Screenshots Channels tab: Boards tab: ## Edition and Platform - Edition: Mattermost plugin - Version: v0.15.0 - Browser and OS: Chrome on Mac and Mattermost Desktop 5.0.4 ## Additional context If you change your status in Channels, the status is updated correctly in the Boards tab. However, if you refresh the Boards tab, it shows "offline" again.
title: Bug: User online status shows "offline" in  Boards until updated

triage.summary: User online status always shows 'offline' in Boards on initial load/refresh; only updates correctly after a status change event is received from Channels. Root cause is likely that Boards does not fetch/sync the current user status on mount and relies solely on real-time status-change events.
triage.test_hint: On mount of the Boards plugin, verify that the current user's status is fetched from the Mattermost store or API and reflected correctly without requiring a status-change event trigger.

The first header (and following text) is indented ## Expected behavior Headers and text should not be indented, even if immediately following a list ## Screenshots ## Edition and Platform - Edition: Personal Desktop / Personal Server / Mattermost plugin - Version: v0.14.0 - Browser and OS: Chrome on Mac ## Additional context The indentation appears correct when editing, but not after saving.
title: Bug: Markdown renders headers indented if immediately following a list

## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Clone the repo at tag `v0.12.1`
2. Make the following changes
<details>
<summary>Changes</summary>

```diff
diff --git a/Makefile b/Makefile
index 0db63694..a5e661cf 100644
--- a/Makefile
+++ b/Makefile
@@ -40,6 +40,11 @@ server-linux: ## Build server for Linux.
        $(eval LDFLAGS += -X "github.com/mattermost/focalboard/server/model.Edition=linux")
        cd server; env GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64 go build -ldflags '$(LDFLAGS)' -o ../bin/linux/focalboard-server ./main

+server-linux-rpi: ## Build server for Linux.
+       mkdir -p bin/linux
+       $(eval LDFLAGS += -X "github.com/mattermost/focalboard/server/model.Edition=linux")
+       cd server; go build -ldflags '$(LDFLAGS)' -o ../bin/linux/focalboard-server ./main
+
```

index c6b177ab..2d4c3510 100644
--- a/docker/Dockerfile
+++ b/docker/Dockerfile
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
 ### Webapp build

+FROM node:16.3 as nodebuild

 WORKDIR /webapp
 ADD webapp/ /webapp

 ADD . /go/src/focalboard

-RUN  make server-linux

title: Bug: Build failing on armv7l (Raspberry Pi)

triage.skip_reason: Build/environment issue with unclear full error log (truncated), requires testing on actual armv7l/Raspberry Pi hardware or emulation; UI/platform decisions involved in Makefile targets
triage.summary: Build fails on armv7l (Raspberry Pi / arm32) due to cypress npm dependency incompatibility and missing ARM-targeted Makefile build target. Workaround involves adding a server-linux-rpi Makefile target and updating Dockerfile to skip optional npm deps, but the cypress dep causes npm install to fail even with --no-optional.
triage.test_hint: Verify `npm install --no-optional` skips cypress on armv7l; confirm `make server-linux-rpi` cross-compiles Go binary without GOARCH=amd64 constraint; build Docker image on or targeting armv7l.

## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Open a card
2. Create a date property and edit it
3. Select a date
4. Enable "Date range" and select an end date
5. Click on the "today" button - it sets start date to today
7. Click "today" again - it sets the end date to today
8. Click "today" again - it clears the date

Clicking "today" the third time should not clear the date.

title: Bug: using domain for mysql ,the docker not started

triage.summary: MySQL DSN parsing fails when using a hostname (domain) instead of IP address; the connection string format 'user:pass@host:port/db' is not being correctly converted to the Go MySQL driver format 'user:pass@tcp(host:port)/db', causing the driver to interpret the host as a network protocol name.
triage.test_hint: Add a unit test for the DSN normalization/parsing function that verifies domain-based hostnames like 'boards:boards@abc.dba.com:3306/boards' are correctly transformed to 'boards:boards@tcp(abc.dba.com:3306)/boards' before being passed to the MySQL driver.

body:
## Summary

The point is not to lose focus on text fields when you press `Caps Lock`. I and probably many more who use several languages configure switching the keyboard layout to the caps lock key for speed and convenience instead of the common `Alt` + `Shift`.

### Expected Behavior:

I print text on a line, switch to another layout by pressing `Caps Lock`, and continue typing text.

### Current behavior:

I am typing text, there is a need to change the layout, I hit on the `Caps Lock` and the cursor loses focus. This results in the text being saved to the database (with a small lag). You have to click on the text field again to continue typing!

Due to the fact that you have to switch the layout very often, this causes very significant inconvenience in work.

The idea of ​​not losing focus when pressing the `Caps Lock` will also be useful in cases where the key is used for its intended purpose — to write characters in uppercase.
title: Enhancement: Don't lose focus on text areas when press Caps Lock

triage.summary: Pressing Caps Lock causes text fields/areas to lose focus, interrupting typing and triggering premature saves. Need to intercept or ignore the Caps Lock keydown event in blur/focus handlers so it does not defocus active text inputs.
triage.test_hint: Add a unit test for the onKeyDown/onBlur handler to assert that a CapsLock keydown event does not trigger blur on text area components; also manually test in a multilingual setup where CapsLock is bound to layout switching.

triage.test_hint: Write a unit test for the date sort comparator ensuring ascending and descending order produces correct results with various date values including null/empty dates.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Deploy on personal server. 
2. Open a page on mobile.
3. Can see an enormous margin on the left, even more on edit mode, almost half of the screen.

## Expected behavior

Thinner margin on the left. The content can fill up the whole screen. 

## Screenshots

![Screenshot](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/66767303/135770766-9aa6d8ae-9af3-4190-83a2-62836ed9a015.png)


## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Server
 - Version: v0.9.1 rc2
 - Browser and OS: Chrome on Android

## Additional context

I think maybe css would do the job? I also hope the whole block won't "jump right" when hover. Just let the `...` show without the whole block moving.
title: Please remove the left margin in mobile view

triage.summary: Excessive left margin in mobile view makes content area too narrow; block also jumps right on hover. Likely a CSS fix needed for mobile breakpoints.
triage.test_hint: Verify layout on a narrow viewport (≤480px) in both view and edit mode; ensure block does not shift horizontally on hover and content fills available width.

body: **Steps to reproduce the behavior:** 1. Open a table 2. Group by a property (e.g. status) 3. Click on the ... menu for a group (e.g. P1), and select Hide 4. Now look for a way to unhide that group, there doesn't appear to be a way 5. Ungrouping, then regrouping doesn't reset the hidden groups either **Expected behavior:** An obvious way to unhide hidden groups. **Screenshots:** **Edition and Platform:** - Edition: Mattermost plugin - Version: v0.9.0 - Browser and OS: Safari on macOS **Additional context:** Suggestions: Add a "Unhide all groups" option to the menu, but what happens if all groups are hidden?
title: Bug: No way to unhide hidden table groups

triage.summary: No UI mechanism to unhide hidden table groups; ungrouping and regrouping does not reset hidden state either.
triage.test_hint: Hide a group in table view, then verify an 'Unhide' or 'Show all groups' option appears and successfully restores the hidden group.

body:
**Steps to reproduce the behavior:**
1. Go to a card.
2. Create a number type field.
3. Introduce text that is not a valid number.
4. Create a email type field.
5. Introduct text that is not a valid email.

**Expected behavior:**
It should validate that fields and, at least, complain about the fields values. But it is allowing me to use any value as part of the field.

**Edition and Platform:**
 - Edition: Mattermost plugin
 - Version: v0.9.0
 - Browser and OS: Firefox
title: Bug: Number/Email fields are allowing any value.

triage.summary: Number and Email custom fields accept any arbitrary text without validation, allowing invalid values to be saved silently.
triage.test_hint: Enter non-numeric text in a Number field and non-email text in an Email field; expect inline validation error or prevention of saving invalid values.

body:
**Steps to reproduce the behavior:**
1. On a board and a gallery view, create a card with some text at first and then a Markdown image tag, with the format `![](IMAGE_URL)`
2. From the gallery view, drag and drop it to change its order with other cards, starting the drag action from the text of the card. The order change should happen successfully
3. Do exactly the same thing, but starting the drag action clicking on the image. After dropping the card, the URL changes to the linked image one, out of Boards

**Expected behavior:**
The drag&drop action should work as described in 2.

**Screenshots:**
This is an example of a card with text and an image that was used to reproduce the problem:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/223323/134032792-b15f5bf7-d7e7-4dba-9cfa-0858901383d3.png)

**Edition and Platform:**
 - Edition: Mattermost plugin
 - Version: v0.9.1 RC1
 - Browser and OS: Firefox 92 on Linux
title: Bug: drag&drop fails if you drag from a Markdown image

triage.summary: Dragging a gallery card starting from a Markdown-rendered image causes the browser to navigate to the image URL instead of performing the card drag-and-drop reorder. The fix requires preventing the default drag behavior on img elements inside card previews, e.g. by adding a dragStart handler that calls preventDefault() or sets draggable=false on img tags rendered within the gallery card content.
triage.test_hint: Add a test that mounts a gallery card containing a rendered markdown image, simulates a dragstart event on the img element, and asserts that navigation does not occur and the card drag handler is invoked instead.

body:
**Steps to reproduce the behavior:** 1. Create a new board 2. Add a description 3. Within the description, insert a large image using a Markdown expression like `![astrofish](https://media.snipettemag.com/seance-uploads/2021/8/ocean-genes/1vobi2tLbk2oQknaeBF3HGQ.jpeg)` 4. The image now spills out of the screen because its dimensions are larger than the screen width. Additionally, one can't scroll down to see the cards below, because not enough space is reserved for scrolling down that far. **Expected behavior:** The maximum width for images should be restricted, as happens for images *within* a Markdown card. Also, enough space should be reserved on the bottom so one can scroll down to the bottom of the page. (Right now, the page height seems to be getting calculated without taking the image height into account).
Screenshots (click to expand) Board containing oversized image: ![Board containing oversized image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7288084/133374789-eb00c6cc-8879-440b-964b-04846d9e0d1c.png) Scroll restriction: I am not able to scroll the page beyond this point to see the table and cards below: ![Board scrolled down as low as possible, but cards still not visible](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7288084/133374850-4a9df373-f5bc-4cf8-bd34-5b2b771a94b8.png) :point_up: can't scroll more beyond this :( For comparison, here is the board without the card: ![Working board without card](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7288084/133374941-dcd49dc6-79b7-46bc-a2f6-bdced63546d0.png) Inline images work fine when inside a card (as opposed to in the main board description): ![Image embedded in card and working fine (view from beginning)](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7288084/133375023-5c601b5d-9124-4c1a-8feb-0b510a25f3ee.png) ![Image embedded in card and working fine (view after scrolling down)](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7288084/133375031-3a912d12-b86a-4709-9f25-ca6ca4a59178.png)
**Platform:** - Browser and version: Firefox 91.0.2 - OS and version: Manjaro 21.1.2 "Pahvo" - Focalboard version: Mattermost plugin, v0.8.2 **Additional context:** This seems to be something that can be solved by adjusting the image class. For instance, simply adding the CSS rule ```css img { max-width: 100%; } ``` got the image back down to a reasonable size, although I still wasn't able to scroll down enough to reach the bottom of the page. The images within a card uses a rule for `.octo-block img`, which I'm guessing images in the board description are missing because they aren't part of an `octo-block`. Expanding the full rule to include board description cards makes things even better, but I am *still* not able to scroll down, so that's the main issue to be investigated. PS: If someone were to give me pointers, I'd be happy to have a go at debugging this myself :slightly_smiling_face:
title: Bug: Inline images in board description overflow from screen

triage.summary: Inline images in board description overflow the screen width and break scroll height calculation. Images inside markdown cards are constrained correctly but the board description markdown renderer lacks max-width CSS constraint on images.
triage.test_hint: Add CSS rule (e.g. `max-width: 100%`) to images rendered inside the board description markdown component, and verify that the page scroll height correctly accounts for the rendered image height. Check that images in card content are unaffected.

body:
**Summary:**
Current theme styling isn't applied to the _About Mattermost_ modal when opened from Boards. 
I'm on a dark theme and the _About_ modal is white with gray text and light blue links which are very hard to read.

**Steps to reproduce the behavior:**
1. Set a dark theme in Mattermost Channels
2. Open Boards
3. Open the app switcher from the global header
4. Click "About Mattermost"
5. Observe the modal styling

**Expected behavior:**
Modal should match the theme styling

**Screenshots:**
![about_modal](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15314183/132700655-d513a28c-8509-4e49-8a7d-dc7c53577516.jpg)


**Platform:**
 - plugin v0.9.0 on community
title: Bug: Theme styling not applied to "About Mattermost" modal

triage.summary: The 'About Mattermost' modal opened from Boards does not inherit the current Mattermost theme, resulting in poor readability on dark themes (white background, gray text, light blue links).
triage.test_hint: Set a dark theme in Mattermost Channels, open Boards, open the About Mattermost modal via the app switcher, and verify the modal background, text, and link colors match the active theme.

body:
**Summary:**
If there is an existing board and a new one is added from the menu in the side bar then the current view is not properly updated and also highlighted active view in the side bar is the wrong one.

**Steps to reproduce the behavior:**
1. Start the server with no boards
2. Click on "+ Add Board" in the side panel and select "Project Tasks"
3. There will be an active table view "All Tasks"
4. Click on "+ Add Board" in the side panel and select "Empty board"
5. Table view "All Tasks" is still highlighted as active in the side bar
6. Center panel contains _table view_ with "Untitled board"

**Expected behavior:**
"Board view" should be highlighted for the new board in the side bar. Also the view in the central panel should be switch to board view instead of table one.

**Screenshots:**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5587620/130913212-5c90a252-9bbd-43c4-870c-3a8b52daecd7.png)

**Platform:**
 - Browser and version: Chrome 92
 - OS and version: Windows 10

**Additional context:**
Reproducible on `v0.9.0-rc7`

@chenilim should this be fixed in `0.9`?
title: Bug: after adding new board the view is not properly updated

triage.summary: After adding a new board from the sidebar, the active view highlight and center panel view are not updated — the previous board's active view remains selected instead of switching to the new board's default view.
triage.test_hint: After creating a second board, verify that the sidebar highlights the new board's default view and the center panel renders that view (not the previous board's view).

body:
**Summary:**
This test seems to produce a different result during `npm run updatesnapshot` and `npm run test` - https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/blob/main/webapp/src/components/cardDetail/cardDetailContents.test.tsx#L99

**Steps to reproduce the behaviour:**
1. Run `npm run updatesnapshot`. Notice that it will have updated snapshot for this test.
2. Run `npm run test`. Notice that the test will fail. Reset the updated snapshots now and the test will pass.

**Expected behavior:**
The test should produce same result
title: Bug: Posiibly flaky unit test

triage.summary: Flaky unit test in cardDetailContents.test.tsx produces different snapshots when running updatesnapshot vs test commands, causing intermittent test failures.
triage.test_hint: Run npm run updatesnapshot followed by npm run test and verify the snapshot matches; investigate non-deterministic rendering (e.g., ordering, async state, date/time usage) in cardDetailContents.test.tsx around line 99.

body: **Summary:** Undo and redo menu items are disabled in macOS standalone app. **Steps to reproduce the behavior:** 1. Delete something 1. Click Edit menu 1. Press cmd+z **Expected behavior:** The undo function works, but the menu item is disabled. **Screenshots:** **Platform:** - Browser and version: Focalboard 0.8.0 (21) - OS and version: macOS 11.4
title: Bug: Undo / redo menu items are disabled (macOS, standalone)

triage.summary: On macOS standalone (Electron) app, the Edit menu Undo/Redo items are always disabled even though Cmd+Z/Cmd+Shift+Z keyboard shortcuts work correctly. The menu items need to be wired up to the Electron menu's undo/redo roles or the webContents undo/redo actions.
triage.test_hint: Open macOS standalone app, delete a card or make an edit, open Edit menu and verify Undo item is enabled and triggers undo when clicked.

body:
**Summary:**
When clicking and dragging to select the text area in Meeting notes' table view, it moves the entire text box.

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/4223530/129629324-29c164af-09dc-4ddd-90c7-30c7aebf644c.mov

**Steps to reproduce the behavior:**
In the Meeting Notes board, select table view. If there's a line with default text, click and drag your cursor as if you want to highlight the text.

**Expected behavior:**
I would expect the text box to be static so I could drag and select all the text I want to replace rather than clicking and having to backspace. 

Alternatively, clicking in the textbox should make the placeholder text disappear. 

**Screenshots:**
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

**Platform:**
 - Browser and version: Firefox 91
 - OS and version: macOS 11.5
title: Bug: Selecting text box moves the whole line

triage.summary: In table view, clicking and dragging inside a text input cell triggers a drag-move of the entire row/cell instead of selecting the text. The mousedown event on the text input is likely propagating to the row's drag handler, preventing normal text selection.
triage.test_hint: In Meeting Notes board table view, click and drag within a text cell to select text — verify no row movement occurs and text gets highlighted normally.

body:
**Summary:**
If you have several different teams using different themes, this isn't reflected in focalboard which will only use one theme regardless of the team it's being accessed from.
This is only a problem when accessing from the channel header icon, accessing from the app switcher applies the theme for every team.

**Steps to reproduce the behavior:**
1. Install focalboard on mattermost
2. Open a team with a dark theme
3. Open focalboard from that team - focalboard has the dark theme applied
4. Switch to a team with the default light theme and open focalboard from that team using the channel header icon - focalboard again has the dark theme applied

**Expected behavior:**
Focalboard should use the default light theme (blue/white)
title: Bug: Focalboard doesn't apply different themes per team when opened from the channel header

triage.skip_reason: Requires understanding of how theme context is passed from channel header vs app switcher; UI/theming decision and no clear spec on fix path
triage.summary: Focalboard does not update its theme when switching teams and opening via the channel header icon; it retains the previously applied team theme instead of adopting the current team's theme.
triage.test_hint: Switch between two teams with different themes and verify the channel header icon path triggers a theme refresh matching the active team's theme.

body:
**Summary:**
Bug: Markdown renderer removes extra line breaks for Card Description

**Steps to reproduce the behavior:**
1. Create a card
2. Add two lines of text in the description
3. Give 10 empty lines breaks in between them
4. Click outside
5. The spacing is removed

**Expected behavior:**
The spacing should not be removed.

**Screenshots:**
Attached a video.
https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11034289/128041967-8a75785a-381b-4970-9dd2-c51d24b16b22.mov

**Platform:**
 - Chrome Version 92.0.4515.107 (Official Build) (x86_64)
 - Mac Os 11.4 (20F71)
title: Bug: Markdown renderer removes extra line breaks for Card Description

triage.summary: Markdown renderer collapses multiple consecutive blank lines in card description, discarding user-intentional vertical spacing when the editor loses focus.
triage.test_hint: Add text with 10 blank lines between two paragraphs in a card description, click outside, reopen card and verify spacing is preserved in both edit and preview modes.

body: **Steps to reproduce the behavior:** 1. Click Settings and set the language to Simplified Chinese 2. The UI shows in English **Expected behavior:** The UI should show in Simplified Chinese. **Screenshots:** **Platform:** - Focalboard v0.8.0 macOS App
title: Bug: Simplified Chinese language setting displays UI in English

triage.summary: Simplified Chinese locale is selected in settings but the UI continues to render in English, suggesting a missing or misconfigured zh-CN locale file or an incorrect locale key mapping.
triage.test_hint: Verify zh-CN locale file exists and is properly imported/registered in the i18n configuration; confirm the language key used in settings matches the locale file key (e.g. 'zh-CN' vs 'zh_CN').

body:
**Summary:**
 Two webapp tests seem to be flaky-

```
1) Create and delete board / card
       Can create a table view:
     AssertionError: Timed out retrying after 4000ms: Expected to find element: `.ViewHeader .Editable[title='Table view']`, but never found it.
      at Context.eval (http://localhost:8088/__cypress/tests?p=cypress/integration/createBoard.js:163:59)

  2) Create and delete board / card
       Can rename the table view:
     AssertionError: Timed out retrying after 4000ms: Expected to find element: `.ViewHeader .Editable[title='Table view']`, but never found it.
      at Context.eval (http://localhost:8088/__cypress/tests?p=cypress/integration/createBoard.js:170:8)
```

More details of the failed CI run can be found here -https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/runs/2952731939?check_suite_focus=true#step:12:139

We need to verify the behaviour and fix the tests.
title: Investigate possible flaky webapp tests

triage.skip_reason: Flaky test investigation requires running the test suite in CI environment and observing timing/race conditions; no clear repro steps or deterministic fix available without deeper analysis
triage.summary: Two Cypress e2e tests ('Can create a table view' and 'Can rename the table view') are intermittently failing due to a timeout waiting for `.ViewHeader .Editable[title='Table view']`, suggesting a race condition or missing wait/assertion before interacting with the view header after creating a table view.
triage.test_hint: Add explicit cy.intercept() waits for relevant API calls (e.g., board/view creation) before asserting on the ViewHeader element, or increase the timeout with a proper retry strategy. Check if the table view tab is rendered asynchronously and ensure the test waits for the correct network response before proceeding.

body:
**Summary:**
randomIcons user setting not stored between launches of the Windows app

**Steps to reproduce the behavior:**
1. Launch Windows app
2. Random icons is on by default
3. Turn it off
4. Quit and relaunch the app
5. Random icons is still on

**Expected behavior:**
randomIcons choice should be persistent

**Platform:**

Windows 10, 10.0.19041 
Focalboard v0.7.0 (focalboard-win.zip)

**Additional context:**
Similar to #375
title: Bug: randomIcons choice not persistent on Windows

triage.summary: randomIcons user setting is not persisted between app launches on Windows; the setting resets to default (on) after restarting the app.
triage.test_hint: Toggle randomIcons off, restart the Windows desktop app, and verify the setting remains off. Check where the setting is read/written — likely needs to use electron-store or similar persistent storage instead of in-memory state.

It is hard to change the name of a group. You drag it more then selecting the text as i would do it.

body: **Steps to reproduce the behavior:** 1. Open a table with many rows 2. Scroll down 3. The row content appears above the header when scroll far enough **Expected behavior:** The board title and rest of header should remain visible, no matter the scroll position. **Screenshots:** https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/46905241/122299259-9ab53700-ceb2-11eb-8f8e-be9e41766b9e.mov **Platform:** - Chrome 91 on macOS 10.15.7 - Also on macOS App (today's build) **Additional context:** Also check the clipping when both horizontally and vertically scrolled. The header should remain visible throughout.
title: Bug: Table view vertical scroll does not clip the top

triage.summary: Table view rows overflow above the sticky header when scrolling vertically; header/title area lacks proper overflow clipping (overflow:hidden or sticky positioning missing on container).
triage.test_hint: Open a table with 30+ rows, scroll down rapidly, and verify row content does not appear above the column header row or board title bar. Also verify combined horizontal+vertical scroll keeps header pinned.

title: Bug: Docker build fails on Debian Buster as of `1cc2337`

triage.summary: Docker build fails on Debian Buster since commit 1cc2337; the Dockerfile or build script introduced a breaking change that prevents successful image construction.
triage.test_hint: Run `docker build -t foobar .` from the `docker/` directory on a clean clone at the affected commit and confirm the build succeeds after the fix.

body:
Submitted on behalf of contributor `jprusch`

**Summary:**
Board > not able to filter on cards that have no status (or "empty" status)

**Steps to reproduce the behavior:**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/13020833/120788519-b7578380-c530-11eb-86e1-14249f6f05e3.png)


**Expected behavior:**
Be able to filter and show only cards that have no status set or status = "empty"

**Platform:**
 - OS and version: Windows 10  20H2 Build 19042.985
title: Bug: Board > not able to filter only cards that have no status (or "empty" status)

triage.summary: Filter UI does not support filtering cards with empty/unset property values (e.g. no status set). Need to add an 'Empty' or 'None' option to the filter value selector so users can find cards missing a given property value.
triage.test_hint: Add a card with no status set, create a filter for status = empty/none, verify only cards without a status value are shown.

body:
**Summary:**
The title says all

**Steps to reproduce the behavior:**
1. Create a Docker container based on the 0.6.7 minimum, and pass your DB settings through environment variable "-e 'focalboard_dbconfig=....'"

**Expected behavior:**
Focalboard should address the specified DB and not the default SQLite DB

**Screenshots:**
not applicable.

**Platform:**
 - Browser and version: [e.g. Chrome 88, Safari 12] : not relevant
 - OS and version: [e.g. Windows, macOS 10.15.7, iOS 13.1] : not relevant

**Additional context:**
This discussion explains the problem with a bit more details:
https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/discussions/491
title: Bug: The docker environment variable to set the DB parameters is not taken into account

triage.summary: Docker environment variable 'focalboard_dbconfig' is not being read/applied at startup, causing the app to fall back to the default SQLite configuration instead of the user-specified database settings.
triage.test_hint: Start a Docker container with '-e focalboard_dbconfig=<postgres_dsn>' and verify the app connects to the specified database rather than SQLite. Check config loading logic to ensure environment variable overrides are applied before DB initialization.

body: **Summary:** When we write left angle bracket (`<`) within [backticks](https://www.markdownguide.org/basic-syntax/#code), it shows `<`. The expected output is to show the angle bracket as is. **Steps to reproduce the behavior:** 1. Create a card 2. Add "text" contents and write angle brackets within backticks: `` `m < n` `` 3. See the resulting text: `m < n`. **Expected behavior:** The resulting text must be `m < n` **Screenshots:** Attached two screenshots: before and after typing `m < n`. **Platform:** - I can see this problem from Linux, Windows, and MacOS. - I am using self-hosted focalboard v0.6.7 on Ubuntu 20.
title: Bug: markdown code is converting angle brackets wrong

triage.summary: Markdown inline code renderer incorrectly HTML-encodes angle brackets (< becomes <) instead of displaying raw characters.
triage.test_hint: Render a card text block containing backtick-wrapped content with angle brackets (e.g. `m < n`) and assert the displayed text is 'm < n', not 'm < n'.

body:
**Summary:**
There is a problem to run focalboard in the kubernetes. Issue to parse the port. Like some kubernetes variable cause the issue.

**Steps to reproduce the behavior:**
Run in any kubernetes cluster:
kubectl run --rm -i --tty focalboard --image=mattermost/focalboard --restart=Never


**Expected behavior:**
focalboard will start as it can pass the port from the config file and should by immune for any other k8s env variables.
It should simply start as it does by running "docker run -it --rm mattermost/focalboard"

**ERROR LOG:**
2021/04/30 07:16:14 Focalboard Server 
2021/04/30 07:16:14 Version: 0.6.6 
2021/04/30 07:16:14 Edition: linux 
2021/04/30 07:16:14 Build Number: dev 
2021/04/30 07:16:14 Build Date: Thu Apr 29 19:28:50 UTC 2021 
2021/04/30 07:16:14 Build Hash: d09d6e0e4080b257e3d0a898cb1cab4d2640efb7 
2021/04/30 07:16:14 Unable to read the config file: 1 error(s) decoding: 
 
* cannot parse 'port' as int: strconv.ParseInt: parsing "tcp://10.43.228.108:8000": invalid syntax 

**Platform:**
 - OS and version: kubernetes cluster v1.20.4
title: Bug: k8s - cannot parse 'port' as int: strconv.ParseInt:

triage.summary: Kubernetes injects service environment variables (e.g. FOCALBOARD_PORT=tcp://10.43.228.108:8000) that conflict with the config decoder expecting 'port' to be an integer. The config loading needs to either ignore/not bind these env vars or sanitize them before parsing.
triage.test_hint: Set env var PORT or FOCALBOARD_PORT to a URL-style string like 'tcp://10.0.0.1:8000' and verify the server starts correctly using the config file value instead of crashing.

6. Delete all text in label, unfocus and refocus label area

**Expected behavior:**
Whether IME are enabled or not, and whatever text set in label, typed text will show up.

**Screenshots:**

Likely caused by incorrect handling of compositionstart/compositionend events or premature state resets during IME composition in the label input component.
triage.test_hint: Add a test that simulates IME composition events (compositionstart, compositionupdate, compositionend) on a status label input that already contains text and assert the composed text is appended correctly. Also verify the empty-field and paste paths still work.

body:
## Summary
Fixes #4843 — Empty cards are created when no changes are made.

## Problem
When a user clicks the **+ New** button to create a card and immediately closes the modal without making any changes, an empty card is left behind.

## Solution
Added cleanup logic in `CardDialog` that automatically deletes the card when the dialog is closed if the card is still empty (no title, no content blocks, no comments, and no attachments). Template cards are excluded.

## Changes
- **`webapp/src/components/cardDialog.tsx`**: Added `handleClose` callback that checks if the card is empty before closing, and deletes it if so.

## Testing
1. Click **+ New** to create a card → immediately close → ✅ empty card removed
2. Click **+ New** → add title/content → close → ✅ card preserved
title: Fix: Delete empty cards when dialog is closed without changes

triage.summary: Delete empty cards automatically when CardDialog is closed without any user input (no title, content, comments, or attachments). Template cards are excluded from cleanup.
triage.test_hint: Verify that: (1) opening +New and immediately closing leaves no card behind; (2) opening +New, adding a title, then closing preserves the card; (3) template cards are never deleted on close; (4) cards with only comments or attachments are also cleaned up correctly.

body:
Commit 1: Fix Docker build failure by updating Go version to 1.21

  Bug Description:
  Docker builds fail during the Go compilation stage with the following error:
  go: errors parsing go.mod:
  /go/src/focalboard/server/go.mod:5: unknown directive: toolchain
  make: *** [Makefile:66: server-docker] Error 1

  The build process exits with code 2, preventing the Docker image from being created.

  What it does:
  Updates the Go compiler version in both Dockerfiles from golang:1.18.3 to golang:1.21.

  Why it's needed:
  - The project's server/go.mod file specifies go 1.21 with toolchain go1.21.8 directive (line 5)
  - The toolchain directive was introduced in Go 1.21
  - Using Go 1.18.3 caused build failures because it doesn't recognize this directive
  - This fix aligns the Docker build environment with the project's Go requirements

  Files changed:
  - docker/Dockerfile (line 13)
  - Dockerfile.build (line 21)

  ---
  Commit 2: Update base image to debian:bookworm-slim for GLIBC compatibility

  Bug Description:
  After fixing the build error, the container builds successfully but crashes immediately at startup with:
  /opt/focalboard/bin/focalboard-server: /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6: version `GLIBC_2.32' not found (required by /opt/focalboard/bin/focalboard-server)
  /opt/focalboard/bin/focalboard-server: /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6: version `GLIBC_2.33' not found (required by /opt/focalboard/bin/focalboard-server)
  /opt/focalboard/bin/focalboard-server: /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6: version `GLIBC_2.34' not found (required by /opt/focalboard/bin/focalboard-server)

  The container exits with status code 1, and docker ps -a shows the container as Exited (1).

  What it does:
  Updates the final stage base image from debian:buster-slim to debian:bookworm-slim.

  Why it's needed:
  - Go 1.21 compiled binaries require GLIBC 2.32, 2.33, and 2.34
  - Debian Buster (oldoldstable) only provides GLIBC 2.28
  - Debian Bookworm (stable) provides GLIBC 2.36, which satisfies all requirements
  - Without this change, the container builds successfully but crashes immediately at startup

  Files changed:
  - docker/Dockerfile (line 25)

  ---
  Impact:
  These two commits work together to fix both the build-time and runtime errors:
  - ✅ Before: Build fails with "unknown directive: toolchain"
  - ✅ After Commit 1: Build succeeds but container crashes with GLIBC errors
  - ✅ After Commit 2: Build succeeds AND container runs successfully
  - ✅ Application starts and serves correctly at http://localhost
  - ✅ docker-compose deployment works as expected
title: Fix/update go version for toolchain support

triage.summary: Update Dockerfiles to use Go 1.21 base image and debian:bookworm-slim to fix build failures and GLIBC compatibility crashes caused by toolchain directive in go.mod requiring Go 1.21+.
triage.test_hint: Build Docker image using docker/Dockerfile and Dockerfile.build, verify container starts without GLIBC errors and focalboard-server runs successfully.

body:
#### Summary
This pull request introduces a fix to ensure that the ModifiedBy field is not overwritten if it already contains a userID. We added a conditional check to verify if the ModifiedBy field is empty before assigning a new userID, enhancing the data integrity of the block modification tracking.

By default the system uses default userID from person, who is importing data. If board.jsonl is already filled with existing IDs from current board or mattermost instance, we should accept it, not overwrite.

#### Ticket Link
https://community.mattermost.com/core/pl/7x9n4kfy4pbdxphsnuhtoodqxy
title: prevent ModifiedBy overwrite

triage.summary: During board import, ModifiedBy field is unconditionally overwritten with the importing user's ID even when the JSONL already contains valid user IDs, losing original authorship metadata.
triage.test_hint: Import a board.jsonl that has pre-populated ModifiedBy fields and verify they are preserved rather than replaced by the importing user's ID.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Run a Docker container using the latest image. (`docker run focalboard:latest`)
2. Register a user. Since the page allows arbitrary registration, HTTP basic auth is implemented.
3. Add a user to OpenResty.
4. Open the address, verify the HTTP basic auth, and return to the login page.
5. Enter the username and password, click the login button, and the message "login failed" is displayed.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: mattermost/focalboard:7.11.4 (docker IMAGE NAME)
 - Version: 7.11.4
 - Browser and OS: Chrome on Mac

## Additional context (optional)
1. Add the ability to turn off registration. I couldn't find it in the docs, so if it's already there, just ignore this.
2. Make sure HTTP basic auth doesn't mess with the login user.

**Best regards.**
title: Bug: HTTP basic auth causes user login failure, with the error message: login failed.

triage.skip_reason: No clear repro in CI environment; requires specific HTTP basic auth proxy setup (OpenResty/nginx) and Docker deployment; root cause likely in how Authorization header from basic auth conflicts with Focalboard's own auth token handling, needs investigation of middleware/header parsing
triage.summary: When HTTP basic auth is placed in front of Focalboard (e.g. via OpenResty/nginx proxy), the Authorization header from basic auth conflicts with Focalboard's own login flow, causing 'login failed' errors even with correct credentials.
triage.test_hint: Check server-side auth middleware to see if incoming Authorization headers from basic auth proxies are being parsed/consumed before Focalboard's own token-based auth logic; add integration test with a simulated proxy that injects Authorization: Basic headers and verify login still succeeds.

body:
[Copied from https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/issues/24148]

#### Summary
Changed date property not reflected in board card

#### Steps to reproduce
1. Create a board.
2. Open a new card for editing.
3. Add a custom date property to the card (and therefore to all cards on the board).
4. Set the value of the custom date property on the new card and close it.
5. Click "Properties" and enable the display of the new property on the board.
6. Open the card back up, change the value of the date property, and close the card.

#### Expected behavior
I expect to see the new value I just entered for the date property to be displayed on the card on the board display.

#### Observed behavior (that appears unintentional) 
The old value of the date field is still displayed on the card on the board display.

The two ways I've found to get the new value to display are:

* quit and restart the app
* disable and re-enable the display of the date property on the board then the displayed value is updated

### But wait there's more

Two other incorrect behaviors I'm observing with date properties which there's a good chance are related to the one described above so I'm adding them here instead of creating new issues:

1. As above, set up a board with a date property which is displayed on cards.
2. Create a new card, set the date property when creating it, and close it.
3. Observe that the value of the date property isn't displayed on the card.
4. If you disable and re-enable displaying the date property on the board, the value shows up.
5. Moving on, drag and drop the card to a different column.
6. Observe that the value of the date property is _erased_. It's not just not displayed on the board; if you open the card you will see that it is _gone_.

Regarding the last observation above, in previous versions I've seen a date property revert to a previous value when a card is moved to a new column, so that's also something to watch out for when fixing these issues.
title: Bug: Changed date property not reflected in board card in macOS app

triage.skip_reason: Multiple related but distinct bugs (UI not refreshing after date change, date erased on drag-drop); requires reproduction and likely involves state management in both frontend and desktop layers; no clear minimal repro isolation
triage.summary: Date property changes not reflected on board cards in macOS desktop app; date value erased when dragging card to new column. UI appears to have stale state issues with date properties, and drag-and-drop causes data loss of date field values.
triage.test_hint: 1) Change a date property on a card and verify board card reflects new value without restart. 2) Drag a card with a date property to another column and verify the date value is preserved both in the UI and in storage.

title: FocalBoard login problem

triage.skip_reason: Requires testing nginx subpath routing and frontend base URL handling; no clear minimal repro beyond user config; likely involves both frontend asset path resolution and API call base URL construction
triage.summary: When Focalboard is deployed under a subpath (e.g. /focalboard), login and registration fail because the frontend makes API calls to /api/v2/login and /api/v2/register without the subpath prefix, causing nginx to serve 404s instead of proxying to the backend.
triage.test_hint: Deploy Focalboard behind a reverse proxy with a subpath prefix; verify that API calls from login/register forms include the subpath in their URLs and that static assets also resolve correctly under the subpath.

body:
Hi,

I have been using Focalboard for a year now. I usually login in every few months to update some things. Watchtower is automatically updating the Docker container of Focalboard. However, today I encountered an unexpected issue - I am unable to log in anymore. 

To resolve this, I followed the steps outlined in the "reset password" section of the Focalboard guide (https://www.focalboard.com/guide/admin/). Since the Docker container doesn't have a text editor, I had to install nano and curl. Curl is required for the reset script.

After installing nano and curl, I executed the following script:

bash reset-password.sh name temporarypassword
(Note: I replaced the actual name with 'name' here)

The output I received was:
{"error":"internal server error","errorCode":500} ...

The container's output while trying to reset the password states:

"user not found (name)" "API ERROR." Why has my user disappeared? Is it possible to check which users exist in the db?

triage.skip_reason: No clear repro steps for the migration/upgrade path; requires investigation of DB migration between 7.5.2 and latest, user schema changes, and Docker volume persistence. UI/data decisions involved.
triage.summary: Upgrading from Focalboard 7.5.2 to latest Docker version causes login failure; users appear missing from the database, and the reset-password script returns a 500 internal server error with 'user not found'. Likely a DB migration issue or schema change that drops or relocates user records during upgrade.
triage.test_hint: Verify DB migration scripts handle user table correctly when upgrading from 7.5.2; check if user data is preserved in Docker volume across container upgrades; test reset-password script against a migrated DB.

## Steps to reproduce the behavior
1. Login to Boards as User A and open a board (i.e., `Board 123`)
2. Go the the Share dialog and add User B to `Board 123` as any role
3. In a separate incognito browser, login as User B and go to `Board 123`
4. Go back to the window with User A and remove User B from the board
5. Go to window with User B and see error - user is taken to the template picker
6. As User A, add User B back to `Board 123`
7. Go to window with User B again and see error - `Board 123` is automatically opened for User B after User A added them back to the board
8. Repeat steps 1-7 on the template editor and see the same issue there as well.

## Expected behavior
**Step 5:** When a user is removed from a board, and they have other boards in their sidebar, they should be taken to the next available board on the sidebar.
**Step 7:** The board should be added back to User B's sidebar, but NOT automatically open for them. User B should remain on their current view.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Go to focalboard
2. Open a card
3. Type a comment
4. Delete a single character with backspace
5. Press ctrl+Z

## Expected behavior

I expect ctrl+Z to restore the character i deleted. Instead, nothing happens.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: the one on the community server, as of today
 - Version: v7.4.2
 - Browser and OS: both on Firefox 105.0.3, and on the Mattermost desktop client. OS is Ubuntu 22.04

## Additional context

This is Sev 3 (minor, non-critical functionality), but it can become a bit frustrating when typing long comments in particular. E.g. something gets deleted by mistake, and it cannot be restored in any way. I am currently typing my comments on a text editor first, then copypasting them into the card, in order to avoid running into this issue again.

A few cases in which ctrl+Z didn't work:
- Type a comment -> delete single character -> press ctrl+Z (it should restore the deleted character, but doesn't)
- Type a comment -> press ctrl+backspace to delete the last word -> press ctrl+Z (it should restore the deleted word, but doesn't)
- Type a comment -> press ctrl+Z (should delete the last word or letter that was typed, but doesn't)

Interestingly, there's also a case in which ctrl+Z worked well:
- Type a comment -> select it all with ctrl+A -> press backspace to delete it -> press ctrl+Z (it successfully restores the full deleted comment, as expected)
title: Bug: ctrl+Z doesn't always undo

triage.skip_reason: Undo behavior in rich text editors requires deep investigation of editor state management and keyboard event handling; no clear minimal fix, likely involves TipTap/Slate editor undo stack configuration
triage.summary: Ctrl+Z undo does not work in card comment editor for character/word deletions; only works after select-all+delete. Affects Firefox and Mattermost desktop on Ubuntu. Likely an issue with the rich text editor's history/undo stack not capturing incremental edits correctly.
triage.test_hint: Focus comment input, type text, delete a character with backspace, press Ctrl+Z and assert the character is restored; repeat with Ctrl+Backspace word deletion and plain Ctrl+Z to undo typed text.

body: Broken out of #3812. We have some error handling/routing logic that does variations of `/boards/error` and `/plugins/focalboard/error` that under the right circumstances will do a infinite recursive redirect loop (see video in original issue). To do: - determine condition(s) that cause this breakage - fix logic to prevent unnecessary redirects A thought: might be worth holding off until multi-product architecture ships as we'll no longer be a plugin in the event that changes some of the URLs or client side errors.
title: Investigate `/error` handling logic to prevent recursive redirects

triage.skip_reason: No clear repro steps; requires investigation of routing logic across plugin and standalone modes; potential architecture changes (multi-product) may invalidate any fix
triage.summary: Error page routing logic can cause infinite redirect loops between /boards/error and /plugins/focalboard/error under certain conditions. Needs root cause investigation before fixing.
triage.test_hint: Identify the navigation path that triggers the recursive redirect and add a guard in the error route handler to detect if the current path is already an error page before redirecting.

body:
While completing #2900 I found the root cause of #2898 to be that `Utils.buildURL` was modified and doesn't build absolute URLs in non-plugin installs even if the 2nd arg `absolute` is present and `true`. At a glance, the pattern inside the codebase seems to be to use `buildURL` to build a relative URL and append it to a URL with a base instead of building an absolute URL string all in one go.

e.g.

```ts
const url = new URL(window.location.origin)
url.pathname = Utils.buildURL('/foo?bar=baz', true)
```

I tracked the regression in `buildURL` to this diff:

https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/pull/2406/files#diff-9e0f028747bfe61f41e35d96170706c27451268275f8dd0303a77986ae329916R520-R523 (`blame` source: https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/blame/9001c3576f1cd13da6953b5c193e0cd7bd712de6/webapp/src/utils.ts#L520)

The intention of this ticket is to gauge whether some sort of refactor to `buildURL` and it's usages is worth it and then the scope of how to proceed with that.
title: Code Debt: `Utils.buildURL` doesn't build absolute URLs in non-plugin installations

triage.skip_reason: Requires understanding of all buildURL call sites and their expected behavior across plugin vs non-plugin install modes; risk of regressions across URL construction patterns throughout the codebase
triage.summary: Utils.buildURL ignores the `absolute=true` argument in non-plugin installations, causing absolute URL construction to silently produce relative URLs. Root cause traced to a regression introduced in PR #2406. Many call sites work around this by manually combining window.location.origin with the result, but the API contract is broken.
triage.test_hint: Add unit tests for Utils.buildURL covering: absolute=true in plugin mode, absolute=true in non-plugin mode, relative (default) calls in both modes, and paths with query strings. Then audit all call sites for correct usage.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Go to a board
2. Select  "Export to CSV" or "Export to board archive"
3. See error in popup window:

```txt
Failed to open URI.
The specified location is not supported.
data:text/csv;charset=utf-8,Name ...
```

or

```txt
Failed to open URI.
The specified location is not supported.
data:text/json, ...
```

4. The Snackbar does not get that there was an error and says "Export Complete!"

---

1. Select "Settings"
2. Select  "Export archive"
3. See error in popup window:

```txt
Failed to open URI.
Operation not supported.
data:text/json, ...
```

## Expected behavior

In the Windows App Store installation on my Windows PC a `.csv` or `.focalboard` file is created.
The bug makes it impossible to export boards.

## Screenshots

Not necessary since the error popup window was transcribed.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Desktop
 - Version: v0.10.0 (built by myself), v0.9.2 (downloaded from GitHub releases)
 - Browser and OS: Manjaro Linux (21.2.0, kernel 5.14.13-1-MANJARO, XFCE 4.16, Pacman)

## Additional context

To run the local desktop build I run:

```sh
make prebuild
make linux-app
cd linux
./bin/focalboard-app
```

because the in the README.md described `linux/dist/focalboard-app` path does not exist and running `linux/bin/focalboard-app` starts a completely white window.
title: Bug: Exporting boards on Manjaro Linux XFCE with Personal Desktop Edition results in an "Failed to open URI." error

triage.skip_reason: Requires Linux desktop environment testing; involves platform-specific URI/file download handling in Electron/desktop shell with no clear minimal repro beyond full OS setup
triage.summary: On Manjaro Linux XFCE with the Personal Desktop Edition, exporting boards (CSV, board archive, or settings archive) fails with 'Failed to open URI / The specified location is not supported' because the desktop app attempts to open a data: URI via the OS file handler instead of using a native save dialog or writing the file directly. Windows works correctly. The export snackbar incorrectly reports success despite the failure.
triage.test_hint: Check how the desktop/Electron layer handles file downloads or data: URI navigation; compare Windows vs Linux code paths for export; consider using Electron's dialog.showSaveDialog with a write stream instead of triggering a data: URI open.

title: Bug: WebSocket connection failed - One or more reserved bits are on: reserved1 = 0, reserved2 = 1, reserved3 = 1

triage.skip_reason: Environment-specific networking issue involving Apache reverse proxy WebSocket configuration; requires infrastructure/proxy setup to reproduce and likely needs proxy config documentation or backend WebSocket handling fix

body:
**Summary:**

The Focalboard plugin v0.8.2 on my Mattermost Teams Edition 5.38.0 installed with docker behind an Apache2 reverse proxy installation has websocket connection interruptions.
Mattermost works perfectly without any issue, chat is updated live and have been working fine since the beginning.
Focalboard works but does not save everything, probably due to the websocket issue.

**Steps to reproduce the behavior:**

1. Install Focalboard plugin v0.8.2 via MarketPlace
2. Update the apache configuration following the documentation [here](https://www.focalboard.com/download/mattermost/#with-apache-unofficial)
3. Activate it
4. Clic on Focalboard icon in a channel

**Expected behavior:**
Focalboard should not show the banner about websocket connection loss.

**Screenshots:**

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/25644865/131575838-6b2346fe-e745-44a7-96c5-c2ccb0bac946.png)

**Platform:**
 - Browser and version: Chrome 92
 - Mattermost Server v5.38.0 (docker)
 - Mattermost Desktop 4.7.1
 - Focalboard plugin v0.8.2
 - OS and version: macOS 11.5.2

**Additional context:**

Here is the relevant part of my Apache2 configuration:
```apache
    RewriteEngine On
    RewriteCond %{REQUEST_URI} /api/v[0-9]+/(users/)?websocket|/plugins/focalboard/ws/* [NC,OR]
    RewriteCond %{HTTP:UPGRADE} ^WebSocket$ [NC,OR]
    RewriteCond %{HTTP:CONNECTION} ^Upgrade$ [NC]
    RewriteRule .* ws://127.0.0.1:8065%{REQUEST_URI} [P,QSA,L]

    RewriteCond %{REQUEST_URI} ^/plugins/focalboard
    RewriteRule ^ http://%{HTTP_HOST}/mattermost%{REQUEST_URI} [R=301]

    RewriteCond %{REQUEST_URI} ^/mattermost
    RewriteRule .* http://127.0.0.1:8065%{REQUEST_URI} [P,QSA,L]

    <Location /mattermost>
            Require all granted
            ProxyPass http://127.0.0.1:8065/
            ProxyPassReverse http://127.0.0.1:8065/
            ProxyPassReverseCookieDomain 127.0.0.1 dsimb.inserm.fr
    </Location>
```
title: Bug: plugin websocket connection interrupted behind Apache2 reverse proxy

triage.skip_reason: Requires environment-specific reproduction with Apache2 reverse proxy and Mattermost plugin setup; involves websocket routing configuration and potential data-loss implications that need manual verification
triage.summary: Focalboard plugin WebSocket connections drop when Mattermost is deployed behind an Apache2 reverse proxy, causing the disconnection banner to appear and potential data save failures. The Apache rewrite rules for /plugins/focalboard/ws/* may not correctly handle WebSocket upgrade headers or may conflict with the redirect rule for /plugins/focalboard.
triage.test_hint: Verify WebSocket upgrade headers are correctly forwarded for the /plugins/focalboard/ws/* path; check if the RewriteRule redirect for ^/plugins/focalboard intercepts WebSocket traffic before the WebSocket rewrite rule can match; test with curl or wscat against the proxy to confirm handshake completes successfully.

body:
**Summary:**
Deleting a card component doesn't update the card's updated at timestamp

**Steps to reproduce the behavior:**
1. Open a card.
2. Add some items to it such as comments or text. Notice that adding these will update the card's updated at timestamp.
3. Now delete some added comments. Notice that doing so brings back the previous updated at for the card.

**Expected behavior:**
Deleting a card component should update car's updated at timestamp to the moment something was deleted.

**Screenshots:**
NA

**Platform:**
 - Browser and version: all browsers
 - OS and version: all OS

**Additional context:**
Card's updated at is calculated from the most recently updated component in the client. Since deleted components are not sent in the blocks API, the client is not aware of their existence. Hence, the outdated updated at time.
title: Bug: Deleting a card component doesn't update the card's updated at timestamp

triage.skip_reason: Requires architectural decision: either include deleted block tombstones in the API response or track deletion timestamps server-side and propagate to the parent card. Not a trivial fix.
triage.summary: Deleting a card component (e.g. comment, text block) does not update the parent card's 'updated at' timestamp because deleted blocks are excluded from the blocks API response, so the client never learns about the deletion and reverts to the previous latest timestamp.
triage.test_hint: Add a block to a card, record the card's updatedAt, delete the block, then assert the card's updatedAt is >= the deletion time via both API response and UI display.

title: Bug: no_workspace error on first-launch with FireFox

triage.skip_reason: Requires environment-specific reproduction (Firefox, non-TLS, Ubuntu) and likely involves auth flow or redirect logic; no clear minimal repro in code
triage.summary: On fresh install without TLS, Firefox redirects to /error?id=no_workspace instead of login/registration screen due to 401 no session token error before workspace initialization
triage.test_hint: Test first-launch auth flow in Firefox over HTTP (non-TLS); verify that unauthenticated requests without a session token redirect to login rather than /error?id=no_workspace

body:
**Summary:** I'm running Focalboard in docker behind the Traefik2 Reverse Proxy that has some middlewares set up. When trying to register I'm presented with an "Invalid registration link, please contact your administrator" error. There are no log messages written. The curious part is, that *any* Traefik2 middleware seems to cause this issue, but leaving them out works. I've tested with a Ratelimit middleware, a Headers middleware that just inserts a same-origin referrer policy and a basic-auth middleware. All of them cause the same issue, which makes a configuration mistake on my side very unlikely. **Steps to reproduce the behaviour:** 1. clone the repo 2. add traefik labels to docker-compose config (full configs below) 3. add db and app containers to network (full configs below) 4. docker-compose build and up 5. go to the domain configured in traefik 6. try to create a account **Expected behaviour:** Being able to create a account. **Platform:** - Browser and version: Firefox 87.0 - OS and version: Server: Ubuntu 20.04 LTS, Client: Windows 10 **Additional context:**
docker-compose.yml

Docker compose with added networks and Traefik2 labels. ```yaml version: "3" services: app: build: context: ./ container_name: focalboard depends_on: - focalboard-db environment: - VIRTUAL_HOST=focalboard.local - VIRTUAL_PORT=8000 volumes: - "./config.json:/opt/focalboard/config.json" networks: - internal - t2_proxy labels: - "traefik.enable=true" - "traefik.docker.network=t2_proxy" ## HTTP Routers - "traefik.http.routers.focalboard-rtr.entrypoints=https" - "traefik.http.routers.focalboard-rtr.rule=Host(`focalboard.domain.com`)" - "traefik.http.routers.focalboard-rtr.tls=true" ## Middlewares - "traefik.http.routers.focalboard-rtr.middlewares=focal-test-chain@file" ## HTTP Services - "traefik.http.routers.focalboard-rtr.service=focalboard-svc" - "traefik.http.services.focalboard-svc.loadbalancer.server.port=8000" focalboard-db: image: postgres:latest container_name: focalboard-postgres restart: always volumes: - "./data:/var/lib/postgresql/data" networks: - internal environment: POSTGRES_DB: boards POSTGRES_USER: boardsuser POSTGRES_PASSWORD: boardsuser-password networks: internal: t2_proxy: external: name: t2_proxy ```

traefik2 ratelimit middleware

Traefik2 middleware config used ```toml [http.middlewares] [http.middlewares.middlewares-rate-limit] [http.middlewares.middlewares-rate-limit.rateLimit] average = 100 burst = 50 [http.middlewares.focal-test-chain] [http.middlewares.focal-test-chain.chain] middlewares = [ "middlewares-rate-limit" ] ````

Request HAR json

This is the HAR from the request that is sent when i click the register button. Domains have been replaced with domain.com and IPs with 0.0.0.0. The error occurs whether or not i enter anything in the form, here i've left it empty. ```json { "log": { "version": "1.2", "creator": { "name": "Firefox", "version": "87.0" }, "browser": { "name": "Firefox", "version": "87.0" }, "pages": [ { "startedDateTime": "2021-04-11T19:20:33.089+02:00", "id": "page_4", "title": "Focalboard", "pageTimings": { "onContentLoad": -1, "onLoad": -1 } } ], "entries": [ { "pageref": "page_4", "startedDateTime": "2021-04-11T19:20:33.089+02:00", "request": { "bodySize": 51, "method": "POST", "url": "https://focalboard.domain.com/api/v1/register", "httpVersion": "HTTP/2", "headers": [ { "name": "Host", "value": "focalboard.domain.com" }, { "name": "User-Agent", "value": "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:87.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/87.0" }, { "name": "Accept", "value": "application/json" }, { "name": "Accept-Language", "value": "en-GB,en;q=0.5" }, { "name": "Accept-Encoding", "value": "gzip, deflate, br" }, { "name": "Referer", "value": "https://focalboard.domain.com/register" }, { "name": "Content-Type", "value": "application/json" }, { "name": "Authorization", "value": "" }, { "name": "X-Requested-With", "value": "XMLHttpRequest" }, { "name": "Origin", "value": "https://focalboard.domain.com" }, { "name": "Content-Length", "value": "51" }, { "name": "DNT", "value": "1" }, { "name": "Connection", "value": "keep-alive" }, { "name": "Pragma", "value": "no-cache" }, { "name": "Cache-Control", "value": "no-cache" }, { "name": "TE", "value": "Trailers" } ], "cookies": [], "queryString": [], "headersSize": 522, "postData": { "mimeType": "application/json", "params": [], "text": "{\"email\":\"\",\"username\":\"\",\"password\":\"\",\"token\":\"\"}" } }, "response": { "status": 401, "statusText": "Unauthorized", "httpVersion": "HTTP/2", "headers": [ { "name": "content-type", "value": "application/json" }, { "name": "date", "value": "Sun, 11 Apr 2021 17:20:02 GMT" }, { "name": "content-length", "value": "12" }, { "name": "X-Firefox-Spdy", "value": "h2" } ], "cookies": [], "content": { "mimeType": "application/json", "size": 12, "text": "{\"error\":\"\"}" }, "redirectURL": "", "headersSize": 136, "bodySize": 148 }, "cache": {}, "timings": { "blocked": 0, "dns": 0, "connect": 0, "ssl": 0, "send": 1, "wait": 26, "receive": 1 }, "time": 28, "_securityState": "secure", "serverIPAddress": "0.0.0.0", "connection": "443" } ] } } ```
title: Bug: Unable to register when running behind traefik reverse proxy with any middleware.

triage.skip_reason: Requires reverse proxy environment to reproduce; likely involves token/header validation or CSRF logic that depends on request origin/referrer headers being modified by middleware — needs investigation of registration link validation code
triage.summary: Registration fails with 'Invalid registration link' error when Focalboard is running behind a Traefik2 reverse proxy with any middleware enabled. The issue occurs regardless of middleware type (rate-limit, headers, basic-auth), suggesting the middleware modifies or strips a request header (e.g., Origin, Referer, X-Forwarded-For, or a token) that the backend uses to validate the registration link.
triage.test_hint: Inspect the registration link validation logic in the backend; check if it validates Referer, Origin, or Host headers and whether those are affected by proxy middleware. Test with curl reproducing headers that Traefik middlewares add/remove.

title: Bug: memory leak

body:
We need to make the permissions model is working as expected.

Specifically, a team member should be able to join any public board for that team. 

Also need to ensure private boards are private (ie, System Admins cannot join.) However, there also needs to be a way to recover a private board if the board admin is no longer a user. How can System Admin update board permissions?
title: Permissions Branch - Determine missing features of permissions model

triage.skip_reason: Large complexity, involves security-sensitive permissions model design decisions, no concrete repro steps, requires architectural discussion
triage.summary: Permissions model is incomplete: team members cannot join public boards, private board access control is broken (System Admins can access private boards), and there is no recovery path for private boards whose admin has left.

body:
**Summary:**
A way to group multiple boards that are related in some way.

**How important this is to me and why:**
High
If Focalboard is to replace our current solution, in which we have 100+ boards spread across 7 different 'workspaces', this is absolutely Critical.

Use cases:
1. This could be a team, with a lot of boards.
2. It could be any one person with multiple boards.
3. Or boards grouped by a topic.

**Additional context / similar features:**
Could be a dropdown menu in the top of the menubar, from which you can switch between groups/workspaces.
Could also be the ability to fold/unfold groups/workspaces in a nested hierarchy in the menubar.

Example from another project tool, that has the name of the first day of the work week.
![Screenshot 2021-08-27 at 10 33 11](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/4245257/131098073-d8241eaf-bd5d-42fe-97fe-18979a176f9a.png)
title: Feature Idea: Grouping of boards

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring UI/UX decisions, architectural changes for workspace/grouping model, and no concrete technical spec
triage.summary: Allow users to group/organize multiple boards into workspaces or folders, with navigation via dropdown or nested sidebar hierarchy

body:
**Summary:**
I'd like to be able to filter by dates, numbers, regex patterns on strings / urls / etc.

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: High

To be able to use focalboard to store data, filtering is a key requirement for me. For example if I want to track tasks, having due dates on those tasks, being able to build a view that shows only tasks that are overdue seems crucial.

Use cases:
1. Show only tasks overdue
2. Show only tasks that can be started after X date
3. Add a numerical priority field and show only tasks with prio > 3

**Additional context / similar features:**
Trello has filtering capabilities of some kind, I'm not familiar with asana / notion but I'd assume they do.

FYI, if this is already implemented, then I'm reporting a bug that on the standalone desktop app on macOS I can only seem to filter by status fields and not others.
title: Feature Idea: Filter by more field types

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring new filter logic for multiple field types (dates, numbers, regex); requires UI/UX decisions, backend query changes, and broad testing across field types
triage.summary: Allow filtering by additional field types including dates (before/after/overdue), numbers (comparisons), and regex patterns on text/URL fields. Currently only status fields appear to be filterable on the macOS desktop app.
triage.test_hint: Verify filter UI exposes date, number, and text field types; test date comparisons (overdue, after X), numeric comparisons (greater/less than), and regex matching on string fields across board and table views

body:
**Summary:**
Be able to assign a card to a user. There are several ways we could implement this:
1. Add an "assigned to" property to each card (so every card has this)
2. Add a new "assigned to" property type (so it can be optionally added to a card)

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: High

Use cases:
1. Show task assignees for projects
2. Notify assignees of changes

**Additional context / similar features:**
A related feature is adding a "due date" property, so that assignees can be notified of pending deadlines.

This is related to the [user property type feature](https://focalboard-community.octo.mattermost.com/workspace/qgsck6cts3fwpqwyjiupjm5cde/shared?id=47aa9bb4-6967-4a96-83c7-11bd6b20f1eb&v=ca1a5441-4c1c-4d0e-856e-88cc744576c8&r=762db9c6-305c-4d13-8ce6-9f11e5232c24&c=2b8cc90a-e91c-4d62-8d95-2f219ab248a7).

Filing this issue to solicit feedback. Are there any other typical use cases you have around this?
title: Feature Idea: Assigning cards to users

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring UI/UX decisions, backend schema changes, and notification system integration; no concrete spec finalized
triage.summary: Allow cards to be assigned to users via a new 'assigned to' property type, enabling task ownership and future deadline notifications.

body:
**Summary:**
A two factor authentication method should be added to greatly improve the security of the application, in addition to that, receiving a email when someone tries to log into our account would be very good as it would allow us to know whether our password got leaked. All of these should be features that the user can enable or disable.

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: High
Security is a fundamental pillar in today's web applications

Use cases:
1. Prevent unauthorized access
2. Get notified when someone tries to access your account using your password

**Additional context / similar features:**
Almost all websites have a two factor authentication option.
A basic implementation of this would be to support the following methods:
* Authenticator app
* Email
* Backup codes
title: Feature Idea: Two factor authentication

triage.skip_reason: Large, security-critical feature requiring significant backend auth infrastructure changes, UI decisions, and third-party integrations; not suitable for automated implementation.
triage.summary: Feature request to add two-factor authentication (TOTP/authenticator app, email OTP, backup codes) and login notification emails, with per-user opt-in/opt-out controls.

body:
**Summary:**
What I really miss right now is the ability to see what is happening on boards I'm participating in. Have people updated descriptions, added comments? The only way to detect these things (even to see if a card has a comment or not) is to regularly open every single one and do a "visual diff." This is not great, it would be really cool to have a view where you can see recent changes to a board, or even get notified about ones about cards I care about.

**How important this is to me and why:**
Pretty important. Right now I end up manually notifying people about my comments out of band (on Mattermost messaging).

Importance: High

Use cases:
1. Better monitor board activity
2. Know when people ask questions in comments

**Additional context / similar features:**
Any examples of good implementations of this capability.

Any type of timeline view would be helpful, if I'd have to pick between notifications that are pushed to me (somehow) or just a stream view (question if this should be per board or per workspace — I'd say per workspace) I'd pick the latter. But notifications could also be posted (optionally, perhaps filtered) on the Mattermost channel the boards are connected to. That could be a "simple" MVP implementation perhaps. "Comment added to card X by Y."
title: Feature Idea: Notifications/event stream

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring backend event streaming, notification infrastructure, UI design decisions, and Mattermost integration; no clear spec narrow enough to implement autonomously.
triage.summary: Add notifications/event stream so users can see recent board activity (comments, edits) and optionally receive push notifications via Mattermost channel integration.

body:
**Summary:**
Due date field type, a la "created" and "updated" but meant to track the date (and possibly time) a task is due.  As with Trello, the colour of the card can change depending on the current amount of time until (or past) the given timestamp we are.

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: High

Use cases:
1. Completely general for both personal and professional use.  If something needs to be done today, or within the next hour, it's valuable to have a program let you track that and remind you of it.

**Additional context / similar features:**
This is a basic feature of Trello for the above reason.

To the extent that the "due date" seen in the screenshots suggests such functionality, it's misleading as currently implemented: The "due date" field in the template turns out to be only a text label.  I guess that's technically sortable if you stick to ISO timestamps / datestamps, but there's no inherent date functionality, i.e. the software cannot display a calendar view, or calculate how far away a task is, or sort correctly if you prefer a 12-hour clock (e.g. `"11:00 AM" > "1:00 PM"`).

(It would make sense that the import scripts would take this data from Trello, Asana, etc. once implemented.)
title: Feature Idea: Actual due dates

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring new field type, calendar rendering, color-coding logic, sorting, and potentially notification/reminder infrastructure — too broad for automated attempt.
triage.summary: Add a native Date field type with calendar picker, proper sorting, and visual urgency indicators (color-coding based on proximity to due date), similar to Trello's due date feature.
triage.test_hint: Verify date field renders calendar picker, sorts chronologically, displays correct color states (upcoming/overdue/due-soon), and imports correctly from Trello/Asana.
