body: - I have been looking at Mattermost for similar issues, but I could not find one myself. - It does not involve security issues - I pretty new to this type of stuff, so try to forgive me the stupid questions I will ask :) #### Summary After updating `Mattermost/mattermost-team-edition:release-7.11` to `Mattermost/mattermost-team-edition:release-9.4` suddenly Mattermost Boards is acting weird. Team boards are dissapearing in one account and they are still available in an other account. And downgrading does not resolve the issue (Or I am not downgrading in the correct way) #### Steps to reproduce `Mattermost/mattermost-team-edition:release-9.4` `postgres:15` 1. Log:`The database was created using collation version 2.31, but the operating system provides version 2.36.` 2. I solved this message, by export and build a new database with a new version. #### Expected behavior Assuming it was a DB issue: after building a new database, I expected that this issues was not happening anymore Assuming it is a Mattermost update issue: After downgrading it should be resolved. #### Observed behavior (that appears unintentional) What did you see happen? Please include relevant error messages and/or screenshots. ## Expected behavior

The card should move directly to the dropped position

## Screenshots
https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/46905241/153519856-3a0fd910-a008-46d9-bfca-fd1ee70e68d1.mov

 - Version: v0.14

In the browser network tab, notice that PATCH Block is called twice. This is because on [onDropToCard in kanban.tsx](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/blob/main/webapp/src/components/kanban/kanban.tsx#L172), the property change and sort order are two separate API calls. Propose refactoring to call UpdateBlocks in a single call.
title: Perf: Drag and drop cards in Kanban board view shows intermediate sort state

triage.summary: Drag-and-drop in Kanban view triggers two separate PATCH Block API calls (property change + sort order), causing a visible intermediate sort state. Fix by merging both updates into a single UpdateBlocks call in onDropToCard in kanban.tsx.
triage.test_hint: After drag-and-drop, verify only one PATCH/UpdateBlocks network request is made and the card appears directly at the dropped position without any intermediate repositioning.

body:
## Summary

linux-app should use `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` and `$HOME/.config` to save everything that requires write access.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: High

Use cases:
1. Distribute as a package
2. Per-user config and database

## Additional context/similar features

I've spent some time working on packaging focalboard into the following formats: rpm, deb, flatpak
While everything is going as planned there's a major issue; apps don't have write access to their installed location by default (in focalboard's case, `/opt/focalboard-app`).

While there might be workarounds, the best practice would be to move anything that requires write access to `.config` (`dbconfig` & `filespath` (?)). Doing so will also allow each user to have their own .db as well as ease backups and restores.

From a look at the source, it seems that `config.json` is being ignored (for the app at least).

Here's some solutions I came up with:

- Use the values from the config.json in combination with the hardcoded ones.
- Use cli flags or env vars to override them
- Change the hardcoded value to use `~/.config`
```go
// linux/main.go
...
func runServer(port int) (*server.Server, error) {
	logger, _ := mlog.NewLogger()
+	configFolder := os.Getenv("XDG_CONFIG_HOME")
+	if len(configFolder) == 0 {
+		configFolder = os.Getenv("HOME") + "/.config"
+	}

+	configFolder = configFolder + "/focalboard-app"

+	if _, err := os.Stat(configFolder); os.IsNotExist(err) {
+		_ = os.Mkdir(configFolder, 0755)
+	}

	config := &config.Configuration{
		ServerRoot:              fmt.Sprintf("http://localhost:%d", port),
		Port:                    port,
		DBType:                  "sqlite3",
+		DBConfigString:          configFolder + "/focalboard.db",
		UseSSL:                  false,
		SecureCookie:            true,
		WebPath:                 "./pack",
		FilesDriver:             "local",
+		FilesPath:               configFolder + "/focalboard_files",
		Telemetry:               true,
		WebhookUpdate:           []string{},
		SessionExpireTime:       259200000000,
		SessionRefreshTime:      18000,
		LocalOnly:               false,
		EnableLocalMode:         false,
		LocalModeSocketLocation: "",
		AuthMode:                "native",
	}
...
```

Some notes on them:
- The third one could be used with #1018 which would handle the creation of the config folder
- #1256 (for linux-app) would also be useful for per-user configuration (plus wouldn't require root to edit it)
- The third solution would unfortunately break existing installations as the hardcoded path would change
- Both the first and second solutions are easy to use with the packages

After a solution to this is found, I'll be able to continue working on packaging focalboard in more formats (rpm, deb, flatpak, appimage, snap).
title: Feature Idea: linux-app should use ~/.config for anything that needs write access

triage.summary: Linux desktop app hardcodes write paths under the install directory (e.g. /opt/focalboard-app) instead of using XDG_CONFIG_HOME / ~/.config, breaking packaging formats like rpm, deb, and flatpak where the install location is read-only.
triage.test_hint: On Linux, launch the app after setting XDG_CONFIG_HOME to a temp dir; verify focalboard.db and focalboard_files are created there. Without XDG_CONFIG_HOME set, verify they land in ~/.config/focalboard-app/. Confirm existing behavior is unchanged on macOS/Windows.

body:
## Summary

When pasting/adding images into a card, there's no way to preview that image or even download it. Due to this nature, it can be hard to review attached images or even download them to preview them in another application.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: High

Use cases:
1. When an image is attached to a card as content, when clicking on the image, it should open up another modal with the image preview
2. From there, the user could then be able to view the image in full size or "fitted" to their screen size and allow them to zoom
3. In the preview they could even download the image for a number of reasons

1. When clicking on the three dots beside an image, it would be nice to have a download option - or even implement one that displays when hovering over images to save a few clicks

## Additional context/similar features

A project I'm currently working on requires sharing of mockup images between designers and developers. Unfortunately, while we can attach images, there's no way for the developers to "enhance" the image in the card to view the full design. This has left us with having to share images outside of the card and including a link to that external source. It would be nice to have it as an all-in-one system to remove the need for an external dependency. Other than that, it's been great!!
title: Feature Idea: Image preview/download for attached images

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX decisions required for modal design, zoom behavior, and download flow; no clear technical spec
triage.summary: Add image preview modal and download option for images attached to cards. Clicking an image should open a lightbox-style modal with fit/full-size view and zoom. A download action should be accessible via the three-dot menu or on hover.
triage.test_hint: Verify clicking an attached image opens a preview modal; verify download triggers correct file download; verify zoom/fit controls work; verify three-dot menu includes download option.

body:
**Summary:**
Add configuration options: 1) enable proxy auth, 2) proxy auth http-header (e.g. `X-Authenticated-User`).

If proxy auth is enabled, user login is bypassed and the user that is present in the specified header is used.

Optional: if no such user exists, automatically create one.

**How important this is to me and why:**
Some on-premise hosting approaches put everything behind a reverse proxy like Traefik and let authentication be handled there. See e.g. [here](https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/middlewares/http/forwardauth/). This feature would enable this kind of deployment.

Importance: High - because it might be really easy and therefore a quick win.

Use cases:
1. on-premise hosting with reverse-proxy auth
2. personal self-hosting

**Additional context / similar features:**
Example: [Filebrowser](https://filebrowser.org/configuration/authentication-method#proxy-header)

Thanks a lot, cheers!
title: Feature Request: Add support for authentication by reverse proxy

triage.skip_reason: Large feature involving authentication flow changes, security implications, and config schema updates — requires human review for security design decisions
triage.summary: Add reverse-proxy authentication support: new config options to enable proxy auth and specify the trusted header (e.g. X-Authenticated-User), bypassing normal login flow. Optionally auto-create users on first seen header value.
triage.test_hint: Test with proxy header present/absent, valid/invalid user, auto-create flag on/off, and ensure normal auth still works when feature is disabled

body:
This adds support for displaying inline and displayed Latex equations using Katex in markdown cells, fixing #1509.

The conversion happens before each paragraph is converted from markdown to HTML. At the moment, any parsing error thrown by KaTeX is caught and the chunk ignored. In other words, the following `$\thisisinvalid$` will render as-is, including the surrounding `$`.

# Demonstration
## Markdown editor
![Screenshot from 2021-11-04 11-02-18](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5411875/140302935-a7862292-e741-4d44-aed6-b8b316f678e7.png)

## Automatic rendering when leaving the editor
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5411875/140302787-e84775a8-d6f2-444d-95e1-572d35762489.png)
title: [GH-1509] Add support for Latex rendering in markdown cells

triage.skip_reason: UI rendering feature requiring visual review and design decision on KaTeX error handling behavior; large dependency addition needs security/bundle review
triage.summary: Add KaTeX-based LaTeX rendering support for inline and display math in markdown cells, with silent fallback on parse errors.
triage.test_hint: Verify inline ($...$) and display ($$...$$) LaTeX renders correctly in markdown cells; confirm invalid LaTeX falls back gracefully; check bundle size impact of KaTeX dependency.

body:
## Summary

As stated by @wiggin77 in this [comment](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/3852#issuecomment-1283984863) notifications by the notification bot will only work if each (non-creator) person involved into a card has clicked "Follow" or has been mentioned manually.

From our point of view:
We have multiple cards that travel along a process of reviews etc. and need to be passed between members of the team.
Therefor we use the Type: "Multiple Persons" and Type: "Person".

Now it seems that people added to this field are not auto subscribed to the card and do not receive notifications until we mention those people in the comments or they subscribe themselves.

There should be a flag for "auto subscribe" for those to field types, so that we can enable automatic notifications for "traveling" cards, which we assign to other people.

## How important this is to me and why
Without notifications the boards are not maintained by the people and tasks will sit around for ages until the person who is responsible remembers to have a look at the boards and filter to cards assigned to them.

Importance: High

Use cases:
1. Chained of task states that are passed between people
2. Centrally managed notifications on assigned people

## Additional context/similar features
Any other Kanban software does automatically follow new assigned persons afaik.
title: Feature Idea: Set Card Owner and Auto Enable Following

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring backend subscription logic changes, frontend UI decisions, and cross-cutting concerns across notification system
triage.summary: Auto-subscribe users to a card when they are added to a Person or Multi-Person property field, so they receive notifications without needing to manually follow or be @mentioned.
triage.test_hint: When a user is added to a Person-type property on a card, verify they are automatically subscribed and receive subsequent card notifications; verify removing them unsubscribes if appropriate.

body:
## Summary

For the filters section, instead of asking for a fixed date or fixed person, make it customized to the time period or whoever is logged in. 

This allows for pages of boards to be created to view outstanding issues due in a short period of time and triage tasks relevant to the person that is looking at the board 

## How important this is to me and why
There are 3 use cases that I can outline. In my view, this feature would greatly reduce confusion and increase the usability of the boards. Because of my current project setup, the number of people and the tasks on the board are too difficult to consume. Not everyone is well versed in changing the filtering to their liking, and once they do, it changes for everyone. Creating views per person is not practical and the boards are unusable without selective filtering. 

Importance: High

Use cases:
1. Filter down large boards to cards relevant to the user 
2. Create dynamic views based on date 
3. When dynamic filters are built, this will allow filters to be present for all users
 

## Additional context/similar features

Any examples of good implementations of this capability.
title: Feature Idea: Dynamic Filters

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring significant design decisions around dynamic filter semantics, UI, and backend query changes; no concrete spec provided
triage.summary: Request for dynamic/contextual filters that resolve relative to the current user (e.g. 'Assigned to me') or current date/time period (e.g. 'Due this week') rather than fixed static values, enabling shared views that are personalized per viewer.

body:
Big thanks for your awesome work in advance!

## Summary

Currently there is no overwrite protection when two people edit one block at the same time: if you defocus a block it gets auto-saved and your local state overwrites potential edits by others. As I think true collaboration (editing at the same time, seeing changes live, ...) is very difficult to implement and comes with other downsides (stable internet connection, performance, ...?) **I would suggest to just lock a block, when someone else has the same block in focus.** 

## How important this is to me and why

_Importance: High_
To me and my team this issue is of extraordinary importance. It is not only the loss of data but also the trust in the tool that makes this a problem for us. See the example:

Use cases:
1. Person A has a block opened and in focus
2. Person B typed loads of content in the same block (like a transcript)
3. A now closes the block to save the content
4. B does not get the update as the block is still open, closes the block and therefore overwrites all edits by A 


## How it could be solved

So I imagine (from a user's perspective, I don't know about the technical details) a lock icon appearing on the left of the block, when someone is already editing this block, hindering me to enter the block at all. 


I use focalsboards as part of a mattermost install on yunohost (v 6.5.0)
Thanks a lot and keep up the good work!
title: Feature Idea: Locking blocks for better collaboration

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring real-time presence tracking, websocket coordination, lock state management across frontend and backend — no clear spec for conflict resolution edge cases and UI decisions involved.
triage.summary: Request to lock blocks when another user is editing them to prevent concurrent overwrites. Proposes showing a lock icon and preventing focus entry on already-edited blocks.

body:
## Summary

Currently, there is no way to know if a card has a "comment" or a "description" without clicking on it.
That would be super neat to have an icon that tells me so at first glance (e.g. a three-dots "..." or an ℹ️  on the bottom right of a card). If there is this clue, I know I have to click the card to see the full context. If not, I know clicking it won't give me any additional info.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: High (to me at least :) )

Use cases:
1. I usually use cards for work-to-do-later. Sometimes the description is enough for me to remember everything two months later. Sometimes I add ideas or URLS in the comments, and I usually have forgotten them two months later. But sometimes I forgot that _I even added_ these details, and I never click the card. So I don't retrieve the hints my past-self left for my current-self, and as a consequence I waste time to re-do what I did previously. If I had a visual clue, that would not happen

## Additional context/similar features

I know very little about web development, so I'm afraid I cannot do this myself, even though I think that should not be super complex to do.
Unless you have very clear instructions or hints, and I might try to do this myself?

Thanks!
title: Feature Idea: add an icon when there is a comment or description

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX design decision required — icon placement, style, and which views (board/gallery/table) to support need product sign-off before implementation
triage.summary: Show a visual indicator icon on card thumbnails when the card has a description or at least one comment, so users can identify cards with additional context without opening them.
triage.test_hint: Verify icon appears on board/gallery cards when description is non-empty or comment count > 0; verify icon disappears when description is cleared and all comments deleted; check all card views (board, gallery, table rows).

body:
## Summary

I often find myself searching for a couple of different cards in quick succession and a constant friction that I notice is having to remove the existing text when I do an alternate search. It would be nice if the search box had a clear button, that I could click to both bring focus to the search box and clear the existing search query. Here's an example of my suggestion/request:

Currently:
![screenshot](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/assets/818633/84977e19-d1f6-4c1d-a281-1eb368458015)

With a search clear button:
![screenshot-clear](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/assets/818633/3b08c5b1-be43-4300-87f3-3a9e97e90cbb)

Obviously there are workarounds e.g. `<Ctrl>`-`<a>` to select all text, then start typing (when focus is already in the search bar), or if focus is elsewhere, then `<Ctrl>`-`<f>` will achieve the same ends. But this inconsistency is part of my friction. I can't just do a consistent single action to clear the search box.

Another workaround with the mouse is to select all search text and start typing, but that involves getting the selection right, which requires more thought and/or mouse accuracy than say, clicking a button :grimacing: .

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium to Low (annoyance)

## Additional context/similar features

IME a search "clear" button is common and almost ubiquitous, anywhere a search box is found. E.g. google search.

I suspect that the implementation would be relatively trivial and I'd potentially be open to having a go myself. However, I don't really know go or typescript/javascript (beyond the very basic basics - and I suspect the latter would be required, possibly the former too?). I'm familiar with a number of other languages, so I'm sure I could fudge my way through it and at least string together a PoC. But without some pointers on where I should start, it feels pretty overwhelming...

Plus there's also the issue that even if I do the work and implement this myself, now focalboard is "community supported", it's unclear when or even if it would get merged?!
title: Feature Idea: Search box "clear" button

triage.summary: Add a clear button to the search box that resets the query and returns focus to the input field, similar to common search UX patterns.
triage.test_hint: Render the search component with a non-empty query, verify the clear button appears, click it, and assert the input value is empty and focused.

body:
I'm pretty sure that this is a shortcoming of the current documentation<s>, although there is a chance that it's a bug</s>.

I'm using Focalboard as a Mattermost plugin and have just recently started playing with the API.

It took me a while to get it going as the docs are a bit suboptimal (I want to complain, but I work in open source too and I get that there are only so many hours in the day).

FWIW these 2 issues are essential reading for getting started with the Focalboard Mattermost plugin API IMO:
- https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/1116
- https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/4420

Plus obviously the actual API docs:
https://htmlpreview.github.io/?https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/blob/main/server/swagger/docs/html/index.html#api-Default-getCards

On face value it all appeared to be working well. Then I noticed that there were some cards missing! When I dug in a little deeper, I realised that it was only returning 100 results<s>?!</s>.

I note that the docs (at least for `cards`) show the suffixes `?page=56&per_page=56`

So I tried checking with `?page=1` and that shows <s>one of</s> the missing cards, so on the right track. Then I tried with `?page=2` and got no results<s>?!</s> I also tried with `?page=0` and that just gave me the same 100 results I was getting initially (so <s>I assume that</s> **with no suffix it defaults to `?page=0&per_page=100`**!<s>?</s>).

After a bit of trial and error, I ended up getting all the cards via a `?page=0&per_page=200` suffix (I have less than 200 cards). So whilst I'm now unstuck, I do have some remaining questions:

- Why does `?page=2` give no results?! Shouldn't whatever doesn't fit on page 1 spill over to page 2 and so on?
  - To answer my own question: Yes. `page=0` is the first page of results, `page=1` is the second page, `page=2` returns no results as I have less than 200 cards!
- <s>How do I ensure that I'm getting all cards (without just increasing `per_page` until it gives no extra results)? Is there a way to page through all results?</s>
- And/or is there a way to force the API to return all results (i.e. turn off pagination)?
title: Doc: How to properly use API pagination?

triage.summary: API pagination docs are unclear: page is 0-indexed, default is page=0&per_page=100, and users are confused about how to iterate through all results. Need clear documentation with examples.
triage.test_hint: Verify API pagination examples in docs cover 0-indexed pages, default values, and a loop pattern to retrieve all results.

New boards are always created under the "Boards" category but this is not always the preferred behavior.  If I have created a new Category and then want to create a board under that category, I'd expect it to be added to that category. I'd like this behavior changed as it makes sense to create the board under the category I'm working in.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium.  There is the "Move to" function where Boards can be moved to another category but this is just an extra step that wastes time and shouldn't be required.

Use cases:
1. Creating boards within the Category I'm working in

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/13020833/211478810-802a3fa4-d8ca-47c8-9b59-ffc7b6f160a8.png)

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/13020833/211478894-4add6b19-0ed5-4da1-9262-763658ce08e9.png)

triage.test_hint: Verify that clicking 'Add board' while a custom category is selected places the new board under that category, and that the default 'Boards' category is used when no custom category is active.

body:
## Summary

Currently, when moving a board to another category from the sidebar, I can only select existing categories. There may be cases where I want to move the board to a new category. To do so, I'd first have to create the new category, then go back to the Options Menu (...) from the sidebar and move the board.

If we can add a "Create New Category" option under the existing categories in the menu (similar to Channels), it would allow users to easily move boards to a newly created category.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Moving boards to a new category

body:
## Summary

It's not immediately clear in the linking modal/process how linking works, in terms of what it is, and why to do it. Most channels will already have linked boards so the default "landing page" won't be displayed. 

It also seems a bit too easy to link a board to a new channel. I wanted to test it but it seemed like it could really break things for the currently linked channel. For example - how would they know the board that they had linked had been removed and re-linked elsewhere? If they try to link it back, the channel that it's now linked to would lose it without notification. Sort of like a boards tug of war. 

## How important this is to me and why

High - this may impact user experience and cause frustration.

## Additional context/similar features

I propose adding help text to the "Link board" modal. Something along the lines of:

"Linking a board adds it to the channel's RHS for easy access. Read our docs for more information." and the docs can provide a detailed breakdown of how it works and the use cases.
title: Feature Idea: UX improvement for linking channels

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX decision requiring design review and product input on copy and behavior
triage.summary: Add help text and clearer UX to the 'Link Board' modal to explain what linking does, why to do it, and warn users about implications of re-linking a board that is already linked to another channel.
triage.test_hint: Verify help text appears in link board modal; verify edge case behavior when linking a board already linked to another channel (notification or warning shown to user).

body:
### Below is the error received 

```shell npm run pack

> focalboard@7.2.0 pack
> cross-env NODE_ENV=production webpack --config webpack.prod.js

sh: line 1: cross-env: command not found
make: *** [Makefile:158: webapp] Error 127
```

## Steps to reproduce the behavior

Following [dev-setup guide](https://developers.mattermost.com/contribute/focalboard/personal-server-setup-guide/#building-and-running-standalone-desktop-apps), 
I did the following steps.
1. git clone the repo git@github.com:mattermost/focalboard.git
2. cd into the cloned repo folder
3. run the command `make linux-app`

## Expected behavior

It should build the standalone linux desktop application without giving the npm dependencies error(mentioned above)

## Screenshots (optional)

If applicable, add screenshots or a screen recording to elaborate on the problem.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Desktop 
 - Version: latest commit from the main branch
 - Browser and OS: ArcoLinux
title: Bug: Gives dependency error when doing linux developer setup following the developer setup guide

triage.summary: The dev-setup guide for building the Linux standalone desktop app is missing a step to run `npm install` (or equivalent) before `make linux-app`, causing `cross-env: command not found` because node_modules are not installed.
triage.test_hint: Verify that adding an `npm install` step (inside the webapp directory) to the setup guide resolves the missing `cross-env` dependency error on a clean Linux clone.

body:
We should have the `npm run i18n-extract` step ran in CI similarly to linting e.g. fail CI if there are i18n changes that weren't committed.

Rough CI steps:

1. `npm run i18n-extract`
2. Check if changes e.g. `git status --porcelain` or `git diff --exit-code`

- https://remarkablemark.org/blog/2017/10/12/check-git-dirty/
- https://unix.stackexchange.com/questions/155046/determine-if-git-working-directory-is-clean-from-a-script
title: Code Debt: Check `npm run i18n-extract` in CI

triage.summary: Add `npm run i18n-extract` as a CI check that fails if i18n files have uncommitted changes after extraction, similar to linting enforcement.
triage.test_hint: Run `npm run i18n-extract` then `git diff --exit-code` in CI pipeline and verify it fails when i18n strings are missing from committed files.

body:
## Summary

As the server gets more sophisticated, we recommend / require multi-user environments to use Postgres instead of SQLite. E.g. there are bugs like https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/1841 which are caused by concurrency issues on SQLite. 

As a result, we should default Personal Server installations to Postgres instead of SQLite.
title: Update the dockerfile to deploy Postgres instead of SQLite

triage.summary: Update the Dockerfile and related configuration to use Postgres as the default database instead of SQLite for Personal Server installations, addressing concurrency issues present with SQLite in multi-user environments.
triage.test_hint: Build the updated Docker image and verify the container starts successfully with a Postgres backend, can connect and run migrations, and that basic board CRUD operations work correctly under the new default configuration.

**Test code arrangement:** ``` it('MM-T4280 Accessing from the Product Switcher', () => { // code }); ``` Note: 1. Use [queries from testing-library](https://testing-library.com/docs/dom-testing-library/api-queries) whenever possible. We share the same philosophy as the [testing-library](https://testing-library.com/) when doing UI automation like "Interact with your app the same way as your users" and so, please follow their guidelines especially when querying an element. If you're interested, please comment here and come [join our "Contributors" community channel](https://community.mattermost.com/core/channels/tickets) on our daily build server, where you can discuss questions with community members and the Mattermost core team. For technical advice or questions, please [join our "Focalboard" community channel](https://community.mattermost.com/core/channels/focalboard). For new contributors, please see our [guide](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard#running-and-testing-the-server).

body:
**Summary:**

Currently the comment section renders above the description section. I personally find this a little inconvenient when working on a board alone because I rarely ever use the comment functionality but I very frequently edit or read the description section.

Even in multi-user scenarios, I think it would be more intuitive to have the two sections swapped. When looking at a card, it seems more likely that you first want to understand all the details about the card before reading other people's comments. As an example, Jira (not that it would be the pinnacle of UX) displays a task's summary, description and attachments before the comment section.

Instead of (or in addition to) swapping the sections, there could maybe also be an option to disable comments or to freely reorder the two sections per card (though that might be overkill).

![Screenshot 2021-04-27 at 14 43 13](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1137962/116243493-48d01a80-a767-11eb-9d54-ae6b89d46c72.png)

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: Low

Use cases:

1. I want to parse the information on a card without other people's comments getting in the way
2. I'm a single user and don't want to use the comments
title: Feature Idea: Swap order of comment and description sections

triage.summary: Swap the order of description and comment sections in the card detail view so description appears above comments, matching common conventions like Jira.
triage.test_hint: Render card detail view and assert that the description section appears in the DOM before the comment section.

body:
**Summary:**
The code in [initialize.go](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/blob/main/server/services/store/sqlstore/initialize.go) loads the default templates in `templates.json` from to Go bindata into the database. That data is currently stored in the (old) JSON format. The export archive format is now JSONL, and the import code plus `template.json` needs to be updated to match.

This will make future updates to the default templates much easier.
title: Update template initialization to use JSONL format

triage.summary: Update template initialization in initialize.go to load templates from JSONL format instead of the legacy JSON format, and convert templates.json to templates.jsonl accordingly.
triage.test_hint: Verify that default templates are correctly loaded into the database on server startup after the format change; run existing store/integration tests to confirm no regressions in template initialization.

<!-- Thank you for contributing a pull request! Here are a few tips to help you:

1. If this is your first contribution, make sure you've read the Contribution Checklist https://developers.mattermost.com/contribute/getting-started/contribution-checklist/
2. Read our blog post about "Submitting Great PRs" https://developers.mattermost.com/blog/2019-01-24-submitting-great-prs
3. Take a look at other repository specific documentation at https://developers.mattermost.com/contribute
-->

#### Summary
<!--
A description of what this pull request does.
-->

Adds OpenGraph tags to the website so it looks more attractive when shared in instant messengers or social media.

More information in the GitHub issue.

You're welcome to suggest changing any particular tag that was set, I just chose what I'd think are sensible values. A better image could be used, but thought I'd leave that one up to you rather, I opted to only pick from what static assets are already being served.

This does do a minor change on the side, if the `title` (tab name) doesn't have `Focalboard` in it, it appends `| Focalboard` to it. (Similar to the behavior you have on the Mattermost website.)

For example, the landing page is still:
`Focalboard: Open source alternative to Trello, Asana, and Notion`

But most other pages went from a format like:
`Personal Edition` → `Personal Edition | Focalboard`

#### Ticket Link
<!--
If this pull request addresses a Help Wanted ticket, please link the relevant GitHub issue, e.g.

  Fixes https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost-server/issues/XXXXX

Otherwise, link the JIRA ticket.
-->

* Closes #3412

#### Previews

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/22801583/180613111-a5b0341a-ef14-4753-899a-1bbb26d724de.png)

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/22801583/180613090-510ad1c9-9282-4607-b60a-5b9dba9b4bef.png)
title: [GH-3412] feat: add opengraph tags to website

triage.summary: Adds OpenGraph meta tags to the Focalboard website so shared links render rich previews in social media and messaging apps. Also appends '| Focalboard' to page titles that don't already include the brand name.
triage.test_hint: Validate OG tags using a tool like opengraph.xyz or the Facebook Sharing Debugger; confirm title suffix logic on pages that already contain 'Focalboard' vs those that do not.

body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing a pull request! Here are a few tips to help you:

1. If this is your first contribution, make sure you've read the Contribution Checklist https://developers.mattermost.com/contribute/getting-started/contribution-checklist/
2. Read our blog post about "Submitting Great PRs" https://developers.mattermost.com/blog/2019-01-24-submitting-great-prs
3. Take a look at other repository specific documentation at https://developers.mattermost.com/contribute
-->

#### Summary
There were three Possible Choices to Implement a Mount for a Config File in Docker:

1) docker run -v:
You could specify a Volume at the Start of a Container, but it has the Problem that the Folder on the Container, gets overwritten by the Host. So the Config File would have needed to be Included Explicitly. Additional, it would have needed to be separated from the Other files, because they would have been deleted otherwise in the Process of mounting. 

2) Docker Volume:
Instead of creating it at runtime, it could be created beforehand, which would solve the Above Issue and would Allow for Sharing of Files from the Container with the Host. But this would complicate the Setup needed even further. Therefore, three was chosen. 

3) Dockerfile Volume:
As the focalboard Database already gets its own Volume Created in the Dockerfile to persist, I added a Volume Creation for the Config File as well in the Dockerfile. This requires less Setup, while still allowing for Easy Access of the Config File.

The Config File Source on the Host System can be found via the following Command:
`docker inspect *Container Name* | grep -B 1 "/opt/focalboard" | grep "Source"`
Be Aware that with a WSL 2 Docker Setup under Windows, the Folder Location differs from the one displayed.

(Note: The Container needs to be Restarted for the Updates to take Place)

#### Ticket Link
Provides Solution too https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/1889
title: Added Docker Config Volume

triage.skip_reason: UI/infrastructure decision requiring maintainer review; PR is stale and involves Docker configuration changes that need deliberate design approval
triage.summary: Adds a Docker VOLUME declaration in the Dockerfile for the Focalboard config file directory, making it easier to persist and access the config file from the host without manual volume mounts at runtime.
triage.test_hint: Build the Docker image, run the container, modify config via the exposed volume source path, restart container, and verify config changes are reflected.

## Summary

At the moment I do see the possibility to archive the entire set of boards I have with `Settings>Export archive`. This gives me an archive of the whole set of boards. 

What if I want to archive just one board or one category that corresponds to a project I completed and so I do not need to see anymore in my Focalboard?


## How important this is to me and why

I put importance `High` because this tool is about getting things done, so once I am done I need to have a clear workflow to get my board out the way (without deleting it)

Use cases:
1. Project is done, need to archive it's board for future reference and don't want to loose the steps, so I do not want to just `Delete Category`

body:
## Summary

Currently database is stored at the /Documents/Focalboard directory in windows, presumably similar in MacOS and Linux. This proposal suggests adding a custom database location.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

I like focalboard as it's a fully portable app, but not being able to have the database in the same dir as the executable makes it more cumbersome to move across devices.

Use cases:
1. Having a focalboard executable and database in a pendrive, so it's fully portable
2. Having a folder with differente databases, being able to tell focalboard at anytime what database to load

## Additional context/similar features

None

Any examples of good implementations of this capability: In the settings menu, add an entry called "Set Database location" that opens a native file explorer dialog and allows to choose an existing .db or a directory where it will be created. This setting is persistent. The focalboard path should be stored in a file next to the executable so focalboard can remain fully portable.
title: Feature Idea: Custom Database location

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX decision required; involves native file dialog integration, config file placement logic, and cross-platform path handling — too broad for automated attempt
triage.summary: Allow users to configure a custom database file/directory location via Settings, storing the path in a config file next to the executable to support fully portable usage (e.g., USB drives).
triage.test_hint: Verify that setting a custom DB path persists across restarts, that Focalboard correctly loads the DB from the new location, and that the config file is written next to the executable on all three platforms (Windows, macOS, Linux).

-
title: Enhancement: Notifications to any change of a card

body:
## Summary

I'm implementing Focalboard across my company, and we created our own templates. We don't plan on ever using the stock/example templates that come with it, so it's quite an inconvenient the fact that all our templates are positioned further down the list and we have to scroll every time we create a new board.

If we were able to either delete/hide standard templates or just rearrange them to be listed further down when compared to our custom ones, UX would be greatly improved.

Furthermore, most of our custom templates strongly inherit from the standard ones, so having the option to edit them as well wouldn't be bad at all.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance:Medium

This is, objectively, a UX improvement that can save an organization considerable time depending on how large their team is.

Use cases:
1. User wants a cleaner template selection UI.
2. User will never use Focalboard's standard templates.
3. User wants to customize standard templates to their own cases/realities.

## Additional context/similar features

Looking very briefly at the code, this functionality [Editing/Deleting] would be very similar to the one already present on custom boards. Reordering, on the other hand, would be a different implementation, but definitely not something complex.
title: Feature Idea: Edit/Reorder/Delete Stock Templates

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX decisions required; involves template management across stock and custom templates with ordering logic
triage.summary: Allow users to edit, reorder, or delete stock/built-in templates so that custom templates can be prioritized in the template selection UI
triage.test_hint: Verify stock templates can be hidden/deleted per workspace, custom templates appear above stock ones, and reordering persists across sessions

body:
Currently, you can only view hidden boards by specifically searching for them. This can make it hard to find a specific board in a list of them hidden. (Side note: documentation on how to find hidden boards is needed.)

A couple of ways of making this easier could be:

1. A toggle in the Settings menu that shows hidden boards
2. A search filter for hidden boards

Then, a ... menu option should be to unhide the board again.
title: Easier way to view hidden boards

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX design decision required; multiple implementation options need product consensus before coding
triage.summary: Add a mechanism to view and unhide hidden boards, either via a Settings toggle or a search filter, plus an 'Unhide' option in the board context menu.
triage.test_hint: Verify hidden boards appear when toggle/filter is enabled, confirm unhide restores board to sidebar, and ensure hidden boards remain hidden without the toggle.

body:
## Summary

Currently I am working on make a custom theme for focalboard, but find that the the theme is changed by color variables in inline styles, this is not friendly to secondary development.

light mode

![截图 2022-08-05 17-34-40](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/34258753/183049574-5e616e5d-73ec-4f4d-bfb6-1ab70d6d5257.png)

when change to night mode

![截图 2022-08-05 17-34-58](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/34258753/183049604-7327438d-6d88-4690-bbf9-61a531b190de.png)

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Convenient for third-party theme development

## Additional context/similar features

I checked the related issue and learned that focalboard does not plan to support the import of custom css files in the short term, so I decided to fork this project, and then make some changes I want to by this "hack" way, but now I find that focalboard does not support custom css files. There is so little support that even css variables are provided inline, which frustrates me.

It's not required, and focalboard will always be an excellent project even without support from 3rd party themes. But it's a good idea for the team to let the project team focus on developing the core functionality and then open up permissions for other interested people to develop the theme, which also benefit more users.

So I hope you can think more carefully about this issue.
title: Feature Idea: Add the switching method of class-level night mode and day mode

triage.summary: Request to switch theme (light/dark mode) implementation from inline CSS custom properties to class-based CSS variables on a root element, enabling easier third-party theme customization via external stylesheets.
triage.test_hint: Verify light/dark mode visuals are unchanged after refactor; confirm CSS variables are applied via body/root class rather than inline styles; test theme switching still works correctly.

## Summary
The text property currently doesn't support text wrapping or multi-line text, so longer text becomes difficult to read. Full text is only visible as a tooltip on hover. To improve the text property, we can consider some of the following options:

1. Text wrapping with an auto-expanding field, so users don't have to keep scrolling to the right and the entire text value is always visible on the card
2. Expand/minimize button, so user can see the full text when they want to without impacting the card layout
3. Auto-expanding to a limit, with button to "Show All" - Combo of option 1 and 2

Open to other suggestions as well!

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Inputting long text values
4. Users who use the `Text` property as a note field

## Additional context/similar features
![Screen Shot 2022-05-26 at 5 41 43 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/93531870/170584795-509b16c1-2ada-45aa-bdb0-fb8cb5555502.png)
title: Feature Idea: Text property auto wrapping/expanding

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX decision required; multiple design options proposed, needs product direction before implementation
triage.summary: Text property in board cards does not wrap or expand for long values; full text only visible on hover tooltip. Request for auto-wrapping, expand/collapse toggle, or hybrid approach.
triage.test_hint: Verify that long text values in the Text property are readable without horizontal scrolling; test expand/collapse behavior if implemented; ensure tooltip still works as fallback.

body:
## Summary

Firstly thank you all for building such an amazing tool.
Our team is now using focalboard as our daily agile kanban. We show this in a pretty big TV and do daily scrum in front of it. However its header and side panel do affect its useful viewport. Only ~70% of the screen is being used.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. See summary.

## Additional context/similar features
We're currently inject a script at our nginx side
```
sub_filter '</body>' '</body><script>$script</script>';
sub_filter_once on;
```
The script is actually a simple JS
```
document.getElementsByClassName("top-head")[0].hidden = true; 
document.getElementsByClassName("Sidebar octo-sidebar")[0].style.display = "none"; 
document.getElementsByClassName("Kanban")[0].style.marginLeft = 0;
```
We understand it's really a hack. Just for your reference.
title: Feature Idea: Show a kanban in fullscreen mode

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX decision required; involves hiding/toggling multiple UI regions (header, sidebar, margins) and needs design input on how fullscreen mode should behave across different view types
triage.summary: Request for a fullscreen/presentation mode that hides the top header and sidebar to maximize usable viewport for kanban boards, useful for team displays and scrum ceremonies.
triage.test_hint: Verify header and sidebar are hidden in fullscreen mode; verify toggling in/out works; verify margin/layout adjusts correctly; test across board view types (kanban, table, gallery).

body:
The `latest` Docker tag is a side-effect of the latest _build_ from `main`, not the latest _release_. We're looking to update it so `latest` is triggered off of _releases_ so it's reasonably more stable. This stemmed from #2592.

The pattern is to have building/tagging/releasing defined in GitLab CI so we'll need to migrate from our config in hub.docker.com. Relevant community thread: https://community-daily.mattermost.com/private-core/pl/bfx4n8dpu3gymybsdm545mpurw
title: Bug: Normalize & migrate Docker tags to GitLab CI

triage.skip_reason: Requires access to GitLab CI config, Docker Hub settings, and internal Mattermost infrastructure; no clear reproducible steps and involves infrastructure/release pipeline decisions
triage.summary: Migrate Docker build/tag/release pipeline from Docker Hub automation to GitLab CI, and fix `latest` tag to reflect the latest release rather than the latest main branch build.
triage.test_hint: Verify that after a release tag is pushed, the `latest` Docker image reflects that release; verify `main` branch builds do not overwrite `latest`.

body:
## Summary

In a chroot webhosting environment it is not reliably possible to listen on an ip, because other users could use the same port. This can be easily solved with a unix socket

Which is the new capability.

focalboard-server optionally listens on a unix socket

## How important is it for me and why?

Importance: Medium

Any examples of good implementations of this capability.
```
{
    "serverRoot": "unix:///tmp/.service/html%2Ffocalboard.sock",
    "port": none,
    "dbtype": "sqlite3",
    "dbconfig": "./focalboard.db",
    "postgres_dbconfig": "dbname=focalboard sslmode=disable",
    "useSSL": false,
    "webpath": "./pack",
    "filespath": "./files",
    "telemetry": false,
    "prometheus_address": ":9092",
    "session_expire_time": 2592000,
    "session_refresh_time": 18000,
    "localOnly": false,
    "enableLocalMode": false,
    "localModeSocketLocation":"/tmp/.service/html%2Ffocalboard-admin.sock"
}
```
title: Feature Idea: launch on unix socket

triage.skip_reason: UI/architecture decision required; no detailed spec for how unix socket interacts with existing port/TLS config, reverse proxy docs, or cross-platform support (Windows)
triage.summary: Allow focalboard-server to optionally listen on a Unix domain socket instead of a TCP port, useful for chroot/shared-hosting environments where port conflicts are a concern.
triage.test_hint: Add integration test that starts server with a unix socket path config, sends an HTTP request over the socket, and verifies a valid response.

body:
## Summary

Currently, the `create_at` and `update_at` columns in various tables are nullable on the database level. Ideally, the columns should never have a null value and should reflect the correct creation/update time.

Following changes are needed to support this-
1. Update `create_at` and `update_at` columns in all focalboard tables (except blocks history table) to be filled by current timestamp wherever the value is null.
2. Add a not-null constraint on `create_at` and `update_at` columns in all focalboard tables (except blocks history table).

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium
title: Add database level defau;t values for create at and update at columns

triage.skip_reason: Requires careful database migration across multiple tables and DB engines (postgres/mysql/sqlite); risk of data loss if nulls exist and migration is not handled correctly
triage.summary: Add NOT NULL constraints and default current timestamp to create_at and update_at columns across all Focalboard tables (except blocks_history), with a backfill migration for any existing NULL values.
triage.test_hint: Write migration tests that verify: nulls are backfilled before constraint is applied, NOT NULL constraint is enforced on insert/update, and rollback leaves schema in prior state. Test against all supported DB engines.

body:
**Summary:**
If I add last updated by to an existing card, I am probably doing it because I want to see when the card was last updated.
However, adding that field causes the last updated date/time to reset to when the property was added.
Having this change not effect last update date/time would allow the user to view when a card was last updated at any point rather than having to have the property already exist.

Importance: Low - Nice to have but not essential


**Additional context / similar features:**

IMO - these fields shouldn't be properties in general, or should be baked in fields somewhere ("view card details in the ... menu?") as well as being properties.  I'd personally prefer they were baked into the card interface because generally these are useful things to know and are related to auditing - not assignment or part of my workflow.
title: Feature Idea: Adding "Last Edited By" Property should not update last edited by field.

triage.skip_reason: Requires backend logic change to distinguish between schema-level property additions and actual content edits when computing last-edited metadata; product/UX decision involved about what constitutes a meaningful edit.
triage.summary: Adding a 'Last Edited By' or 'Last Edited At' property to an existing card should not itself update the last-edited timestamp/user, since users add the property retrospectively to observe existing edit history.

body:
**Summary:**
When running as a plugin, Boards should use the Mattermost language setting if none is currently set and that language translation is available. It should fall-back to English as the default if the language is not available.

**Additional context / similar features:**
For now, the user can choose a language in Boards independent of Mattermost, but Boards should default to the Mattermost language setting for consistency.

Also, to clarify the expected behavior:
* If a user select a language (e.g. Spanish) in Mattermost Channels then switches to Boards for the first time, the interface should be in that language (Spanish).
  * Unless the translation is not available, in which case default to English.
* If the user chooses a different language in Boards (e.g. Indonesian), Boards will display in that language, but this will not affect the Channels setting
  * From this point on, Boards will show in that explicit language setting (Indonesian) regardless of the Mattermost setting
* Channels will always show the language in the Mattermost setting
title: Honor the Mattermost language setting if possible

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX behavior decisions and plugin integration logic require human review
triage.summary: When running as a Mattermost plugin, Boards should default to the Mattermost language setting on first use if that translation is available, falling back to English. Once the user explicitly sets a language in Boards, that preference takes precedence over the Mattermost setting.
triage.test_hint: Verify that on first load as plugin with no Boards language set, the UI language matches the Mattermost user language if available; verify fallback to English for unavailable translations; verify that an explicit Boards language selection persists and overrides the Mattermost setting on subsequent visits.

body:
### Summary
Please allow us to add userscripts and userstyles to the DOM.

### How important this is to me and why:
Importance: Medium

### Use cases:
There are loads of feature requests and bugs in the issue tracker. Obviously there are many great ideas but not enough time to implement them all.

Some apps allow you to use custom userscripts (using tampermonkey, for example) and custom userstyles (using stylish, for example) to mod the interface. I expect that would lead to less feature requests for little UI tweaks and thus allow devs to focus on the important stuff.

For example, I added a [feature request](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/1055) to rename the "NO STATUS" column. I expect many don't care for that, so it may take forever to be implemented, if ever. But if I could use a userscript, I'd just find that node in the DOM and either delete it or rename it - I wouldn't bother asking for it as a formal feature.

### Implementation
The easiest way to implement this is to allow for `custom.js` and `custom.css` files that are appended to the DOM at the very end of the loading process. They could be placed in the same location as `/opt/focalboard/config.json`.

**So if `/opt/focalboard/custom.js` and `/opt/focalboard/custom.css` are found, they're appended to the DOM, else not.**

### Additional context
If this is implemented, we could share our useful js/css in a github gist, until they make their way into the actual code.

Note that custom js/css can be used right now, but only for you - it wouldn't work for focalboard's other users. Hence this feature request.
title: Feature Idea: Custom css and js

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX architectural decision with security implications (injecting arbitrary JS/CSS); requires maintainer approval on approach
triage.summary: Allow server admins to provide custom.js and custom.css files that are served and injected into the Focalboard UI, enabling user-level customization without code changes.
triage.test_hint: Verify files are served only when present, injected at end of DOM load, absent files cause no error, and path traversal/injection attacks are mitigated.

body:
#### Summary

This PR adds persistence for the user settings in the native Linux app. The settings are written to `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/focalboard/settings`. If `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is unset, the app falls back to `$HOME/.config`.

The change hooks into the existing settings export already used in the native macOS app which means the settings are persisted immediately on change.

Unfortunately, the golang webview package uses a custom native binding technology and doesn't allow to define WebKit message handlers. As a result, a dedicated message handler function was added to the existing `NativeApp` object. In the native macOS app, said handler is short-circuited to `window.webkit.messagehandlers.[NAME].postMessage`. This has the benefit that the web app remains agnostic of the particular native binding mechanism.

I have virtually zero experience with Go so while this appears to be working in my testing, the code very likely is an abomination. I'm looking forward to candid feedback. 🙂 

I'd also appreciate some more testing. When running the Linux app on a mac, I _once_ got a blank screen on launch but didn't manage to reproduce again. When testing on Linux, I didn't observe any issues so far.

#### Ticket Link

Relates to https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/314.
title: [GH-314] Persist user settings in native Linux app

triage.skip_reason: UI/native binding decisions, author self-reports low Go experience and requests human review, intermittent blank-screen issue needs investigation, cross-platform desktop code requires manual testing
triage.summary: Adds XDG-compliant persistence of user settings for the native Linux Focalboard app, mirroring existing macOS settings export by wiring a new message handler through the NativeApp object.
triage.test_hint: Launch native Linux app, change user settings, restart app and verify settings are restored; also verify $XDG_CONFIG_HOME fallback to $HOME/.config; regression-test macOS native app settings persistence.

body:
Summary

Enable a mirroring card feature across multiple boards — when a card is updated on one board, all its mirrored instances on other boards automatically sync and reflect the same changes (title, description, comments, attachments, due dates, etc.).

This would allow teams working across different boards to stay aligned without manual duplication or updates.



How important this is to me and why

Importance: High

Keeping cards synchronized across multiple boards is essential for cross-functional collaboration. Currently, duplicating cards and manually updating each one is error-prone and time-consuming. This feature would reduce friction, ensure data consistency, and improve visibility across teams or departments.

Use cases:

Cross-departmental projects: A Product card on the roadmap board is mirrored in the Design and Engineering boards — updates on progress or specs reflect everywhere.

Stakeholder dashboards: A leadership overview board shows key mirrored cards from multiple teams, always up to date.

Templates or shared tasks: Standard operating procedure cards mirrored across projects stay consistent when updated.

Additional context / similar features

Examples of similar implementations:

Notion: Linked database views automatically reflect updates from the original source.

Trello Power-Up “Mirror Cards”: Allows partial mirroring (limited sync capabilities).

ClickUp “Synced Tasks”: Updates to fields sync across mirrored tasks.

Asana “Multi-homing”: One task appears in multiple projects simultaneously.

Future improvements (beyond MVP):

Granular control over what fields are mirrored (e.g., status, due date, or comments).

Sync direction options (one-way vs. two-way).

Visual indicators showing mirrored relationships.

Permissions and visibility control for mirrored cards.
title: Feature Idea: Mirroring Card in different board

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring significant architecture changes across backend and frontend; no concrete specs or acceptance criteria; UI/UX decisions required; cross-board sync introduces complex data consistency and permissions concerns
triage.summary: Feature request to support mirroring cards across multiple boards with automatic bi-directional sync of all card fields, comments, and attachments.

body:
**Summary**
A set of enhancements for the "Company Goals & OKRs" template and card functionality to improve usability and flexibility:

1. Allow users to add and delete main Goal cards in the "Company Goals & OKRs" template.

2. Remove or provide an option to disable the automatic emoji assignment for cards.

3. Add rich text formatting (bold, lists, underline, strikethrough) to the card description field.

### How important this is to me and why
### Importance: High

### Use cases:

1. Dynamic Goal Management: Companies have more than three goals per quarter/year. The current static template is unusable for real-world planning, forcing users to look for alternatives.

2. Professional Appearance and Clarity: Automatically assigned, irrelevant emojis make boards look unprofessional and chaotic. Manually correcting them is a significant waste of time.

3. Effective Communication: Without text formatting (lists, bold), it is impossible to structure information clearly within a card description, leading to messy and hard-to-read notes.

**Additional context/similar features**
1. Goal Management: Any other task management tool (like Trello, Asana, ClickUp) allows users to create and delete items freely. The inability to do so for the main goals in this template is a critical limitation.

2. Emoji Handling: The best practice is to make emojis optional or allow users to set a default one. Forced automation in this case reduces usability.

3. Text Formatting: This is a standard feature in any modern text editor and most productivity apps (Notion, Trello, Asana). The description field currently feels like a plaintext field from the 1990s.
title: Feature Idea: Enhance Company Goals & OKRs template and card formatting

triage.skip_reason: Multiple distinct feature requests bundled together; involves UI/UX decisions on emoji behavior, rich text editor integration, and template mutability. Too broad and requires product/design input.
triage.summary: Three enhancement requests for Company Goals & OKRs template: (1) allow adding/deleting goal cards in template, (2) make automatic emoji assignment optional/disableable, (3) add rich text formatting (bold, lists, underline, strikethrough) to card description field.

body:
## Summary

If you don’t register an account, how can you achieve real-time synchronization and storage of information? Without real-time synchronization, how can you use it for remote collaborative work? If it doesn’t provide real-time network synchronization, there’s a risk that if your computer experiences a sudden system failure one day and doesn’t automatically save, you could lose data, resulting in significant losses. Therefore, having account synchronization functionality and mobile apps is essential. Thank you.

## How important this is to me and why
Real-time synchronization is an essential feature for both individuals and enterprise teams collaborating remotely. Additionally, having mobile apps is equally crucial. 

Those who have the capability should not be restricted to working solely on PCs. For instance, during travel or while on the move, it’s unlikely that one would carry bulky electronic devices like a PC at all times.

Importance: High
title: Feature Idea: Without registering an account, it is impossible to achieve real-time synchronized storage and updates for services like. This lack of security is concerning. I hope that these platforms can also support mobile apps.

triage.skip_reason: Multi-part feature request covering account sync, real-time collaboration, and mobile apps — requires product/UX decisions and significant engineering scope; no concrete specs or repro steps
triage.summary: User requests account-based real-time sync, cloud storage, and mobile app support for Focalboard so data is not lost without an account and remote collaboration is possible on mobile devices.

body:
## Summary

Within a playbook run, when a tag is used in any of :
1. Checklist title
2. Task title
3. Task description

Users will be able to click the tag to see tagged messages in right sidebar

## How important this is to me and why
One of the shortcomings of playbook runs is the inability to trace (both ways) between checklists/tasks and related messages in an associated channel. Enabling tags would be a "low-hanging-fruit" way to allow playbook run participants to jump to messages related to a checklist/task with one click to get up-to-speed on the related discussion.

Why aren't threads enough? 
1. Threads are easily broken if playbook run participants fail to use Reply even once. And reconnecting messages to a thread is difficult. Tags would make it much easier to bring messages under a checklist/task for easy reference.
2. Connecting a thread to a task can occur through a message link in the task description, but it requires creation of a message for the head of the thread that contains some textual reference to the checklist/task for context (e.g. I've used numbering schemes on checklists+tasks to provide reference IDs). Getting to messages associated with the checklist/task is a two-step process: click on thread head message link and then click on message or replies icon to see thread. Using tags allows users to get to messages related to checklist/task in one click, and would not require creation of a thread head message, so checklist/task tags could be set in the playbook itself, or at least before any messages related to the checklist/task are created.

Importance: High

Use cases:
1. Using playbook runs to structure discussions and provide two-way links between messages and related checklists/tasks.
2. 
6.

## Additional context/similar features
* Boeing is using self-hosted Team(?) version 7.5.2, I believe
* I would prefer Mattermost add a more sophisticated way to connect messages to playbook checklists/tasks. E.g. native checklist/task linking support (similar to channels, messages, boards, cards). From a checklist/task, I'd like to be able to "See checklist/task thread", "Reply to task" to add to task-related thread. From a message, I'd like to be about to "Mark outcome/decision" as a way to flag the message as the "outcome/decision" related to a task.

Any examples of good implementations of this capability.
title: Feature Idea: make tags work in playbook checklists

triage.skip_reason: Large cross-product feature requiring coordination between Playbooks and core messaging (hashtag/search sidebar); no technical spec, involves UI decisions and potentially significant backend changes
triage.summary: Allow hashtags in playbook checklist titles, task titles, and task descriptions to be clickable, opening tagged messages in the right sidebar — enabling two-way traceability between checklist tasks and channel discussions.

body:

body:
## Summary

I'm just starting using Mattermost Board (Focalboard in Matterrmost) in one of the companies. I'd like to keep all my workflow with this program, but I see a potential issue for the future:
My workflow is not a single project, but contiguous. I won't close it soon, but I plan to work with it for years. So after some time I got dozens of closed tasks (visible in `Done` column), and after a year there will be hundreds.
I'd like to have a function to archive `Done` tasks, not to include them in current view, but have them in history.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

## Additional context/similar features

So far, a kind of "override" is to create a column (e.g. `Archived`), and hide it.

In other programs, the function is implemented:
- Jira: "close" issue
- Wekan, Kanboard: archive task / card
title: Feature Idea: Possibility to archive done tasks

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring UI design decisions, backend archival logic, and filtering mechanics; not suitable for auto-attempt.
triage.summary: Request to archive completed cards/tasks so they are hidden from the active board view but retained in history, similar to Jira close or Wekan/Kanboard archive functionality.
triage.test_hint: Verify archived cards are hidden from default board/gallery/table views, appear in an archive view or filter, and can be restored. Ensure archival state persists across page reloads and sessions.

triage.summary: Feature request to freeze/pin the first column in table view, similar to Excel's freeze pane functionality, so it remains visible while horizontally scrolling.

Items in Jira cannot be removed, but can be hidden, and found in the future.

body:
## Summary
Sync with Notion.  I really love focalboard, and it's simplicity.  I also love notion and it's note-taking features and database functionality.  They recently introduced database sync for Jira (laughable choice I know) and Github Issues & PR.  It would be amazing if focalboard could sync with Notion's database.   Here's a github example https://github.com/makenotion/notion-sdk-js/tree/main/examples/notion-github-sync.
In my opinion there are no good all in one project mangament platforms (chat, wiki/notes/witeboard, kanban), at best the platforms out there are able to combine two of those (like MM & focalboard), but people are left trying to stitch things together (and is a reason why slack is still so populare despitet zero improvements in years).  While MM is the best chat plattform IMO, focalboard isn't as fully featured as others, and the platform as a whole lacks any good capability/integrations with note-taking/md/wiki tools.
As Notion only has those two integrations for db sync right now, I think it would help spread awareness to users about this project and help people see the light (most people I've spoke to love MM, but are unaware about focalboard).

What the new capability is.
Sync Notion database with focalboard

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: High
I really want to use focalboard, as I love Mattermost and the project as a whole- but it just doesn't have enough features & integratitons compared to the alternatives.  This would change that, focalboard would be tigtly integrated with our chat, and make it easier to keep projects updated.

Use cases:
1. Add extra info/attach documents in Notion
2. Keep projects synced, and your team far more organized
3. Allow (some) users to stay inside mattermost while still staying on the same page

## Additional context/similar features

Any examples of good implementations of this capability.
You can try it at notion.so with github, but here's the code: https://github.com/makenotion/notion-sdk-js/tree/main/examples/notion-github-sync

My major concern is how conflicts happen, both on focalboard's end and notion, considering they integrated it with jira I'm tempted to believe it's possible though.
title: Feature Idea: Sync Boards with Notion

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring external API integration with Notion; involves significant architectural decisions, authentication flows, and ongoing maintenance of third-party sync logic. No concrete spec or design provided.
triage.summary: Feature request to implement two-way sync between Focalboard boards/databases and Notion databases, similar to Notion's existing GitHub and Jira integrations.

body:
## Summary
Board templates are only supported in English currently. If users switch their language settings to a non-English language, the standard templates will always appear in English regardless of the language selected. Board users rely on standard templates to help them learn about the product and to get started quickly. 

For non-native English speakers looking to use Boards, this is problematic because it prevents them from learning and adopting the tool in the language they are most comfortable with. The mixed languages also creates a poor and inconsistent experience while using Boards.

#### What the new capability is:
When a non-English language is selected in the settings menu, display the translated standard templates instead of the English templates. All content on the standard templates should be translated, including:

- Board/template name
- Board description
- View names
- All sample card content


## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Non-native English speakers looking to learn more about Boards using the Welcome template
2. Non-native English speakers creating new boards from any of the standard templates

## Additional context/similar features

See [thread](https://community.mattermost.com/core/pl/1bhz6ufmu7be5kkxgqp7kbjzsw) for reference.
title: Feature Idea: Translated standard Boards templates

triage.skip_reason: Large scope requiring translation of all template content (names, descriptions, views, card content) across multiple languages plus backend/frontend coordination; not suitable for automated attempt
triage.summary: Board standard templates are hardcoded in English and do not respect the user's language setting. All template content (board/template name, description, view names, sample card content) should be localized and displayed in the user's selected language.
triage.test_hint: Switch UI language to a non-English locale, open the template picker, and verify all standard template content (names, descriptions, view names, card content) is rendered in the selected language rather than English.

body:
Hello!

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Team Edition + Mattermost Boards (plugin)
 - Version: 7.1.1 + 7.1.0

## Description

We are considering Mattermost with Mattermost Boards Plugin as a solution in our company. The main concern is the email notifications for any actions in cards and due dates. Right now all the communication between Boards and Mattermost (and end user) are based on the messages passed through Boards bot.

If you check all the messages in the direct message history it looks like a flow of separate notifications. It doesn't look like threads or something to group or separate differend boards and cards messages from the complete bot conversation feed.

Also you will nevere get message in email inbox until "you’ve been offline for more than 60 seconds or away from Mattermost for more than five minutes". You cannot control this setting.

The special case is due dates - there is no notifications at all.

It is very important for us while you working on several separate projects at the same time and get a lot of notifications be able to track your tasks events without distracting from your work process. When you get all the info into your GMail inbox it much more easier (also you can search in your inbox to track taks history, for example).

What we expect and why it is important:
1. Be able to control email notifications in Mattermost and especially Mattermost Boards plugin and be able to set it "always" (without rules of away/offline for a certain amount of time). It is important that the end users will be notified guaranteed and know that every notification can be found in their inbox.
2. Receive notification of the due date. Also it will be good to customize notifications i.e. get notification in 1 day or somethig before due date will arrive. Also it can be applied to start date (in case of start date->end date time interval set).

## Additional questions
1. Do you have that functionality in your roadmap?
2. Also how it can be customized on the our end - for example custom bot or something?
title: Feature Idea: email notifications

triage.skip_reason: Large feature request requiring significant backend and frontend work including email notification infrastructure, due date scheduling, and user preference controls. No clear spec and involves UI/UX decisions.
triage.summary: Feature request to add email notifications for card actions and due dates in Mattermost Boards, including configurable notification timing (e.g., 1 day before due date) and always-on email delivery independent of user online/offline status.

title: Feature Idea: Bulk actions on Boards sidebar

triage.summary: Add bulk actions (move/hide/delete) for multiple boards or categories in the Boards sidebar to help users manage large numbers of boards after v7.2 migration.
