body:
## Summary

It would be really cool to translate any new or edited issue into focalboard cards. In this way one might have a single board that holds both, the code issues from gitlab, and the tasks discussed and distributed across members from the related Mattermost Channel.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: High

Use cases:
1. A project requires the development of code (e.g., a data analysis in R). This code is hosted on GitLab and an MM channel is linked to that project. Code related issues are generated directly at GitLab or in the IDE that is used for code development. However, the discussion of additional tasks that are not directly linked to the code (e.g., brainstormings, tasks to retrieve additional data) takes place at MM. A focal board at MM that automatically shows the GitLab issues as well as the MM tasks as cards would be great to have the big picture at hand.
2. The same can happen for Manuscript writing. The LaTeX sources are hosted at GitLab, writing issues are distributed as issues among authors, while discussions on what to include in the text, which journal to select, will take place at MM and may also end up in tasks. Having all GitLab issues and assigned MM tasks in one board would make the whole writing process really transparent.

## Additional context/similar features

I did not came across some so far.
title: Translate GitLab issues into FocalBoard cards

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring GitLab API integration, webhook handling, bidirectional sync design decisions, and significant backend/frontend work. No concrete specs or acceptance criteria provided.
triage.summary: Feature request to sync GitLab issues as FocalBoard cards, enabling a unified board view combining GitLab issues and Mattermost tasks.

body:
## Summary

Ability to hide certain properties on a per-card level, so users can hide properties not relevant to the specific card. Properties are additive on a board, and if certain properties are needed for a select few cards, they get applied to all cards on the board, but users may not want to see all the properties on every card. Deleting a property on one card also deletes it for all cards.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Multi-purpose board where many properties are available, but not all of them are relevant or needed for every card on the board.

## Additional context/similar features
See this [thread](https://community.mattermost.com/private-core/pl/7hzunk4kuprhpq6cappejnje7o) from @michaelgamble for more details:

> Also completely different topic, I've been thinking about my use of boards / cards - one feature I'm thinking i would like is the ability to define different card types that basically define different sets of properties for each card type... 
> 
> The reason I'm thinking about this is I have a board right now where I'm tracking:
> 
> - Tasks
> - Stories
> - Bugs
> - Component Releases
> - Text Font Releases
> - Icon Releases
> 
> Each of these use cases have a slightly different set of requirements in terms of data fields as properties for example "Icon Releases" would have a URL for GitHub PR - but many of the other ones don't....  IF i want to see them in the same board, I'm forced to keep adding more and more and more properties to a card in order to manage the different types and its very quickly feeling like it could grow out of hand... 
> 
> I'm not sure what the solution is but in quickly thinking about it, makes me think the solution might lay in the "having a card on multiple boards" explorations you guys are approaching.. the only problem though is i'm pretty sure the requirement you've currently set out is that they all need to have all properties additively matching...
> 
> The reason I'm bringing this up here is maybe there is a case for not adding all the property fields, but some other in-between solution maybe (have not put a lot of thought into this)... perhaps identifying which fields will be common on the board that has cards from multiple sources??
> 
> Anyways food for though.. just wanted to share... oh and also i was thinking of the parallel of jira and issue types while visualizing this
title: Feature Idea: Hide properties on cards/card templates

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring significant UI/UX decisions and backend schema changes for per-card property visibility
triage.summary: Allow users to hide specific properties on individual cards without affecting other cards on the board, supporting multi-purpose boards with heterogeneous card types.

body:
## Summary

I found we've been using other tools for these retrospective / quadrant view, and realized that this would be a perfect use case for boards.. if each quadrant could be like the current kanban, where you can add items, order them, and drag and drop them into quadrants.. board cards would be perfect.

The things that makes boards more powerful then other tools in my opinion is four fold:

1. being able to have a continued conversation on a card
2. being able to create multiple views for taking action on items as outcomes of the retrospective
3. being able to have integration for the sake of cross posting
4. the power of being able to replicate the card into other boards (coming in that future feature)

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: High/Medium/Low

Use cases:
1. Retrospective quadrants
2. market fit quadrants
3. magic quadrants
4. the list goes on and on...

## Additional context/similar features

Right now we use figjam for retrospectives, but the usefulness of it ends because the follow up needs to happen outside of that ecosystem.. in our case inside the MM ecosystem.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/31866506/170327533-13408569-ac22-4590-9cf2-4dcbf8528bcd.png)

body:
## Summary

- Allow setting zoom level as we can do currently with CTRL + Mouse Scroll buttons.

- I think adding zoom level adjustment will increase the productivity and visibility of boards. 

- As it occurred to me when we have more columns in board we have to scroll on the right side to see the entire board but instead if zoom level is present then it will improve visibility of entire board properly. 

- My suggestion is if this feature is going to be implemented then it should be placed around top right corner near feedback placeholder.

- Zoom level should be from 10% - 100% [or maximum zoom can be more than 100%].

- It should also retain zoom level after application is closed.

- Currently we can do this adjustment via CTRL + Mouse Scroll buttons, but unfortunately this gets reset on restart.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:

1. When more columns are present in board. In my case I have (5 columns) very low, low, medium, high, super high priorities in tasks and 8 columns in task status [backlog, in development, analysis, in progress, testing, finished, not finished, parked]. Viewing by priority or status requires horizontal scrolling.

2. When working on generally popular screen resolution of 1366 X 768 with above conditions.

3. When we need entire board to be visible without horizontally scrolling for more fields.

Above mentioned some of the use cases can be fulfilled by adding zoom level adjustment feature.
title: Feature Idea: Adding Application Zoom Level Adjustment

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX design decision required, large scope involving persistent zoom state across frontend and desktop, no clear technical spec
triage.summary: Allow users to set and persist a board zoom level via a UI control (top-right area), ranging from 10%-100%+, as an alternative to the transient CTRL+scroll zoom that resets on restart.

body:
## Summary

Currently, the only attachments that can be uploaded to a card are image files. These images are embedded into the card description, but cannot be enlarged, downloaded, or edited. Other file types such as PDF's and videos are not supported either.

Cards would be more functional if they supported:

1. Opening/enlarging attached images
2. [Toggling through and previewing multiple attached images](https://community.mattermost.com/core/pl/98e7c9khmtnyxfcjupeo7jnciy)
3. [Pasting images/screenshots onto cards](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/262)
4. Editing attached images
5. [Uploading generic file types such as PDFs and video files](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/111)
6. Downloading attached files
7. Directory view of all attached files on the board or across the team
8. Uploading/downloading multiple files at the same time

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Viewing or downloading images attached to the card
9. Attaching documents or other files as a reference for the card
10. Editing/cropping images for gallery view
title: Feature Idea: Card Attachment Improvements

triage.skip_reason: Large feature request with multiple sub-features (image preview, multi-file support, PDF/video, editing, directory view); requires UI decisions and significant backend/frontend work.
triage.summary: Enhance card attachments to support image enlargement/preview, toggling through multiple images, paste-to-upload, image editing, generic file types (PDF, video), file download, directory view, and bulk upload/download.

body:
Currently is seems users can only subscribe to individual cards. This is nice, but not at all a viable solution for large boards and/or large teams where people need or want to be notified about all changes. For example I just had 3 people join a board that has 181 cards. In order to get notifications they would need to click on 543 cards and follow them individually. That's a minimum of 1629 clicks and a lot of scrolling. When they are done they still won't be notified of new cards and will need to find and follow each as they get added. Every time a new team member joins they have to go through the whole thing.

I suggest a mechanism to follow a whole board instead of only following on a per-card basis.

Somewhat related, issue #563 suggests a notification channel per board.
title: Feature Idea: Follow boards instead of just cards

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring backend subscription model changes, frontend UI decisions, and notification system redesign
triage.summary: Allow users to follow/subscribe to an entire board to receive notifications for all card changes and new card additions, instead of requiring per-card subscriptions.

body:
## Summary

It would be lovely to have the ability to _import_ redmine issues as focal board cards.
Perhaps based on projects/categories (or a custom field)

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

At our organisation, we use mattermost for all our internal comms and Redmine for project management. We have just begun to integrate focalboard (or rather mattermost boards) into our workflows. This integration would vastly expand the use-cases of focal board for us.

Use cases:
1.  For teams looking to move their project management from redmine to focal board
2.  For teams needing a constant flow from different redmine projects to different focalboard/mattermost boards
3. The work on such a plugin will easily lend itself to adaptations to other integrations -- a mattermost command, a teello API script, etc...

## Additional context/similar features

The [mattermost plugins for redmine](https://github.com/altsol/redmine_mattermost) has all the foundations for extracting information from the Redmine API and importing into the Mattermost chat, it can probably re-used and modified to integrate with Boards.
title: Feature Idea: Webhook/Integration with Redmine

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring design decisions around integration architecture, external API dependencies, and unclear scope; no concrete spec or repro steps.
triage.summary: Feature request to import Redmine issues as Focalboard cards, supporting project/category-based mapping and ongoing sync between Redmine and Focalboard/Mattermost Boards.

body:
## Summary

It's related to dates but not quite to [the calendar view](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/338).

I just stumbled over [Timestripe](https://timestripe.com/) which is basically a Kanban board but each of the columns is grouping cards by a certain time.
For instance like this:

![grafik](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/372620/154719045-ac0acc26-eefa-466f-bb2a-6713c727ca2d.png)

Or an overview per week:

![grafik](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/372620/154719734-a270491d-6d93-4aa1-acaa-d5be6f768201.png)

Or see Trello's take on this:

![Calendar_2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/372620/165177679-8f53145b-461a-423a-9ea9-9cac6e9ecf96.jpg)

![Calendar_3](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/372620/165177789-5d9ec1be-ae2b-436b-a73e-0da75e2f78b4.jpg)

In my opinion Focalboard is able to do even more than Timestripe in general but the dynamic grouping gives a different user experience for a Kanban board than the current calendar view.

Maybe this is even possible somehow and I'm just missing the bit how to configure it properly.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: it's just a UX improvement (but obviously so good, that [Timestripe](https://timestripe.com/) made it their business model) and [Trello made it into a popular extension](https://trello.com/power-ups/55a5d917446f517774210011/calendar-power-up).

Use cases:
1. A good time based overview

## Additional context/similar features

The calendar view is somehow similar in regards of having a timely overview
title: Feature Idea: automatic time based grouping views (calendar / date related view)

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring significant UI/UX design decisions and complex frontend/backend work for time-based dynamic grouping
triage.summary: Add automatic time-based grouping views (calendar/kanban hybrid) that group cards by date periods (day/week/month) similar to Timestripe and Trello Calendar Power-Up

body:
## Summary

The current `Date` property type works great for specifying specific dates or date ranges but it doesn't allow me to capture the more generic concept of duration. I currently am using the `Number` property type with a label that specifies a fixed unit of time but this is not very clean when durations are too large or small and it also does not display the units of time in every view which make it hard to interpret (in the calendar for instance)

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. In order to track how long you expect a given issue to take, it would be useful to have a property type that understood the concept of time, allowed for better interfaces for setting durations and showed units of time in all views

For example - issues with expected work hours of 100 or 0.25 - depending on the view, I might prefer to see `4 day, 4 hours` instead of 100 hours and `15 minutes` instead of 0.25 hours. Similarly - adding some friendly input support would be helpful to auto convert between units (all of these should be accepted and mean the same : 720m, 12h,  0.5d).
title: Feature Idea: Consider adding a Duration Property Type

triage.skip_reason: New property type requiring UI design decisions, unit conversion logic, input parsing, and multi-view rendering changes — too large and involves UX decisions for auto-attempt.
triage.summary: Request to add a Duration property type that supports human-friendly input (e.g. 720m, 12h, 0.5d) and displays formatted duration (e.g. '4 days 4 hours') across all board views including calendar.
triage.test_hint: Verify duration parsing accepts equivalent inputs (720m, 12h, 0.5d), correct storage and display in table/gallery/calendar views, and edge cases like 0 and very large/small values.

body:
## Summary

Currently, I can view each single board within each single channel. And within a card, I can have a property to assign a card to somebody.

I would like to see all the cards assigned to me, within 1 single view.

## How important this is to me and why
We use cards to manage todos. This is more efficient to have 1 single view, compared to looking into each single board.

Use cases:
1. We have 2 channels: _Project_ and _Kitchen_
2. In _Project_, 2 cards (TaskA and TaskB) are assigned to me, within the property _Who_
3. In Kitchen, 1 card (TaskC) is assigned to me, within the property _Who_
4. I would love to have a view showing TaskA, TaskB and TaskC together. A simple table would be a great start. And a calendar view would be even better (not a priority though)

## Additional context/similar features
Trello does something similar, as far as I understand: https://help.trello.com/article/819-viewing-all-of-your-cards
title: Feature Idea: View all my cards from all boards

triage.skip_reason: Large cross-board aggregation feature requiring significant backend query changes, new frontend view, and UX design decisions
triage.summary: Add a global 'My Cards' view that aggregates cards assigned to the current user across all boards/channels, similar to Trello's card overview.

body:
Nice work Boards team on the v0.9.1 enhancements! Posting here to share a few thoughts on further improvements you may or may not have already considered.

1. Welcome to Boards screen seems like it could be scaled up quite significantly

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14333569/135539472-f305dc5f-d48d-4655-a0e0-0877b8d7a455.png)

2. With no CTA button on the empty screen for a workspace I kind of feel lost with what to do and where I am when I open the board for an empty channel. I'd propose just to duplicate the "+ Add Board" button as a CTA on the empty screen and maybe add an illustration to make it feel friendly and more inviting with a clear action to take (see example proposal). Especially on big monitors the add boards button is reeeally out of the way (see pic). 

Example of empty state with large screen:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14333569/135539919-9a420bbb-c40b-4769-b948-3ee187ac0a37.png)

Proposal (rough wireframe):
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14333569/135540100-ff628aca-3819-46c7-955e-1fdc99f48f8a.png)

3. If boards do exist in a channel it would REALLY improve UX if we just opened the first board in the sidebar rather than showed a white screen with instructions to select a board

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14333569/135539877-329ffae1-4cd5-4c16-92c8-259aadb1d813.png)

4. Not sure what the effort involved, but sorting the boards in the dashboard and switcher by recently viewed would make both these features significantly more useful. Typing to search is nice, but if I can just see what I'm looking for at a glance it would be better. I bet it's unlikely that users would be actively working in more than a few boards, so surfacing the most active ones would be a big UX win

5. The dashboard view doesn't seem to scroll if there's a lot of channels with boards in them. If the dashboard instead became a "Recently viewed" list with a max number (ie 10), you might not have to support scrolling at all. I know there are some more improvements to the dashboard coming, so just sharing some ideas. 

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14333569/135540874-3f103b41-a825-4559-8aeb-afbe354df08d.png)

6. I was confused to see the dashboard view had tiles for channels with 0 boards in them. Should we just hide those tiles?

7. I found the reference to "change channels" near the switcher and "All Channels" in the dashboard to be confusing. I guess we are purposely trying to make the connection between boards and channels by doing this.

8. Tiles in the dashboard view did not have an elevation or hover effect
title: FTUE boards UX enhancements

triage.skip_reason: Multiple UI/UX design decisions with wireframes and no single clear spec; requires design review and significant frontend work across several distinct areas
triage.summary: FTUE (First Time User Experience) enhancements: scale up welcome screen, add CTA on empty workspace state, auto-open first board when boards exist, sort boards by recently viewed in dashboard/switcher, and fix dashboard scroll with overflow boards.

body:
**Summary:**
I'd like to have an overview of all my projects on a single board. Then I'd like to be able to click from that board directly to another board for each project. This would be possible in the web based version using simple URLs, but not in the desktop version.

In the spirit of mattermost/mattermost-server#23273 it would be cool to have a property type which contains a link to another board. Both as a choice (so I can choose one of my existing boards as a dropdown value) and as a "link" which allows me to click to that board.

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: Low

Use cases:
1. Maintaining an overview board that shows an overview of individual projects, each which have their own board
2. Being able to take 1 list of tasks and break it out into a list of boards (so each task gets a whole board)
3. Maintaining a list of persons assigned to boards by having a meta board that shows the person and the board per card
4. Adding metadata to boards by having a single overview board that tracks boards and extra metadata
5. Allowing for custom "navigation" scenarios, where users can be sent to a single board to start with and from there find their way to other boards

**Additional context / similar features:**
Not sure if this is implemented elsewhere or not.
title: Feature Idea: Property type that is a link to another board

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring new property type, UI for board picker dropdown, navigation/linking logic across web and desktop, and cross-board data model changes — too broad for automated attempt.
triage.summary: Add a new card property type that links to another board, rendered as a dropdown picker of existing boards and a clickable link navigating to that board (both web and desktop).
triage.test_hint: Verify board-link property can be created, a target board selected from dropdown, the link navigates correctly in web and desktop, and the property value persists after reload.

body:
**Summary:**
The plugin for the Mattermost app currently opens as a "separate window" to mattermost, however this is not selectable from the windows taskbar. Instead, you have to open the mattermost application and then the focalboard window pops up on top.

**How important this is to me and why:**
If I've got a project that I'm working on, it would be nice to have the board for that project available all the time, so that I can make focus directly on that board instead of having to open mattermost, then focus on the board.

It would also be useful to be able to switch between channels within the focalboard window.


Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. User has a project that they want to focus on without being distracted by the mattermost window in the background
2. User wants to go directly to the focalboard project instead of opening Mattermost, then focalboard.
3. User does not want to have focalboard project appear in the way of their mattermost window when they return to mattermost.

**Additional context / similar features:**
good implementation / workaround of this capability: Using mattermost from a browser. You can open the focalboard plugin in a separate tab, put it in to a separate window, etc.
title: Feature Idea: Enable plugin as a separate window to Mattermost

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX and desktop integration decision requiring Mattermost desktop app architecture changes; no clear technical spec and involves cross-team coordination

body:
**Summary:**
Each card can have an auto incrementing number similar to issues in Github/Jira in order to better reference cards when developing software (in Commits or MRs/PRs)

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Software development
2. Talking about/referencing issues within Mattermost or in Meetings

**Additional context / similar features:**
- Jira does this with adding project ID and a auto-incrementing number before each ticket
 Example: `https://jira.example.com/browse/OWSWS-422` -> ID = `OWSWS-422`
- Trello has this at the beginning of the unique identifier in the URL
 Example: `https://trello.com/c/[boardid]/5893-weddingvideo-n2` -> ID = `5893`
title: Feature Idea: Show ID/Number on each card

triage.skip_reason: Requires schema changes for auto-incrementing card IDs, UI updates to display IDs on cards, and careful design decisions around ID format/scope (per-board vs global). Too many product/design decisions for auto-attempt.
triage.summary: Add auto-incrementing numeric IDs to cards (similar to GitHub/Jira) so users can reference cards in commits, PRs, and conversations.
triage.test_hint: Verify new cards receive sequential IDs per board, IDs are displayed on card front and detail view, IDs persist after card moves/renames, and existing cards receive backfilled IDs on migration.

body:
**Summary:**

The mac app currently bundles a server and starts it on launch. It'd be great, if I could also connect the app to an external server. I know I could simply use the browser to connect but the native app is nicely separated from my myriad of browser tabs which makes it a lot easier to switch back and forth between Focalboard and other apps.

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: Low

Use cases:

1. You run a Focalboard server at home and want to connect to it from different devices, one of them being a mac
2. You run a Focalboard server at work and want to connect to it with a native(-ish) app that doesn't live inside a browser tab

**Additional context / similar features:**

I've had this same use case for Jira for years. Unfortunately, when they finally made a native mac app, it turned out to not have the same set of features that the website had. The Focalboard native app being an exact copy of the web UI is a huge plus for me.

I believe this would also be a prerequisite for generalizing the mac app to run on iOS as well (#160).

body:
**Summary:**
1. Write a message on Mattermost, for example: `/card @tom Buy cookies`
2. This creates a card **Buy cookies** in Focalboard, assigned to **Tom**

This could be improved in the future with the following ideas:

3. Define a specific syntax to include additional information (content, date, board, status, priority, project, ...). For example `/card @tom Buy cookies [2 chocolate + 1 nuts]` would add the content **2 chocolate + 1 nuts** to the card
4. A message is automatically created on Mattermost, in reply to the original message (in the same thread), providing the link to the card on Focalboard
5. If no project/category is specified, it could be automatically flagged as "to be sorted"

As far as I understand:

6. I'm not sure how all this is already the project of https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/discussions/349
7. This feature would be the mirror feature of https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/282.

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: High

Use cases:
1. We use Mattermost extensively to manage todos. But the current plugin is not advanced enough and not transparent enough (not everybody can see all the pending tasks)
2. We discuss a topic on Mattermost, and a todo is decided: the follow-up is currently not easy, while Focalboard could provide that

**Additional context / similar features:**
With Asana, sending an email with a specific syntax creates a card/todo in Asana.
title: Feature Idea: create card/task from Mattermost

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring Mattermost slash command integration, backend card creation API, assignment resolution, and cross-system linking; needs design decisions on syntax, board targeting, and permissions
triage.summary: Add a Mattermost slash command (e.g. `/card @user Title [content]`) that creates a Focalboard card, assigns it to the mentioned user, and optionally replies with a link to the created card.
triage.test_hint: Integration test: POST slash command payload to plugin endpoint, assert card is created via Focalboard API with correct title, assignee, and content; verify reply message contains card URL.

body:
**Summary:**
Add the possibility to embed web content, e.g. with iframes.

**How important this is to me and why:**
Medium

Use cases:
1. Makes it possible to still use other services or applications, without the need to integrate via API's or similar.

**Additional context / similar features:**
Similar to how Notion handles embedding of content.
title: Feature Idea: Embed web content

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring UI decisions, security review for iframe embedding, and no concrete spec
triage.summary: Add ability to embed web content (iframes) in cards, similar to Notion's embed blocks

body:
**Summary:**
Agregated cross table which can display information from some or all other tables

**How important this is to me and why:**
I have a growing list of project tasks board, and I have to go for each projects in order to list my high priority tasks and pick the one I should prioritize instead of having a global overview of every pending tasks.

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. I create a agregated view
2. I pick the boards and I'd like to agregate
3. I can search, sort and filter card of this agregated view.

**Additional context / similar features:**
Notion has a kind of cross board feature
title: Feature Idea: Cross table

triage.skip_reason: Large cross-table aggregation feature requiring significant architectural design across frontend and backend; no clear implementation spec
triage.summary: Add aggregated cross-table view that pulls cards from multiple boards, supporting search, sort, and filter across all selected boards (similar to Notion cross-board feature).

body:
**Summary:**
Automation engine for performing tasks based on actions/events inside Focalboard.

**How important this is to me and why:**
Medium to High

Use cases:
1. Trigger other web services based on actions/events inside Focalboard.
2. Easily automate workflows/processes involving other API enabled applications.

**Additional context / similar features:**
I see that as a first version of this, just having the possibility to send web requests for triggering other services would be enough.
title: Feature Idea: Automation engine

triage.skip_reason: Large open-ended feature requiring significant architecture design, UI decisions, and backend engineering for an automation/webhook engine.
triage.summary: Add an automation engine that triggers configurable actions (e.g. outbound webhooks/web requests) based on events and actions occurring within Focalboard boards.

body:
**Summary:**
Add a theme editor, where one could define whatever colors they would like for different elements.

When creating new themes there should already be defined usage for different colors, so all different elements uses e.g. "Primary color", "Secondary color" etc. However, if the user would want to override the color for a specific element, it should be possible to do so as well.


**How important this is to me and why:**
Low

Absolutely not essential.
Though, with that said, the first thing I thought when switching to the dark theme was, "Hmm, I wonder if I can easily change the colors". The reason simply being that the colors does not match my perception of a dark theme, but this is of course just personal preference (but therefore the feature request).

Use cases:
1. Create your own themes.
2. Possibility to easily share themes with other users via import/export functionality.

**Additional context / similar features:**
I am not familiar with how the current themes have been implemented, but I assume there is a `.css` file or similar for each theme, so this theme editor would just create additional of these theme files.

For the import/export functionality I am thinking of just having that being a simple `.json` file, having it easily shareable with others and only text. There could even be a text input where one could paste the entire JSON string to import a theme, instead of having to download a file and browse for it.
title: Feature Idea: Theme editor

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring significant UI design decisions, CSS architecture changes, and import/export functionality. No concrete specs or mockups provided.
triage.summary: Add a theme editor allowing users to customize colors for UI elements, create/save custom themes, and import/export themes as JSON.

body:
**Summary:**
Sync contents between application instances.

Maybe this is solved with the server edition, but I understand the server edition more about being able to create multiple users and such. My use case is more along the lines of being a single user, so still only personal use, but being able to use the same database instance on both my desktop computer, multiple laptops etc.

**How important this is to me and why:**
High. For me, this is absolutely essential for this type of application.

Use cases:
1. Being able to switch between computers.
2. Pave the way for mobile apps as requested by issue #160.

**Additional context / similar features:**
I see this could be implemented in a couple of different ways.
- Perhaps storing the `focalboard.db` and `files` folder, as mentioned [here for backups](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/253#issuecomment-822765544), in a synced folder, e.g. Dropbox, OneDrive, Google Drive etc. would be enough?
If so, then simply adding an option in the settings for specifying the location of these files would be enough.
- If the above would not be reliable, e.g. conflicts or locks of having the database open in multiple instances at once, then some check for changes would need to be implemented.
However, if going this route I guess just using the server edition would be the better choice, but that would of course require a server to be always on.
title: Feature Idea: Sync contents between application instances

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring architectural decisions about sync strategy, conflict resolution, and multi-instance coordination; no clear spec or acceptance criteria
triage.summary: Single-user multi-device sync: allow personal edition to share the same database/files across multiple desktop instances via configurable storage location or lightweight sync mechanism.

body:
**Summary:**
Lookup column/data type to relate cards/items in different boards.
This lookup column should be clickable to open up the card in the other board directly.
There should also be the possibility to add additional columns from the other board to the board with the lookup column.


**How important this is to me and why:**
Medium

Use cases:
1. Ability to have a board with e.g. Clients where each card contains lots of client information. Then have a projects board where the client can be specified in a lookup column, with an easy link back to the card in the Client board.
2. Ability to add additional cards from e.g. the Clients board (e.g. Client contact person) to the projects board, therefore eliminating the need for having to open the entire Client card.

**Additional context / similar features:**
Similar to lookup columns in SharePoint.

This might be loosely related to issue mattermost/mattermost-server#23273 but when reading that issue I felt these two warranted their own issues.
title: Property type: Lookup

triage.skip_reason: Large cross-board relational feature requiring significant schema design, backend changes, and UI decisions; no clear spec or acceptance criteria.
triage.summary: Add a 'Lookup' property type that links cards across boards, displays related card data inline, and allows navigation to the linked card.

body:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/27766499/116107723-90956a00-a6b3-11eb-8720-ecf0f6535ddc.png)

triage.skip_reason: UI layout redesign requiring significant frontend architecture changes and design decisions
triage.summary: Add multi-column layout support for blocks within a card, similar to Notion's column blocks feature

body:
It would be very useful to be able to set a WIP limit (work in progress limit), that enables us to set a limit of how many items can be in one "column" (probably most useful for "doing column", but also for other colums).

This is important since it is one if the central ideas of Kanban board.
See [here](Kanban_boards):
> As described in books on Kanban for software development,[4][3] the two primary practices of Kanban are to visualize your work and limit work in progress (WIP).

Importance: High

Use cases:
1. Managing (mental) workload
2. Helps keep task list [SMART](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SMART_criteria) 
3. If used in physical production, helps minimize resources and therefore capital kept "in production"
title: Feature idea: WIP limits

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring UI design decisions, data model changes, and no concrete specification of behavior (warnings vs hard limits, per-user vs board-level, etc.)
triage.summary: Add WIP (Work In Progress) limits to Kanban board columns, allowing users to set a maximum number of cards per column — a core Kanban methodology concept.
triage.test_hint: Verify limit can be set per column, that exceeding the limit triggers a visible warning or block, and that limit persists across sessions.

body:
**Summary:**

I have a number of recurring "chore" tasks that I have to take care of each day. Every morning, I move them from my "Done" group to my "To do" group. It would be nice if there was an option to configure a card to automatically move into a specific group at a given date, ideally with the option to make it recurring.

![Screenshot 2021-04-21 at 08 06 11](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1137962/115504642-8bca5380-a278-11eb-8ab3-6d08b64f67c4.png)

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: Low

Low importance because this might be a very specialized use case.

Use cases:

1. I have a recurring task that repeats every day, like reading emails in the morning
2. I have a recurring task that repeats every week, like writing release notes
title: Feature Idea: Cycling cards / scheduled card transitions / recurring tasks

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring scheduler logic, recurring task engine, and UI decisions for recurrence configuration; no clear spec beyond high-level description
triage.summary: Add support for recurring/scheduled card transitions — automatically move a card to a specified group/column on a recurring schedule (daily, weekly, etc.) to support repeating chore tasks.

body:

Today when I add a checkbox it seems like I can just add one item. To add multiple check boxes one thus needs to add multiple content boxes. This might be a bug on my system, or it's just not fully implemented.

It would be nice with a multilevel multiple checkbox style content box. 

Example

Packing list:

- [ ] Clothes
   - [ ] Jacket
   - [ ] T shirts
   - [ ] underwear
   - [ ] Hoodie
- [ ] Toiletries  
   - [ ] Toothbrush
   - [ ] Toothpaste
   - [ ] Plackers
- [ ] Electronics
   - [ ] Computer
   - [ ] phone charger
   - [ ] computer charger
   - [ ] camera

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: Medium
Really useful feature

Use cases:
1. Make quick checklists in cards sorted into neat groups
title: Feature Idea: Checkbox content type multiple checkboxes

triage.skip_reason: UI/UX design decisions required for nested checkbox hierarchy; no clear spec for data model changes needed to support multi-level checkbox content blocks
triage.summary: Request for a multi-level nested checkbox content type within cards, supporting hierarchical checklist items (e.g., packing list with sub-categories). Currently each checkbox is a separate content block with no nesting or grouping support.

body:
**Summary:**
Keep track of deleted content.

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. It's easy to mis-click sometimes, and once a card is deleted, it would be good to be able to restore it.
2. Currently there's no backup (#253) so there's no way to restore from there
3. Currently exporting is broken on the personal edition (#248) so that's one less option for the previous point (I guess that leaves making frequent copies of the .db file and digging in with SQLite if one needs to restore an individual card?)

**Additional context / similar features:**
Trello has this with its "archive" feature.  Asana has something similar.

(It would make sense that the import scripts make use of this once this is implemented.  Currently, the Trello import script takes all cards that were archived in Trello the same as if they were not archived.)
title: Feature Idea: Recycle bin / archive individual cards

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring UI design decisions, backend schema changes for soft-delete, and restore workflows; no clear spec for implementation details.
triage.summary: Add recycle bin / archive functionality so deleted cards can be restored. Includes soft-delete support in backend, archive UI, and restore flow. Related to lack of backup (#253) and broken export (#248).

body:
**Summary:**
I know markdown supports checklists but I would want to be able to sort tasks in a better way. Say we have a project that consists of stages planning, building, testing, reporting, lessons learned and that planning is made up of brainstorming, selecting ideas, scheduling and reporting is built of writing draft, filling in missing information, fixing appearance, fixing references, proof reading, editing, publishing. How would I sort this in a way so that not everything is just added in a long list of tasks? Maybe by adding a property and sort by it? When working in larger projects this would be very useful.

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: Medium
This would make the product much more useful, but it might be hard to implement in a good way. One can find work arounds but a native solution would be very nice.

Use cases:
1. Divide large projects into smaller parts to get a better understanding of progress.
title: Feature Idea: Subtasks

triage.skip_reason: Subtasks require significant architectural changes to data model, UI hierarchy, and filtering/sorting logic; no clear spec and involves major UI decisions.
triage.summary: Feature request for native subtask support to hierarchically organize tasks within a project, beyond flat markdown checklists.

body:
**Summary:**
If I have a large project I might want a separate board for this. When it's done I don't need it in the left hand side any more but might still want to reference it in the future.

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: Medium
It would be a nice feature, as most people have just started using focalboard it is not important yet but will be once closed projects start piling up.

Use cases:
1. A board is not needed anymore for whatever reason, one wants to store the information for future reference but does not want to see it in the sidebar all the time.
2. Search in active boards or active plus archived boards. makes it easier to find what you are searching for (see #247)
title: Feature Idea: Archive board

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring UI decisions, backend schema changes, and unclear full spec for archive/restore/search integration.
triage.summary: Allow users to archive boards so they are hidden from the sidebar but remain accessible for future reference. Integrates with search (#247) to filter active vs archived boards.
triage.test_hint: Verify archived boards disappear from sidebar, can be restored, and are searchable when archive filter is enabled.

body:
**Summary:**

This app could be really nice to use with something like emacs org mode or markdown files. This makes using this program in a serverless setup (e.g. via syncthing) very easy and would make it ultimately even possible to have a scrum board checked in with the source code for colaboration. 

Having a slim and content (Blocks and Comments/Atachments) oriented (Go) Interface in order to make it easier to add a new storage  backend would certainly be a good first step. A generalised storage backend plugin could use shell scripts as adaptors for reading file based storage backends (converting eg. between org-mode and json two ways). 

**How important this is to me and why:**

I personally have been looking for an application like this for a long time but keep all my textural data file based and searcheable with grep. If I could make this application could fit into my existing file based notes, dates workflow I'd  start using it immediately. 

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Accessing and editing the board both from IDE and Focalboard app
2. Serverless Collaboration between people via SMB / Dropbox / Syncthing folders.
3. Generally easier integration into existing development ecosystems and pipelines.  

**Additional context / similar features:**

While using one file-per-board, file-per-lane or even file-per-block would certainly improve the problem of edit collisions (as compared to project-per-sqlite-db ) when working on a shared storage it doesn't completely solve the problem of conflicts. This situation has been addressed by: https://github.com/39aldo39/DecSync 

I offer to help with the implementation of such a feature If it finds wider appreciation.
title: Feature Request: File based storage backend

triage.skip_reason: Large architectural feature requiring new storage backend abstraction, file format design decisions, conflict resolution strategy, and significant backend work. No clear spec or acceptance criteria.
triage.summary: Request for a file-based storage backend (e.g. Markdown/org-mode files) to enable serverless collaboration via Syncthing/Dropbox and integration with text-based workflows. Requires a generic storage backend interface and possibly a plugin/adapter system.

body:
Right now, we have [scripts to import basic info](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/tree/main/import) from popular systems like Trello, Asana, and Notion. They can pull in the card titles, descriptions, and basic properties from a single board. Expand these further to do things like:
* Import advanced properties (e.g. dates, text, numbers)
* Handle multiple boards
* Handle Trello PowerUp properties
* Handle sub-tasks in Asana
* Use the [Notion API](https://www.notion.so/api-beta) to import rich data
* Import from other popular systems

This high-level issue can be split into smaller ones to handle targeted improvements
title: Extend import scripts

triage.skip_reason: Large scope with multiple sub-tasks, requires UI/data-model decisions, and integration with external APIs; not suitable for automated attempt.
triage.summary: Extend import scripts (Trello, Asana, Notion, others) to support advanced properties, multiple boards, sub-tasks, PowerUp properties, and the Notion API.
triage.test_hint: For each importer, verify that advanced properties (dates, numbers, text), multiple boards, and system-specific features (Trello PowerUps, Asana sub-tasks, Notion rich data) are correctly mapped to Focalboard cards and properties.

body:
Keyboard navigation is needed in general. This issue tracks the need to be able to use the table editor with just a keyboard. E.g.

body:
## Context

Earlier this week Trello announced [new collaborator limits](https://www.atlassian.com/blog/trello/new-collaborator-limits) on Free workspaces, with 10 members being the cut-off limit for the Free plan.

This change is disrupting the work pipelines in some of the non-profit projects I am involved with, and I am looking for alternatives.
It's not just me; there are a few popular topics on Reddit from people who've faced the same problem ([tap](https://www.reddit.com/r/selfhosted/comments/1b85rq3/alternative_to_trello/), [tap](https://www.reddit.com/r/trello/comments/1b7g4sm/completely_free_alternatives_to_trello/)).

Focalboard keeps popping up -- followed by a note about "but it's discontinued, so.."
Either way, currently this repo attracts attention from folks considering to use or self-host a Trello alternative.

## Opportunity

This could be an opportunity to bootstrap a community effort around Focalboard.
The crowd might big enough to run an "official community fork" and start working on it.
Otherwise the effort could be duplicated, or people will move on to other projects.

## Call for action

Please comment if you're willing to contribute. 
Please do mention areas you'd like to support (i.e. front-end, back-end, UX etc) and anything else you consider relevant.

## Personal story

IMO Focalboard has a friendly codebase which is easy to navigate.
I don't have much experience with Typescript or Go. Still, I was able to enhance the Trello importer with some medium effort (#4874, not converted into a PR yet), adding import support for custom fields etc.
title: Trello is enforcing more limits, could this be an opportunity for Focalboard? Official community fork?

triage.skip_reason: Community/strategic discussion about forking and project governance, not an actionable engineering task. Requires human decision-making and organizational consensus.
triage.summary: User suggests leveraging Trello's new collaborator limits as an opportunity to establish an official community fork of Focalboard, calling for contributor interest and coordination.

body:
Guys, I have a few machines running Windows 7 SP1 x64, with Net 4.8 and WebView2, yet Focalboard refuses to launch. Yes, that OS is out of Silicon Valley favour, Microsoft hegemon urges to spend time and money for software and hardware upgrades I do not need, but frankly could not care less because _it works_.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/373718/200879782-32d38cae-23f6-4558-b822-98d9f0dc2d3a.png)

So what sauce do you have under the hood that requires Windows 10 specifically? Or it is just a matter of compilation flag? If it's about the latter, then please consider adding that flag to make more people productive beyond the so-called golden billion.
title: Windows 7 support

triage.skip_reason: Unclear root cause (could be OS API, Go runtime, or Electron/WebView2 dependency); requires environment-specific investigation and testing on Windows 7 hardware; no repro steps beyond screenshot of launch failure
triage.summary: Focalboard desktop app fails to launch on Windows 7 SP1 x64 even with .NET 4.8 and WebView2 installed. Root cause unknown — may be Go runtime minimum OS requirement, Electron/WebView2 compatibility, or explicit Windows version compilation flag.
triage.test_hint: Run app on Windows 7 SP1 x64 VM with .NET 4.8 and WebView2 installed; check Go runtime minimum OS support (Go 1.18+ dropped Windows 7); inspect manifest or build flags for minimum OS version; review Electron/WebView2 Windows 7 compatibility matrix.

body:
> We promise that we will not enforce the copyleft provisions in AGPL v3.0 against you if your application (a) does not
link to Focalboard directly, but exclusively uses Focalboard's Admin Tools and Configuration Files, and
(b) you have not modified, added to or adapted the source code of Focalboard in a way that results in the creation of
a “modified version” or “work based on” Focalboard as these terms are defined in the AGPL v3.0 license.

If I take some portions of the backend code and modify it to produce an application that is `not similar` to Focalboard and/or Mattermost, for example use this code to create a B2B application. Is it allowed to do so?

Thanks
title: Requesting clarification in your license

triage.skip_reason: Legal/licensing clarification requires human review by maintainers or legal counsel — cannot be auto-resolved
triage.summary: User is requesting clarification on whether modifying backend code to create a dissimilar B2B application is permitted under the AGPL v3.0 license exception described in the repo.

body:
## Summary

Emphasizing the use of websockets in the Docker Personal Server documentation could prevent issues in the future.

## Link to documentation page

[Docket setup guide](https://www.focalboard.com/download/personal-edition/docker/)

## (Optional) Additional context and/or screenshot

Add additional context and/or a screenshot of the product feature you'd like explained in documentation.

I am a bit new to self hosting, and am running the Personal Server with NGINX Proxy Manager. I didn't enable websockets support which [caused issues](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/2183#issuecomment-1831272394).

The Ubuntu setup guide mentions that websockets are used, but those setting up with Docker would likely not see this.
title: Doc: Emphasize the need for websocket support in reverse proxy setups.

triage.summary: Add a note to the Docker Personal Server setup guide emphasizing that WebSocket support must be enabled in reverse proxy configurations (e.g., NGINX Proxy Manager) to avoid connectivity issues.
triage.test_hint: Verify the Docker setup guide page includes a visible callout or note about enabling WebSocket support in reverse proxies, and cross-references the Ubuntu guide's existing WebSocket mention.

Updates `actions/upload-artifact` from 4.6.2 to 7.0.0
Release notes

Sourced from actions/upload-artifact's releases.

v7.0.0

v7 What's new

Direct Uploads

Adds support for uploading single files directly (unzipped). Callers can set the new archive parameter to false to skip zipping the file during upload. Right now, we only support single files. The action will fail if the glob passed resolves to multiple files. The name parameter is also ignored with this setting. Instead, the name of the artifact will be the name of the uploaded file.

ESM

To support new versions of the @actions/* packages, we've upgraded the package to ESM.

What's Changed

  • Add proxy integration test by @​Link- in actions/upload-artifact#754
  • Upgrade the module to ESM and bump dependencies by @​danwkennedy in actions/upload-artifact#762
  • Support direct file uploads by @​danwkennedy in actions/upload-artifact#764

New Contributors

  • @​Link- made their first contribution in actions/upload-artifact#754

Full Changelog: https://github.com/actions/upload-artifact/compare/v6...v7.0.0

v6.0.0

v6 - What's new

[!IMPORTANT] actions/upload-artifact@v6 now runs on Node.js 24 (runs.using: node24) and requires a minimum Actions Runner version of 2.327.1. If you are using self-hosted runners, ensure they are updated before upgrading.

Node.js 24

This release updates the runtime to Node.js 24. v5 had preliminary support for Node.js 24, however this action was by default still running on Node.js 20. Now this action by default will run on Node.js 24.

What's Changed

Updates `github/codeql-action` from 3.28.19 to 4.35.1
Release notes

Sourced from github/codeql-action's releases.

v4.35.1

  • Fix incorrect minimum required Git version for improved incremental analysis: it should have been 2.36.0, not 2.11.0. #3781

v4.35.0

  • Reduced the minimum Git version required for improved incremental analysis from 2.38.0 to 2.11.0. #3767
  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.25.1. #3773

v4.34.1

  • Downgrade default CodeQL bundle version to 2.24.3 due to issues with a small percentage of Actions and JavaScript analyses. #3762

v4.34.0

  • Added an experimental change which disables TRAP caching when improved incremental analysis is enabled, since improved incremental analysis supersedes TRAP caching. This will improve performance and reduce Actions cache usage. We expect to roll this change out to everyone in March. #3569
  • We are rolling out improved incremental analysis to C/C++ analyses that use build mode none. We expect this rollout to be complete by the end of April 2026. #3584
  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.25.0. #3585

v4.33.0

  • Upcoming change: Starting April 2026, the CodeQL Action will skip collecting file coverage information on pull requests to improve analysis performance. File coverage information will still be computed on non-PR analyses. Pull request analyses will log a warning about this upcoming change. #3562

    To opt out of this change:

    • Repositories owned by an organization: Create a custom repository property with the name github-codeql-file-coverage-on-prs and the type "True/false", then set this property to true in the repository's settings. For more information, see Managing custom properties for repositories in your organization. Alternatively, if you are using an advanced setup workflow, you can set the CODEQL_ACTION_FILE_COVERAGE_ON_PRS environment variable to true in your workflow.
    • User-owned repositories using default setup: Switch to an advanced setup workflow and set the CODEQL_ACTION_FILE_COVERAGE_ON_PRS environment variable to true in your workflow.
    • User-owned repositories using advanced setup: Set the CODEQL_ACTION_FILE_COVERAGE_ON_PRS environment variable to true in your workflow.

  • Fixed a bug which caused the CodeQL Action to fail loading repository properties if a "Multi select" repository property was configured for the repository. #3557

  • The CodeQL Action now loads custom repository properties on GitHub Enterprise Server, enabling the customization of features such as github-codeql-disable-overlay that was previously only available on GitHub.com. #3559

  • Once private package registries can be configured with OIDC-based authentication for organizations, the CodeQL Action will now be able to accept such configurations. #3563

  • Fixed the retry mechanism for database uploads. Previously this would fail with the error "Response body object should not be disturbed or locked". #3564

  • A warning is now emitted if the CodeQL Action detects a repository property whose name suggests that it relates to the CodeQL Action, but which is not one of the properties recognised by the current version of the CodeQL Action. #3570

v4.32.6

  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.24.3. #3548

v4.32.5

  • Repositories owned by an organization can now set up the github-codeql-disable-overlay custom repository property to disable improved incremental analysis for CodeQL. First, create a custom repository property with the name github-codeql-disable-overlay and the type "True/false" in the organization's settings. Then in the repository's settings, set this property to true to disable improved incremental analysis. For more information, see Managing custom properties for repositories in your organization. This feature is not yet available on GitHub Enterprise Server. #3507
  • Added an experimental change so that when improved incremental analysis fails on a runner — potentially due to insufficient disk space — the failure is recorded in the Actions cache so that subsequent runs will automatically skip improved incremental analysis until something changes (e.g. a larger runner is provisioned or a new CodeQL version is released). We expect to roll this change out to everyone in March. #3487
  • The minimum memory check for improved incremental analysis is now skipped for CodeQL 2.24.3 and later, which has reduced peak RAM usage. #3515
  • Reduced log levels for best-effort private package registry connection check failures to reduce noise from workflow annotations. #3516
  • Added an experimental change which lowers the minimum disk space requirement for improved incremental analysis, enabling it to run on standard GitHub Actions runners. We expect to roll this change out to everyone in March. #3498
  • Added an experimental change which allows the start-proxy action to resolve the CodeQL CLI version from feature flags instead of using the linked CLI bundle version. We expect to roll this change out to everyone in March. #3512
  • The previously experimental changes from versions 4.32.3, 4.32.4, 3.32.3 and 3.32.4 are now enabled by default. #3503, #3504

v4.32.4

  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.24.2. #3493
  • Added an experimental change which improves how certificates are generated for the authentication proxy that is used by the CodeQL Action in Default Setup when private package registries are configured. This is expected to generate more widely compatible certificates and should have no impact on analyses which are working correctly already. We expect to roll this change out to everyone in February. #3473
  • When the CodeQL Action is run with debugging enabled in Default Setup and private package registries are configured, the "Setup proxy for registries" step will output additional diagnostic information that can be used for troubleshooting. #3486
  • Added a setting which allows the CodeQL Action to enable network debugging for Java programs. This will help GitHub staff support customers with troubleshooting issues in GitHub-managed CodeQL workflows, such as Default Setup. This setting can only be enabled by GitHub staff. #3485
  • Added a setting which enables GitHub-managed workflows, such as Default Setup, to use a nightly CodeQL CLI release instead of the latest, stable release that is used by default. This will help GitHub staff support customers whose analyses for a given repository or organization require early access to a change in an upcoming CodeQL CLI release. This setting can only be enabled by GitHub staff. #3484

v4.32.3

  • Added experimental support for testing connections to private package registries. This feature is not currently enabled for any analysis. In the future, it may be enabled by default for Default Setup. #3466

... (truncated)

Updates `microsoft/setup-msbuild` from 1.3 to 3
Release notes

Sourced from microsoft/setup-msbuild's releases.

v3 Update to move to Node24

What's Changed

  • Fix small typo in README.md by @​GeckoEidechse in microsoft/setup-msbuild#129
  • Update to Node24 by @​timheuer in microsoft/setup-msbuild#145

New Contributors

  • @​GeckoEidechse made their first contribution in microsoft/setup-msbuild#129

Full Changelog: https://github.com/microsoft/setup-msbuild/compare/v2...v3

v2

This is a major version bump with no functionality changes. The major version change is due to moving to node20 for the node version per the deprecation announcement for GitHub Actions (https://github.blog/changelog/2023-09-22-github-actions-transitioning-from-node-16-to-node-20/). This change from node16 to node20 could cause breaking changes to other workflow actions steps (see #125) running and needing/leveraging expected functionality in node16 and thus as a safe boundary, changing the version here.

Full Changelog: https://github.com/microsoft/setup-msbuild/compare/v1.3...v2

v1.3.3

This is a patch update that reverts the node16->node20 change which introduced unintended breaking changes for users of this action. The v1, v1.3 tags have been updated to this release as well.

Full Changelog: https://github.com/microsoft/setup-msbuild/compare/v1.3.2...v1.3.3

body:
Hi team,

I wonder if the September 15th, 2023 move to "fully community supported" model and the "We encourage the community to fork this repository for continued development and contributions." statement warrant an update of LICENSE.txt?

Currently it [outlines a mix of MIT, GNU AGPL v.3.0 and commercial license](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/blob/main/LICENSE.txt), as follows:

```
You are licensed to use compiled versions of Focalboard produced by Mattermost, Inc. under an MIT LICENSE

-	See MIT-COMPILED-LICENSE.md included in compiled versions for details

You may be licensed to use source code to create compiled versions not produced by Mattermost, Inc. in one of two ways:

1. Under the Free Software Foundation’s GNU AGPL v.3.0, subject to the exceptions outlined in this policy; or
2. Under a commercial license available from Mattermost, Inc. by contacting commercial@mattermost.com

You are licensed to use the source code in Admin Tools and Configuration Files (webapp/html-templates/, app-config.json,
config.json, webapp/i18n/, server/model/, plugin/ and all subdirectories thereof) under the Apache License v2.0.

We promise that we will not enforce the copyleft provisions in AGPL v3.0 against you if your application (a) does not
link to Focalboard directly, but exclusively uses Focalboard's Admin Tools and Configuration Files, and
(b) you have not modified, added to or adapted the source code of Focalboard in a way that results in the creation of
a “modified version” or “work based on” Focalboard as these terms are defined in the AGPL v3.0 license.
```

I am not sure to what extent it reflects the "community supported" model. It sounds like patched and self-hosted deployments may need to be careful in using the Focalboard name / logo and/or any © signs if they are displayed somewhere in the app(s).

Any advice?

P.S. I am a developer, not a lawyer.
title: Does "fully community supported" warrant an update of LICENSE.txt?

triage.skip_reason: Legal/licensing decision requires human judgment from Mattermost legal team, not a code change that can be automated
triage.summary: Community asks whether LICENSE.txt should be updated to reflect the September 2023 'fully community supported' model, as the current license still references commercial Mattermost licensing terms and AGPL enforcement policies that may no longer apply.

## Summary

I wanted to install Focalboard on my Oracle Cloud Free Tier VM (which has an arm64 cpu) to use it with my team but in the tags information on Docker Hub I've seen that the provided docker image is just for amd64 architectures.
However, from Issue #124 of this repository I understand that now also ARM64 architectures are supported.
Therefore, I would like to know how could I spin up Focalboard on an arm machine and, since this kind of machines is getting quite popular among servers, I think it would be useful to add those informations to the install instructions of the project (which at the moment are not that exhaustive, for example not reporting how to have persistent storage of Focalboard container data either)

https://www.focalboard.com/download/personal-edition/docker/
title: Doc: Add documentation on how to run Focalboard with docker (or docker-compose) on ARM64 machines

triage.summary: Docker install docs lack ARM64 guidance and persistent storage instructions. Users on ARM64 machines (e.g., Oracle Cloud Free Tier, Apple Silicon) cannot easily find how to run Focalboard on non-amd64 architectures. Also missing docker-compose examples with volume mounts for persistent storage.
triage.test_hint: Verify updated docs include: ARM64 image tag or multi-arch manifest reference, docker-compose example with named volume or bind mount for /opt/focalboard/data, and note about supported architectures.

## Summary

When working with a Personal Desktop on windows I see a Port listed in my application window header. I have confirmed that if I go to my http://localhost:{port} I do reach a login page for focalboard but cannot create a user or sign in with any default credentials that I can find. I see in the files unzipped from the raw download there is a config.json that has port 8088 defined which differs from the port in the header and is also non-functioning by default. What is the purpose of that file?

What I would like to do is have the ability to load the tool on a machine and share the webview URL where the board could be reviewed perhaps over a team meeting live to make updates. This ideal scenario would be better if it were possible to use local tag-like Assignees instead of different account holders if its not possible to create accounts on the local/webview interfaces. 

If this is not possible; could some comment or documentation be added regarding the webview's access from the personal desktop application and its non-functionality? Something that if someone else discovers in the future they can confirm is desired behavior vs a bug.

As a final note: I am unable to use the server edition of your software as only the personal desktop is accessible FOSS for internal usage. 

## Link to documentation page
N/A

## (Optional) Additional context and/or screenshot

Add additional context and/or a screenshot of the product feature you'd like explained in documentation.
![image](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/assets/70120966/286cb050-1cf3-497b-8265-40d40c5325b0)

triage.summary: User confusion around Windows Personal Desktop WebView port behavior: the port shown in the app header is functional but lacks docs explaining its purpose, default credentials (none), config.json port differences, and limitations around multi-user/assignee sharing via the local webview.

Similar to #3096, check `make swagger` in CI to verify the API docs stay up to date. The embedded Swagger YAML definitions inside Golang comments can be a bit fragile with vague error messages being hard to follow.

Rough CI steps:

1. `make swgger` (this may error on it's own)
2. Check if changes e.g. `git status --porcelain` or `git diff --exit-code`
title: Code Debt: Check `make swagger` in CI

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Go to [LICENSE.txt](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/blob/main/LICENSE.txt)
2. See the terms `You are licensed to use compiled versions of Focalboard produced by Mattermost, Inc. under an MIT LICENSE`
3. MIT is a **source code** license specifically granting permission to "modify, merge, publish, distribute, sublicense, and/or sell copies of the Software". You are using it on specific binaries (that I cannot "modify, merge, ..." without making them *other* binaries)
4. None of the MIT license terms mean anything in this context. The rest of the MIT license is about the copyright notice text, which is not included in your binary (how can text be included in binaries?)

## Expected behavior

Your licensing terms let me know how your software is licensed.

## Additional context

This is like selling someone a toaster and telling them they have to abide by US driving laws when using it. What does it mean? Do I have to use turn-signals before removing toast? Do I need a state-issued ID to operate it? I can't even consider using it.

**If you mean to say that I can use those binaries unmodified for any purpose, just say that. Don't tell me I can use them under the terms of the Civil Aviation Law rules, none of which apply to your tool.**
title: Bug: license terms

triage.summary: LICENSE.txt applies MIT license terms to compiled binaries, which is semantically incorrect. MIT is a source-code license and its terms (modify, distribute, etc.) do not clearly map to binary usage. The license text should be clarified to explicitly state the permitted uses of the distributed binaries.
triage.test_hint: Review and update LICENSE.txt to clearly distinguish binary distribution rights from source code rights; verify the updated text accurately reflects the intended licensing terms.

body:
**Summary:**
Set a date for regular releases, e.g. 16th of the month (to match Mattermost) and 1st of the month (because we can ship faster as an early project) 

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Deliver features to community faster, so community gets more value, and we can get more feedback, iterate, and demonstrate project velocity 
2. Our [changelog](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#work-in-progress---v07-release---date-tbd) isn't overflowing by the time of next release. 
3. We encourage ourselves to think about upgrades, at least to Personal Desktop Edition 
4. Build excitement, anticipation and awareness among community 

**Additional context / similar features:**
Watching the changelog grow and not being able to get the new improvements and features is like having our noses pressed up against a glass wall, looking at amazing software we can see but can't yet use.
title: Feature Idea: Release cadence

triage.skip_reason: Release cadence is a process/organizational decision requiring human coordination, not a code change
triage.summary: Proposal to establish a regular release schedule (e.g. 1st and 16th of each month) to deliver features faster, align with Mattermost cadence, and maintain a manageable changelog.

## Summary

Especially after this [PR](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/pull/3888) made them work in an equivalent manner. We should implement a "base Person property" and update Person and MultiPerson to call this property with its specific needs. 

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: High

As a sameple, see how the various text properties (Text, Email, Phone, and Number) use BaseTextEditor.
title: Code Debt: Share common code between Person and MultiPerson properties

triage.skip_reason: Refactoring task requiring UI/component design decisions and careful deduplication across two non-trivial React components; not a simple mechanical change
triage.summary: Refactor Person and MultiPerson property components to share a common BasePersonEditor, similar to how text properties share BaseTextEditor, reducing code duplication introduced/solidified by PR #3888.
triage.test_hint: Ensure existing person and multi-person property rendering, selection, editing, and display behaviors remain unchanged after refactor; add unit tests for the shared base component.

body:
## Summary

While adding Unit Tests on [this issue](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/832), I found that we are importing `@testing-library/react` in all the test files to extend the RTL (React Testing Libray) custom jest matchers. `jest` actually provides an easier way to handle this where we can add a setup file that is present for all the tests before they start.

This way, we can add the RTL import there and avoid all tests importing the same.

[Reference](https://jestjs.io/docs/configuration#setupfilesafterenv-array)

## How important this is to me and why

Nothing customer-facing but as a developer, this makes me write tests seamlessly without worrying about those special imports. I would consider this as `High` 

## Additional context/similar features

Not specific ones but I have seen many open source projects using common files for jest RTL imports. 

Would be willing to submit a PR if this sounds good 🙂
title: Feature Idea: add common jest import file to configure RTL imports

triage.summary: Add a Jest setupFilesAfterFramework file to centralize @testing-library/react imports, removing the need to import RTL matchers in every test file individually.
triage.test_hint: Verify existing tests still pass after removing per-file RTL imports and adding the shared setup file to jest.config.js setupFilesAfterFramework array.

body:
As of August 28th, 2024 this repository now only contains the standalone Focalboard project. The Mattermost plugin has been moved to [mattermost/mattermost-plugin-boards](https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost-plugin-boards) where it will be maintained separately.

This project is not currently being maintained and we are looking for community members, with past experience maintaining open source projects, who are interested in becoming maintainers of this project.

If you're interested, please reply to this issue and we'd be happy to discuss.
title: Call for Maintainers - Standalone Focalboard

triage.skip_reason: Community/governance announcement, not a technical task requiring code changes
triage.summary: Call for community maintainers for standalone Focalboard after Mattermost plugin separation
